articles.title: Combined impact of smoking, alcohol and social inequalities on premature mortality in urban India
data_sources: Registrar General of India — Chennai/Tamil Nadu age-sex-specific mortality rates
World Bank Gini index (cross-national contextual reference)
articles.core_conclusion: In a 20-year prospective cohort of 458,609 adults in Chennai, education alone is the strongest predictor of premature mortality (RR ~2.9 for no schooling vs. tertiary education) across all nine major disease categories, even among non-smokers and non-drinkers. In men, smoking and drinking each independently amplify risk, and their combination nearly doubles mortality (RR 1.89). Low-education men are three times more likely to smoke and drink, compounding social inequalities. Alcohol shows a dose-response relationship with no evidence of cardiac protection at moderate intake.

articles.title: Divergent surface urban heat island responses to background aerosol mitigation
methods: CESM1 Single Forcing Large Ensemble (20 members)
aerosol-only causal attribution (XAER vs ALL forcing)
two-sided Student t-test for ensemble significance
data_sources: Global seamless 1 km daily LST dataset (Zhang et al. 2022)
Global Urban Boundary (GAIA-derived, Li et al. 2020)
articles.core_conclusion: Background aerosol mitigation produces divergent surface UHI responses depending on hydroclimate: aerosol reductions amplify warm-season surface UHI in humid climates (urban warming exceeds rural warming due to limited evaporative cooling over impervious surfaces vs. increased rainfall and vegetation in rural areas) but weaken UHI in arid regions (reduced rainfall and vegetation suppress rural evapotranspiration more than urban). These hydroclimate-dependent responses call for prioritizing urban heat mitigation in humid regions under continued aerosol reduction and rapid urbanization.

articles.title: Neighbourhood design and the environmental and social costs of suburbanization
methods: composite GCD index from OpenStreetMap street layouts
instrumental variable (IV) using historical GCD adoption waves
data_sources: NHGIS 2000 US Decennial Census (CBGs and tracts)
US EPA Smart Location Database v3.0
HISDAC-US (Harvard Dataverse, 250 m gridded, historical settlement)
articles.core_conclusion: Garden city design (GCD) — winding streets, cul-de-sacs, hierarchical road networks — independently drives greenhouse gas emissions, social isolation and sedentary behaviour across more than 60,000 US neighbourhoods. Neighbourhood design accounts for 25–41% of suburbanization's sustainability costs, independent of where suburbs are located.

articles.title: Towards autonomous medical artificial intelligence agents
articles.core_conclusion: MIRA, an autonomous AI agent operating inside a sandboxed FHIR-compliant EHR, achieved 87.8% diagnostic accuracy across 8 emergency-department conditions, outperforming board-certified physicians (78.1%, P<0.001) and a mixed-seniority cohort (71.1%, P<0.001). It navigated more than 85,000 clinical action options, showed +35 percentage-point higher guideline adherence, higher procedure recall (53.5% vs. 38.3%), and zero high-severity drug-safety events in a blinded audit, suggesting EHR-integrated AI agents can perform structured clinical workflows at physician-level while prospective real-world validation remains essential.

articles.title: An AI-based symptom checker integrated into MomConnect maternal healthcare platform: a prospective, observational mixed-methods study
methods: Bayesian network diagnostic decision support system
physician panel safety rating (exact binomial test, Clopper-Pearson CI)
composite barrier score (district health system + individual socioeconomic indicators)
Stuart-Maxwell test for paired categorical behaviour change
International Wealth Index via principal component analysis
thematic analysis of qualitative phone interviews
choropleth mapping with OCHA administrative boundaries
data_sources: Humanitarian Data Exchange - South Africa subnational boundaries (OCHA)
International Wealth Index / Global Data Lab
articles.core_conclusion: Integrating a Bayesian-network DDSS into South Africa's government WhatsApp maternal health platform MomConnect was associated with clinically safe advice in 98.4% of 184 physician-reviewed cases and improved health-seeking behaviour: 44.6% of users changed care-seeking intentions, safe behaviours rose by 18.1 percentage points, and care-seeking prevalence climbed from 17.0% to 43.0% at one-week follow-up — even among the 86.3% facing multiple barriers to care. Income, not geography, was the primary predictor of failing to act safely on the advice received.

articles.title: City racial segregation statistics are robust to aggregation bias
articles.core_conclusion: Simulating millions of alternative Census tract boundary configurations via redistricting software reveals no systematic bias in official segregation estimates: official values closely track the mean of the simulated distribution. Aggregation-induced variance is primarily determined by the number of spatial units, being largest in small cities and converging in large ones. Census tracts are practically robust as units of segregation measurement, and the framework provides a general method for diagnosing spatial aggregation error.

articles.title: Maternal metabolomic signatures for risk of preterm and early term birth in African American women
articles.core_conclusion: High-resolution untargeted metabolomics of maternal serum reveals distinct, phenotype-specific metabolic profiles differentiating spontaneous from medically indicated preterm and early term birth in African American women. Perturbations in amino acid pathways (arginine, proline, methionine, branched-chain amino acids) dominate spontaneous early birth while acylcarnitines and aldosterone characterize medically indicated birth. Thirteen metabolites were internally validated; a six-metabolite panel for spontaneous PTB achieved AUC = 0.77.

articles.title: Urban congestion relief experiments through routing-app interventions
data_sources: Google Maps aggregated and anonymized traffic data (proprietary)
UK DESNZ Greenhouse Gas Conversion Factors 2023
articles.core_conclusion: A switchback experiment across ~100 congested road segments in 10 major US cities shows that rerouting less than 2% of Google Maps trips produces a city-average 2% speed increase on targeted segments, 0.7% drop in total travel times, and potential annual CO2-equivalent savings exceeding 1,000 tonnes per city in most locations. Marginal, network-aware routing interventions measurably improve road-network efficiency at city scale without meaningfully harming rerouted drivers.

articles.title: Association of mortality and combined oxidative capacity of ozone and nitrogen dioxide
authors: Micheline de Sousa Zanotti Stagliorio Coelho
Susana das Neves Pereira da Silva
data_sources: MCC Multi-Country Multi-City Collaborative Research Network
National and regional air-quality monitoring stations (per-country)
articles.core_conclusion: The weighted oxidative capacity index Ox_wt (average of O3 and NO2 concentrations weighted by standard electrode potential) associates more strongly with mortality than either pollutant alone: a 10-ppb increase over 2 days links to +0.82% total, +1.09% cardiovascular, and +0.88% respiratory deaths across 380 cities in 23 countries/regions (1985-2020). Ox_wt attributable deaths exceed those from O3 or NO2 individually but are fewer than their sum, confirming partial non-additivity and supporting Ox_wt as a parsimonious public health indicator.

articles.title: Patterns of medication use across society from national primary care dispensing data
data_sources: NHSBSA primary care dispensing data (NHS England SDE)
GDPPR (General Practice Extraction Service Data for Pandemic Planning and Research)
NHS SDE curated demographics asset (BHF Data Science Centre)
articles.core_conclusion: Analysing 5.8 billion primary care medication dispensings for 52.6 million individuals in England (November 2019 – December 2024), this study shows the COVID-19 pandemic disrupted medication initiation differently across drug classes: gastrointestinal and mental health medications remained persistently below pre-pandemic levels, while cardiovascular and diabetes medications recovered and exceeded them. Stark inequities are revealed: women receive up to twice the antidepressant volume of men in young adulthood; Bangladeshi and Pakistani groups have dispensing rate ratios up to 2.5-fold higher by age 60; the most deprived quintile has rates twice those of the least deprived from age 40. By age 70, 42% of the population are on five or more concurrent medications.

articles.title: Rooftop photovoltaic-powered electric vehicle charging for accelerated decarbonization
methods: hexagonal microgrid tessellation (r = 2–16 km, 8 resolution levels)
exponential scaling law derivation (Pearson correlation, log-log regression)
carbon mitigation accounting (RPV + EV substitution + ESS emissions)
data_sources: EV Charging Transaction Records (22,000 piles, 40 cities, 2023)
World Settlement Footprint 3D (Esch et al., Remote Sens. Environ. 270, 2022)
SSP-consistent Rooftop Area Growth Projections (Joshi et al., Sci. Data 11, 2024)
IEA Global EV Data Explorer (STEPS and APS scenarios)
articles.core_conclusion: Across 40 global cities, RPV generation and EV charging demand follow an exponential scaling law across urban microgrids of varying size. By 2050, RPV supply will remain sufficient to meet EV charging demand despite 4–1759-fold demand growth, projecting US$3,173 billion in revenue and 11.9 Gt cumulative carbon mitigation from 2025 to 2050.

articles.title: Author Correction: Global expansion of the ecological impact of extra-urban road traffic
data_sources: Global extra-urban traffic volume time series (Sci. Data 2024, 10.1038/s41597-024-03287-z)
articles.core_conclusion: A code error in traffic volume predictions for 1975 and 1990 was identified and corrected. The corrected headline finding is that extra-urban road effect zones grew by 61% (revised from 53%) since 1975, reaching 239 million hectares in 2015 (1.8% of global terrestrial land). In 2015, 63% of key biodiversity areas were impacted by moderate to very high traffic. The core narrative — extra-urban road traffic causes rapidly expanding, globally significant ecological damage — is reinforced by the correction.

articles.title: Real-world clinical and economic evaluation of remote programming for deep brain stimulation in Parkinson's disease
methods: Demographically Adjusted Parkinson Care Inequity Index (DPII)
Bass diffusion model for technology adoption
data_sources: Global Burden of Disease Study 2021 (IHME / GHDx)
National Bureau of Statistics of China
City-level population projections (figshare, Cai & Zhang et al.)
Resource and Environmental Science Data Platform (China basemap)
articles.core_conclusion: Across 20,383 Parkinson's disease patients and 42,163 remote programming sessions at 439 hospitals in China (2012–2024), RP for DBS is clinically equivalent to onsite programming while reducing the demographically adjusted healthcare access inequality index (DPII) 30% faster. Economic modelling projects annual benefits of ¥1.09 billion (reduced domestic medical tourism) and ¥8.15 billion (labour savings), with cumulative gains of ¥115–270 billion by 2050 — concentrated disproportionately among low-income, remote, and advanced-disease patients whose cost savings are 2–10 times greater.

articles.title: A thalamus–brainstem attractor network drives history-biased decisions
methods: principal component analysis of population activity
data_sources: Larval zebrafish brain connectivity atlas (Du et al., 2025)
Hierarchical attractor network SNN model (Zenodo + GitHub)
articles.core_conclusion: A hierarchical thalamus–brainstem attractor–integrator circuit underlies history-biased decisions: discrete attractors in the dorsal thalamus encode the most recent sensory event as persistent categorical memory (lasting 10–20 s), while a downstream hindbrain integrator accumulates multi-trial history and combines it with incoming sensory cues. Optogenetic manipulation causally confirms that the dorsal thalamus drives serial dependence. A whole-brain SNN model reveals heterogeneous inhibitory subtypes as the key mechanism enabling flexible state transitions.

articles.title: Deep learning four decades of human migration
methods: ensemble uncertainty quantification (15 networks x 100 stock draws)
data_sources: National statistics (Sweden, New Zealand, Finland, ~30 countries net migration)
World Bank / IMF / Maddison Project GDP
articles.core_conclusion: An ensemble of deep recurrent neural networks produces the first annual, globally consistent origin-destination migration dataset spanning 230 countries and regions from 1990 to 2023, outperforming all existing five-year stock-differencing methods on held-out data. The framework integrates UN migrant stocks, QuantMig harmonized flows, national statistics, and Facebook digital-trace estimates, captures both long-term trends and short-term shocks (COVID-19, armed conflicts), and propagates uncertainty to generate calibrated confidence bounds that expose data-poor regions. Global migration rose from ~13 million people per year (2000) to ~35 million (2023), with the Middle East receiving the highest total inflow — primarily from South Asia.

articles.title: Digging into the costs of urban soil rehabilitation
data_sources: Institut Paris Région / CDC Biodiversité renaturation manual (Castaing et al., 2022)
articles.core_conclusion: In France, median urban soil rehabilitation costs range from €50–310 m−2 for compacted, sealed, or built-up soils and exceed €800 m−2 for polluted soils. Rehabilitation involves up to ten sequential steps; preliminary diagnostics are cheapest while building deconstruction and pollution remediation are most expensive. These first-ever cost benchmarks support spatial prioritization of rehabilitation areas and the design of economic incentives, while also underscoring the value of preventing soil degradation.

articles.title: Efficient and accurate neural-field reconstruction using resistive memory
methods: implicit neural field representation (MLP + Gaussian positional encoding)
data_sources: NeRF synthetic dataset (Mildenhall et al., Commun. ACM 2021, doi:10.1145/3503250)
articles.core_conclusion: A software-hardware co-optimization framework pairs implicit neural-field representations (compressed via low-rank decomposition and structured pruning) with a resistive-memory computing-in-memory chip to reconstruct complex signals from sparse inputs, achieving up to 32.3x energy-efficiency and 38.8x parallelism gains over digital baselines across 3D CT reconstruction, novel view synthesis, and dynamic-scene synthesis — without sacrificing reconstruction quality.

articles.title: Compound effects of extreme heat and wildfire-PM2.5 air pollution on child hospitalizations in multiple countries
keywords: relative excess risk due to interaction
methods: relative excess risk due to interaction (RERI)
articles.core_conclusion: Across 954 communities in five countries (2000-2019), days when an extreme heat event and a high-level wildfire-PM2.5 pollution day co-occurred carried a 3.8% higher risk of all-cause child hospitalization (RR=1.038, 1.029-1.048), greater than either hazard alone. The interaction was synergistic (positive RERI) for respiratory, renal and diabetes admissions, and consistently stronger than the heat x non-fire-PM2.5 combination.

articles.title: Sustainable battery recycling through spatial and technological alignment
methods: XGBoost / ANN / SVM / random forest ensembles
data_sources: EV Compulsory Traffic Accident Liability Insurance records
articles.core_conclusion: EOL EV battery recycling in China is constrained mainly by the spatial mismatch between where batteries retire and where licensed treatment capacity sits, compounded by provincial differences in grid carbon intensity and recycling technology rather than by transport (<1% of process emissions). Over 364 cities, ~300 projects and 24 chemistries, 2020-2030 EOL volumes reach 16.67-19.99 Mt with a northeast->southwest->northwest hotspot migration; interprovincial coordination raises capacity utilization by 67.12% (relative) but cannot eliminate the mismatch, while supply-demand planning can cut emissions by up to 44% and raise lithium recovery by 53%.

articles.title: Cost-effectiveness analysis of human–AI collaboration in disease screening
data_sources: Lifeline Express diabetic retinopathy screening programme (China)
articles.core_conclusion: Across 8 AI-based screening strategies and 270 human–AI collaboration scenarios modelled on a real-world diabetic retinopathy programme in China, a 'copilot human–AI' strategy (AI triage + human review of filtered cases) is most cost-effective. An annual copilot strategy across all ages would save US$1.32 million while gaining 14.73 QALYs over a lifetime — net monetary benefit US$1.88 million per 100,000 population. Human involvement is indispensable to maximize clinical benefit and cost-effectiveness; fully autonomous AI is dominated in the reference setting.

articles.title: Geographic proximity dampens ideological policy disagreement in urban politics
data_sources: Borealis / Scholars Portal Dataverse replication archive
articles.core_conclusion: Left-right ideology structures attitudes toward municipal policy, but its influence is significantly dampened when residents consider the same issues at the geographically proximate scale of their own neighborhood. A topic-sampling experiment (40 issues, >40,000 preferences, ~4,000 Canadian respondents) reconciles the view that urban politics is ideological with the view that it is place-based and non-ideological: both hold, depending on geographic scale.

articles.title: A circular hydrothermal refinery for sustainable aviation fuel from food waste
data_sources: US DOE SAF Grand Challenge Roadmap (2022)
US DOE Wet Waste HTL State of Technology PNNL-33622 (2022)
US EPA Wasted Food Report EPA-530-R-23-005 (2023)
articles.core_conclusion: An integrated closed-loop hydrothermal refinery converts urban food waste into a 100% drop-in sustainable aviation fuel meeting all Jet A specifications without blending, while a paired electrochemical stage recovers acetic acid, nutrients and hydrogen from the aqueous phase. With renewable electricity, the system achieves market competitiveness at US$3.82 GGE-1 and a net-negative carbon footprint of -8.5 kg CO2e GGE-1.

articles.title: Clinical outcomes of a genomic newborn screening study in Qingdao, China
methods: untargeted blood-spot metabolomics (PLS-DA, KEGG pathway enrichment)
data_sources: QDnewborn WGS cohort (GVM000685, Genome Variation Map, CNCB/NGDC)
Traditional NBS records (MS/MS + OAE/AABR auditory screening)
articles.core_conclusion: A prospective WGS screen of 9,992 newborns in Qingdao found a 2.7% positive rate and 19 clinically confirmed monogenic disease cases, of which 8 were missed by traditional biochemical and auditory screening. Reanalysis of 19 symptomatic infants revealed pathogenic variants in 42.1%, supporting WGS integration into routine neonatal screening in China.

articles.title: Effects of a cash-plus intervention combining conditional cash transfer with social and behaviour-change communication on pregnancy weight and birth weight in India
data_sources: Government mobile counselling app (MMMPY / Rajasthan WCD)
MMMPY conditional cash payment portal (Government of India)
Digital hospital weighing scales (123 maternity hospitals)
articles.core_conclusion: A government-led cash-plus intervention in Rajasthan, India — combining conditional cash transfers (MMMPY) with social and behaviour-change communication — was associated with a 7.4 percentage-point absolute reduction in low birth weight (21% relative reduction) and a 3.4 kg increase in mean third-trimester pregnancy weight among 90,732 newborns and 60,899 mothers over 44 months, evaluated by interrupted time-series analysis.

articles.title: Health effects associated with alcohol consumption: a Burden of Proof study
data_sources: IHME Burden of Proof visualization platform
articles.core_conclusion: Analysing 843 cohort and case–control studies across 20 health outcomes with the Burden of Proof meta-analytic framework, alcohol is unambiguously carcinogenic even at low consumption levels, with a five-star (>105% increased risk) rating for pharyngeal cancer and three-star ratings for cirrhosis, pancreatitis and several other cancers. J- or U-shaped protective signals for cardiovascular and metabolic outcomes are weak and inconsistent after heterogeneity absorption (one-star), undermining the cardiovascular benefit narrative. High intake is harmful across all 20 outcomes, and no universally safe threshold exists.

articles.title: Importance of elephants for dung beetle biodiversity and ecosystem functions
data_sources: Paired landscape observational surveys (elephant vs. livestock-dominated)
articles.core_conclusion: Elephants are central hubs in the East African herbivore-dung beetle interaction network; their extinction would trigger disproportionate coextinction cascades in dung beetles. A 15-year exclosure experiment confirmed that excluding elephants depressed beetle abundance, diversity, and ecosystem functions far more than excluding smaller ungulates; observational data from livestock-dominated landscapes corroborated the result. Large, extinction-prone animals act as keystone resource-provisioners sustaining the biodiversity and ecosystem services of linked consumers.

articles.title: Strong demand for clean air policies in Majority World cities
articles.core_conclusion: In a pre-registered survey of 11,417 adults across four severely polluted Majority-World cities (Accra, Delhi, Jakarta, Johannesburg), air quality was a highly prioritized concern and majorities supported all six mitigation policies, including cost-bearing ones. Concern, institutional trust and low perceived behavioral control were the strongest correlates of support, challenging the view of weak environmental demand in highly exposed developing settings.

articles.title: Global divergence in urban demographic change and migration patterns
methods: static vs. dynamic boundary robustness checks
data_sources: GHS-UCDB (Global Human Settlement Layer Urban Centres Database)
articles.core_conclusion: Across more than 10,000 cities (2000-2020), urban demographic change is highly uneven and hidden by national averages: the global urban dependency ratio fell from 0.87 to 0.59, smaller cities stayed younger (especially in Africa), sex ratios diverged sharply (male surpluses in MENA tied to labor migration), and 45% of urban growth came from net migration vs. 55% from natural increase, with large between-city variation.

articles.title: Predicting schistosome transmission in rural Uganda using water contact data from wearable GPS devices
methods: radius of gyration for human mobility
recursion analysis for GPS contact detection
data_sources: Wearable GPS loggers (i-gotU GT-600, Mobile Action Technology)
SchistoTrack cohort (field mapping + Kato-Katz infection data)
Open Data Kit Collect household surveys
articles.core_conclusion: Ten days of wearable GPS data from 452 individuals in rural Uganda show that simple exponential spatial decay functions of household distance predict individual-level open-water contact (AUROC = 0.87) and tap/borehole usage (AUROC = 0.92) without needing mobility or WASH covariates. Integrating decay-based visitation rules into a spatially explicit individual-based model reproduced observed one-year S. mansoni reinfection prevalence (40.5%) and ranked water sites by transmission potential. Safe-water provision (WASH) failed to displace open-water contact even in counterfactual scenarios, with fewer than 2% of individuals modeled to fully substitute.

articles.title: Tension between spatial pattern and hydrological adaptability in early Yangtze River Basin settlements
articles.core_conclusion: Across 2,106 early settlements in the middle Yangtze (8.5-2.72 ka BP), hydrological factors consistently governed settlement location even as communities built hydraulic infrastructure to manage floods and water. Cultural decline still occurred, revealing a persistent asymmetry between settlement spatial pattern and hydrological adaptability that seeded long-term vulnerability in flood-prone environments.

articles.title: Reward magnitude determines reinforcement learning efficiency
articles.core_conclusion: Outsized rewards dramatically accelerate reinforcement learning efficiency in mice across five behavioral paradigms through dissociable effects on within-session acquisition, across-session consolidation, and task engagement. Ventral striatal dopamine release duration and magnitude scale continuously with reward size; prolonged optogenetic boosting of dopamine partially reproduces the large-reward learning advantage. Animals' reinforcement learning capacity has been systematically underestimated, and absolute reward magnitude — not merely its presence — governs task engagement via dopamine signaling.

articles.title: Tracking human mobility for wildlife conservation
methods: coupling human and animal movement data
data_sources: human mobility data (class; specific sources not stated)
animal movement / telemetry (class; specific sources not stated)
articles.core_conclusion: Large-scale human mobility data can expand ecological analyses of how people affect wild animals: by measuring where and when people actually go, mobility data turn the static human footprint into a dynamic, behaviorally explicit driver of wildlife disturbance, offering a new evidence base for conservation.

articles.title: A critical initialization for biological neural networks
data_sources: Two-photon calcium imaging (jGCaMP8s / GCaMP6f, Janelia Research Campus)
articles.core_conclusion: Spontaneous brainwide neural activity in mice is consistent with linear dynamics governed by a critically normalized symmetric random connectivity matrix, producing a 2/3 power-law eigenvalue spectrum. This critical initialization generates high-dimensional global activity modes that persist under sparse/clustered/spatial connectivity, and confers a zero-shot working memory advantage over non-symmetric and echo-state alternatives. Hippocampal CA1 is an exception, exhibiting a flatter spectrum consistent with an efficient, uncorrelated code.

articles.title: A deep-learning framework reveals whole-body perturbations at cell level
data_sources: Uchl1-eGFP reporter mouse vDISCO whole-body LSFM scans
Cd68-eGFP reporter mouse vDISCO whole-body LSFM scans
VesselFM pre-trained 3D vessel segmentation foundation model (Zenodo)
Human embryo beta3-tubulin LSFM dataset (hudeca.com, CC BY-NC 4.0)
Human post-mortem trigeminal ganglion proteomics (PRIDE PXD071172)
articles.core_conclusion: MouseMapper, a foundation-model-based deep-learning suite with three modules (Nerve, Immune, Tissue), enables automated whole-body 3D segmentation of peripheral nerves, immune cells, and 31 organs/tissues in the entire mouse body. Applied to diet-induced obesity, it revealed a previously unrecognized structural impairment of the infraorbital trigeminal nerve (58-61% reductions in nerve endings, edges, vertices) linked to functional sensory deficits, with conserved proteomic signatures (axon remodelling, complement pathways) in obese human trigeminal ganglia — establishing a scalable, open framework for systemic disease mapping at cellular resolution.

articles.title: Divergent urban storm response to convective, frontal and tropical systems
articles.core_conclusion: Urban effects on storms depend on storm type and scale. Local-scale single-cell and isolated convective storms become more frequent over cities (7-31%), especially at night; cold-frontal storms weaken over cities by 16-28% (roughness and thermal disruption) while warm fronts modestly intensify; tropical systems shift high-reflectivity cores to lower altitudes without changing frequency or intensity. This event-based, storm-type framework supersedes the simple urban wet/dry island model.

articles.title: Scaling intra-urban climate fluctuations
articles.core_conclusion: Across 142 cities worldwide, the full marginal and joint probability distributions of intra-urban temperature and PM air pollution collapse onto a small set of universal scaling functions inspired by finite-size scaling in statistical physics. A logarithmic relation links street-network properties to these climate fields, so average network features alone characterize the full variability of heat and pollution within and across cities, enabling climate information to enter reduced-complexity urban models.

articles.title: Unequal spatial patterns and differentiated city roles in the intercity telemedicine system
data_sources: China Digital Inclusive Finance Index (Peking University)
articles.core_conclusion: China's intercity telemedicine system forms a highly interconnected network that raises systemic efficiency but generates new inequalities by concentrating flows in a few dominant hubs. It partly breaks the traditional central-place hierarchy, reorganizing around medical expertise and patient need rather than nearest administrative center. City roles are sharply differentiated: dominant hubs centralize activity, many cities stay marginal, yet some low-resource cities use digital platforms to attract cross-provincial demand - a dual mechanism concentrating supply in top hubs while expanding outreach for ordinary cities.

articles.title: Tumour recurrence, gastrointestinal symptoms and inflammation associated with microplastic-positive intestinal tumours
data_sources: Clinical tumour specimens (188 intestinal tumours, China)
WHO Microplastics in Drinking Water report (2019)
articles.core_conclusion: Microplastics were detected in 56.4% of 188 clinical intestinal tumour specimens using complementary high-resolution spectroscopic and imaging techniques, with PET as the dominant polymer. MP-positive tumours displayed localized inflammation and histopathological changes, and patients with MP-positive tumours showed a trend toward higher recurrence rates and significantly elevated gastrointestinal symptom severity over 34 months of follow-up, implicating MPs as a potential contributor to colorectal cancer burden.

articles.title: Efficient robot navigation inspired by honeybee learning flights
authors: Guido C. H. E. de Croon
articles.core_conclusion: Bee-Nav, a navigation strategy inspired by honeybee learning flights, enables tiny drones to home accurately over long distances using a 3.4-42.3 kB self-supervised convolutional neural network trained during a brief learning flight around the home location. In real-world tests, the drone achieved 100% homing success within 0.5 m for 30-110 m flights and 70% for 200-600 m outdoor flights under windy conditions, using three orders of magnitude less memory than state-of-the-art map-based methods.

articles.title: Mortality from short-term exposure to particulate matter, nitrogen dioxide and ozone across Europe
data_sources: QML air pollution model (Chen et al. 2024)
articles.core_conclusion: Using daily mortality and air pollution exposure data from 653 regions across 31 European countries (2003–2019), short-term exposure to PM2.5, coarse particulates, NO2, and ozone each raises mortality risk by 0.54–1.14% per 10 µg/m³. Their combined effect accounts for an estimated 146,517 annual premature deaths (95% CI: 119,992–173,771). Vulnerability is strongly heterogeneous by sex and age, and uniform-risk models systematically underestimate burden in Eastern and Southeastern Europe.

articles.title: Reduced urban air pollution and mortality from the transition to new energy vehicles in China
methods: random forest ensemble (100 RF-based regression models)
data_sources: Ministry of Public Security of China (city-level vehicle ownership)
articles.core_conclusion: By 2023, China's new energy vehicle transition reduced PM2.5 by 23.80% (8.97 µg/m3) and CO by 30.67% (0.26 mg/m3) across 150 cities, preventing approximately 262,000 non-accidental deaths and 75,000 all-cause deaths. Benefits are concentrated in economically developed cities; reductions in PM10 and NO2 are low, revealing pollutant-specific disparities and socio-economic inequalities that call for heavy-duty diesel electrification and stronger NEV deployment in less-developed regions.

articles.title: State media control influences large language models
data_sources: GPT-4o, DeepSeek-R1, Claude Sonnet (commercial LLM audit)
articles.core_conclusion: Across six studies, government control of media already shapes LLM outputs through the training data pipeline. LLMs queried in the language of a country with lower media freedom return systematically more pro-government responses than the same queries in higher-freedom languages. A controlled pretraining experiment on Chinese state-coordinated media confirms a plausible causal mechanism, and cross-language audits of commercial models demonstrate that this effect extends beyond open-weight models, creating strong strategic incentives for states to leverage media control to shape AI at scale.

articles.title: Commuter loss and retention in US employment centers during COVID-19 and its aftermath
data_sources: MIT Election Lab U.S. House returns
articles.core_conclusion: Using >20 million monthly device traces (2019-2024), the authors identify US employment centers (ECs) and build a time-dependent commuting network, then use ML to explain commuter loss and recovery. Central-city ECs lost more commuters than suburban ones but recovered faster; centers with workers from Democratic-leaning districts lost more commuters; and about one-quarter of ECs never recovered from the initial pandemic shock.

articles.title: Global mapping of city-level economic growth decoupling from fossil fuels
data_sources: gridded admin-2 GDP per capita PPP (Zenodo v.3)
articles.core_conclusion: By combining satellite tropospheric NO2 (a fossil-fuel-combustion proxy) with admin-2 gridded GDP per capita PPP, the study builds a globally consistent, repeatable framework for tracking decoupling of urban economic growth from fossil fuels. Across 5,435 cities (2019-2024), 2,475 show significant trends; ~80% are green (cleaner + richer, mainly China/Europe/North America) and ~16% are brown (dirtier + richer, mainly India/Middle East). Results capture relative, not absolute, decoupling and serve as a scalable screening tool for green-growth and policy monitoring.

articles.title: Socio-economic factors impact vulnerability to and burden of heat- and cold-related mortality in Europe
articles.core_conclusion: Socio-economic deprivation and inequality amplify vulnerability to both heat and cold across 654 European regions, while higher GDP, life expectancy, and household income paradoxically increase heat vulnerability. The mortality burden attributable to socio-economic disparities is substantial: inability to keep homes warm (+301,799 excess deaths), population ageing (+183,071), and income inequality (+180,402) over 2000–2019. Equity-focused adaptation strategies are urgently needed.

articles.title: Plasticity and language in the anaesthetized human hippocampus
articles.core_conclusion: The human hippocampus continues to perform complex sensory processing under general anaesthesia: unconscious patients showed oddball tone discrimination, representational plasticity growing over ~10 minutes, and robust encoding of semantic and grammatical features of natural speech — including predictive coding of upcoming words — at the level of single neurons and local field potentials. These results demonstrate that high-order cognition in the hippocampus does not require conscious awareness.

articles.title: Domain generality is an emergent, not inherent, property of metacognition
data_sources: behavioural experiments (episodic memory + area perception tasks)
Open Science Framework (OSF) preregistrations, data and code
articles.core_conclusion: Confidence in memory versus perception is distinct yet commensurable: metacognitive sensitivity and efficiency do not correlate across domains, but bias does. From age six, children and adults can compare confidence across memory and perceptual decisions without an accuracy cost, indicating a shared internal scale. Domain-general confidence is therefore an emergent developmental achievement rather than an inherent property of metacognition.

articles.title: Improving access to essential medicines via decision-aware machine learning
data_sources: Sierra Leone National Health Facilities Dataset v01 (MoHS / Columbia CIESIN)
Oxford/MAP global friction surface 2019 (Google Earth Engine)
articles.core_conclusion: A decision-aware ML framework combining multi-task learning (for sample efficiency) and catalytic priors (for equitable allocation) was deployed nationwide in Sierra Leone as a medicine allocation decision support tool. Econometric evaluation showed an estimated 19% increase in consumption of allocated products in treated districts. The tool subsequently scaled to cover an estimated two million women and children under 5, demonstrating that ML can improve efficiency and equity in resource-constrained global health settings at very low cost.

articles.title: Training language models to be warm can reduce accuracy and increase sycophancy
articles.core_conclusion: Fine-tuning language models to be socially warm systematically degrades factual accuracy (+10 to +30 percentage-point higher error rates) and amplifies sycophancy — the tendency to validate incorrect user beliefs — particularly when users express sadness. These effects hold across five model architectures (8B to trillion-parameter scale) and are invisible to standard capability benchmarks (MMLU, GSM8K), revealing a fundamental warmth–accuracy trade-off that current evaluation pipelines fail to detect.

articles.title: A global survey on trust, digital health literacy and health information quality
data_sources: World Bank Country and Lending Groups (income classification)
articles.core_conclusion: Across 30 countries (N = 31,000 adults), the most valued qualities of health information are globally consistent (clarity, accessibility, source identification), while messenger credibility varies. Trust in healthcare providers was consistently higher than in government or community sources, and identical messages attributed to providers elicited stronger concern, appeal and behavioural intent. Most respondents preferred mixed words-and-images formats, but acceptance of AI-generated content varied widely.

articles.title: Decarbonizing emissions from last-mile deliveries in Chinese cities
data_sources: platform delivery orders & courier location traces
articles.core_conclusion: Using 14 billion orders and traces from 1.9 million couriers across 365 Chinese cities, the first nationwide fine-grained estimate of last-mile delivery GHG emissions shows emissions decouple from order growth (83.5% more orders gave only 31.3% more emissions), smaller lower-density cities emit up to 4x more per order due to lower delivery efficiency, and mitigation could cut emissions by up to 84.2%.

articles.title: Mobile phone data show spatial and socioeconomic inequalities in hospital utilization
data_sources: Fudan Chinese Hospital League Table (Fudan CHLT)
articles.core_conclusion: 80.63% of patients in 11 Chinese cities bypass their nearest hospital, incurring 211.84% extra travel distance. Low-SES patients show a paradox: lower bypass rate but stronger bypass intensity (8.02% more hospitals skipped), explained by 5.9-fold higher willingness to travel for quality as compensation for local medical quality deficits. This bypass-driven spatial sorting unintentionally reduces experienced social segregation within hospitals by 42.15% for low-SES groups, challenging strict bypass-control reforms and calling instead for strategic redistribution of high-level clinical capacity into underserved districts.

articles.title: Rapid earnings growth in finance concentrates top earnings in a few large cities
methods: country-level panel OLS with Driscoll-Kraay errors
data_sources: World Bank Global Financial Development Database (GFDD.DM.02)
articles.core_conclusion: Rapid earnings growth in finance concentrates top earnings in the few cities hosting national financial markets. Across ten global-north countries (1989-2019), workers in financial cities account for ~65% of the rise in the national top-1% earnings share, and financial sector jobs explain ~26% of national and ~50% of local top-earnings growth there. The pattern extends beyond global cities (New York, London, Tokyo) to smaller hubs (Frankfurt, Stockholm, Oslo, Madrid), driven by urban sectoral specialization and finance-sector rent-sharing rather than city scale or skill sorting alone.

articles.title: Impact of daylight saving time on physical activity patterns
data_sources: NIH All of Us Research Program (Fitbit wearables)
All of Us Researcher Workbench (code repository)
articles.core_conclusion: DST transitions produce no net change in total daily steps; activity is reallocated across times of day — fall transitions increase morning steps by ~202 and reduce evening steps by ~180, spring transitions show the reverse. Effects vary substantially by chronotype phenotype and demographics, suggesting structural constraints (rigid work schedules, neighbourhood safety) limit adaptive flexibility for some populations. Resting heart rate shows subtle intraday mirroring but is clinically insignificant.

articles.title: Evaluating large language models for accuracy incentivizes hallucinations
articles.core_conclusion: LLM hallucinations are a structural incentive problem, not merely a training deficiency. Next-word pretraining creates unavoidable statistical pressure toward guessing for rare singleton facts (proven via IIV reduction and singleton-rate lower bound), and dominant accuracy-based evaluations reward confident wrong answers over honest abstention. Open rubric evaluations — which explicitly state error penalties — realign incentives and allow frontier models to adopt hallucination-reduction mitigations without sacrificing headline accuracy scores.

articles.title: A multi-agent framework combining large language models with medical flowcharts for self-triage
articles.core_conclusion: A three-agent system (retrieval, decision, conversation) constrains an LLM to walk 100 published AMA self-triage flowcharts, combining free-text conversational flexibility with auditable clinical protocols. On large synthetic benchmarks it achieves 84.1% flowchart-retrieval accuracy (N=2,000) and 99.1% flowchart-navigation accuracy (N=37,200), showing that protocol grounding can make conversational AI self-triage transparent, accurate and trustworthy.

articles.title: Microplastics and nanoplastics in brain tumours and the healthy human brain
data_sources: Beijing Tiantan Hospital surgical cohort (primary data)
China National Human Brain Bank for Development and Function (CAMS & PUMC)
articles.core_conclusion: Microplastics and nanoplastics (MNPs) are present in 99.4% of 156 diseased brain samples from 113 brain-tumour patients and 100% of 35 healthy post-mortem brain samples. MNP concentrations are significantly elevated in peri-tumoural tissue compared to healthy brain, implicating the compromised blood-brain barrier as an MNP entry route. A positive correlation is found between microplastic surface area and tumour proliferation (Ki-67 index), though causal links remain unestablished.

articles.title: Urban wetlands as hotspots of antibiotic resistomes and their potential viral transmission
data_sources: Urban raw sewage / sewage effluent datasets
articles.core_conclusion: Across 17 urban wetlands in China, antibiotic resistance genes occur at average abundances about nine times higher than in natural lakes and comparable to raw urban sewage, making these green-infrastructure wetlands resistome hotspots. They harbor human bacterial pathogens and horizontal-gene-transfer signals, and ARG-carrying phages may spread resistance through bacterial communities. Higher economic development is associated with lower ARG abundance, implying sewer-infrastructure quality shapes the geography of resistance; stormwater should be treated before reaching wetlands.

articles.title: Visible expressions of Islam in public space affect Muslim immigrants' place integration
articles.core_conclusion: Contrary to the assumption that visible Islam drives Muslim immigrants to withdraw, in-situ exposure to embedded Islamic symbols increases approach-oriented behaviour (slower pace, lower acceleration, lingering, measured from GPS) and raises place attachment. Twin AR field experiments in Paris (N=151) and London (N=147) show medium effects (d=0.51-0.63, P<0.05), corroborated by surveys (N=403+400). The pattern holds across two opposite religious-visibility policy regimes.

articles.title: Public use of a generalist LLM chatbot for health queries
articles.core_conclusion: Analysis of 617,827 de-identified Microsoft Copilot health conversations reveals that nearly one in five involves personal symptom assessment; personal health queries spike in the evening and nighttime when traditional healthcare is least accessible; device type strongly predicts intent (mobile = personal health, desktop = professional/academic); and one in seven personal health queries concerns a dependent, positioning conversational AI as a caregiving tool.

articles.title: Composable neural emulators accelerate thermoelectric generator design
methods: composable emulator assembly via constant-current rule
data_sources: COMSOL Multiphysics finite-element simulations (in-house generated training data)
articles.core_conclusion: TEGNet, a composable neural network emulator, predicts thermoelectric generator performance with greater than 99% accuracy while using only 0.01% of the computational time of commercial finite-element solvers. Its composability — enabled by the constant-current rule of series-connected TE legs — allows material-specific emulators to be assembled at inference time for segmented and n–p paired generator architectures without retraining. Experimentally validated devices achieved conversion efficiencies of 9.3% and 8.7%, ranking competitively high among published TE generators.

articles.title: Language models transmit behavioural traits through hidden signals in data
methods: semantic filtering (LLM judge + hard-coded banned terms)
articles.core_conclusion: Model distillation causes subliminal learning: a student fine-tuned on teacher-generated data inherits the teacher's behavioural traits — including misalignment — even when those traits are completely absent from the data semantically. The effect is demonstrated across number sequences, Python code, and chain-of-thought reasoning traces, and occurs only when teacher and student share the same base-model initialization. A formal theorem proves this is a general property of neural networks under broad conditions.

articles.title: The global economic burden of digestive tract diseases and cancers from 2020 to 2050
methods: counterfactual scenario analysis (disease eradication vs. status quo)
articles.core_conclusion: Digestive tract diseases and neoplasms (DTDNs) will impose a US$26.1 trillion global economic burden from 2020 to 2050, equivalent to a 0.65% annual GDP tax across 190 countries. Cirrhosis and chronic liver diseases account for the largest share (30.2%). The burden is concentrated in upper-middle-income countries (46.2%) and shifts from infection-related to lifestyle-related conditions as income rises, while physical capital losses dominate in high-income countries and human capital losses dominate in low-income countries.

articles.title: The linkage between microbial community dynamics and urbanization age
methods: GAIA-derived urbanization age metric (1985–2018, 30 m resolution, 1-km neighbourhood average)
Kraken2 + Bracken taxonomic profiling against NCBI RefSeq
genome-resolved functional annotation (metaSPAdes, MetaBAT2, Prodigal, eggNOG-mapper, KEGG level 4)
co-occurrence network analysis (Spearman correlation, BH correction, Gephi closeness centrality)
TITAN threshold indicator taxa analysis (500 bootstrap replicates)
data_sources: GAIA global artificial impervious area dataset (urbanization age)
World Bank Climate Change Knowledge Portal (temperature)
articles.core_conclusion: Across 2,110 metagenomic samples from 45 cities on 6 continents, increasing urbanization age drives a progressive decline in microbial alpha diversity, a compositional shift from nature-associated to human-associated microorganisms, and a rise in the relative abundance of opportunistic human pathogens. A critical tipping point at approximately 20 years of urbanization marks abrupt shifts in multiple pathogenic species. These temporal microbial dynamics, quantified via a GAIA-derived urbanization age metric, demand stage-targeted public health policies and urban regeneration strategies tailored to each neighbourhood's urbanization trajectory.

articles.title: Ubiquitous mobility data enable near-real-time emission estimates for urban traffic
data_sources: anonymized mobile phone location data (1.75 million devices)
articles.core_conclusion: Fusing footage from more than 300 traffic cameras with anonymized location data from 1.75 million mobile devices, and coupling DTALite dynamic traffic assignment with MOVES-Matrix microscopic emission modeling, enables near-real-time road-level estimates of PM2.5, NOx, CO and CO2 across Manhattan. Manhattan's congestion pricing programme led to a 16-22% reduction in traffic emissions within 8 weeks of implementation. Conventional emission inventories introduce systematic biases: omitting traffic signals underestimates PM2.5 by 49%, while using citywide average fleet composition or speed shifts NOx estimates by -15% to +25%.

articles.title: How NGOs reshape urban green space justice in Beijing
articles.core_conclusion: In Beijing's constrained governance environment, NGOs strategically downplay distributive inequities in urban green space and instead pursue procedural justice through supervision, networking, advocacy and legal frameworks to secure participation. On the recognitional dimension they subtly alter dominant narratives and align with official expectations to stay operational. The study highlights counter-narratives, information disclosure and earlier NGO involvement in planning as the key levers for advancing UGS justice.

articles.title: The gentrification paradox of green–blue adaptation in African cities
articles.core_conclusion: In the first continent-wide causal assessment (5,503 cities/municipalities across 32 African countries, two decades of satellite imagery plus socio-economic panels, difference-in-differences), green-blue adaptation infrastructure is causally associated with rising housing prices, incomes, consumption and population growth — signs of early-stage gentrification. Climate-resilient greening can therefore deepen socio-spatial inequality and displacement unless paired with equitable housing and land-use policy.

articles.title: Multiomics and deep learning dissect regulatory syntax in human development
articles.core_conclusion: The Human Development Multiomic Atlas (HDMA) profiles chromatin accessibility and gene expression from 817,740 fetal cells across 12 organs (PCW 10-23), mapping 1,032,273 candidate cis-regulatory elements and 203 cell types. Deep learning models (ChromBPNet) decode a lexicon of 508 regulatory motifs, uncovering hard syntax rules (precise motif spacing/orientation) and soft rules (flexible arrangements) mediating transcription factor cooperativity. Disruption of positive and negative motifs by genetic variants produces concordant directional effects on gene expression, linking fetal regulatory grammar to human disease risk.

articles.title: Single-cell spatiotemporal dissection of the human maternal–fetal interface
data_sources: 10x Genomics Chromium Next GEM Multiome (Stanford/UCSF tissue banks)
articles.core_conclusion: A comprehensive spatiotemporally resolved single-cell multiomic atlas of the human maternal-fetal interface across GW5-39, integrating 191,735 paired snRNA-seq/snATAC-seq nuclei with ~1.1 million submicrometre spatial transcriptomic profiles and CODEX multiplex imaging, reveals novel arterial endothelial state transitions during spiral artery remodelling, a novel DSC4 stromal subtype that suppresses trophoblast invasion via endocannabinoid/CB1 signalling, a LASSO model predicting EVT invasiveness from transcriptomes, and cell-type-resolved genetic risk maps for pre-eclampsia, preterm birth, and miscarriage.

articles.title: Global health benefits and cost-effectiveness of indoor air purification to mitigate PM2.5 from wildfire smoke
methods: spatio-temporal stratification by income group and continent
data_sources: Han & Zhao 2025 residential air exchange rate dataset
articles.core_conclusion: Indoor air purifiers could avert 60.8 million DALYs attributable to wildfire PM2.5 and 2.2 billion DALYs from all-source PM2.5 globally over 2003-2021. Interventions are cost-effective in high-income countries but face severe affordability constraints in LMICs, exposing a stark global equity divide that requires targeted subsidies and international climate-health finance to resolve.

articles.title: Impacts of nature-inclusive urban development on well-being and fairness perceptions
authors: Sophus O. S. E. zu Ermgassen
articles.core_conclusion: Even an ecologically successful nature-inclusive urban development can produce uneven social outcomes. In Qunli New Town, former agriculturalists reported a sharp happiness decline (OR=0.15), while happiness gains tracked perceived social (OR=1.30) and aesthetic (OR=1.53) value of green space, not housing. Fairness perceptions diverged by settlement wave: original residents saw the change as economically fairer (OR=1.93) but ecologically less fair (OR=0.54) than newcomers, driven by rural reference points and loss of customary landscape access turned into exclusive club goods.

articles.title: Large-scale implementation of non-invasive prenatal testing for fetal aneuploidies
methods: relative risk analysis for adverse neonatal outcomes
data_sources: City-level GDP per capita (public statistical data)
articles.core_conclusion: A fully publicly funded, province-wide first-tier NIPT programme covering 1,185,416 pregnant women in Hebei, China (2019-2022) achieved 93.3% population coverage and demonstrated NIPT's feasibility at scale. NIPT reduced invasive prenatal diagnosis by 97.4% compared to maternal serum screening with better cost-effectiveness. Critically, NIPT-positive results — including false positives — identify pregnancies at significantly elevated risk for adverse perinatal outcomes, suggesting NIPT as a broader pregnancy risk indicator beyond chromosomal anomalies.

articles.title: Large-scale screening of blinding eye diseases from routine blood tests
methods: subgroup analysis by age and comorbidity
data_sources: Supplementary source data (available from corresponding author via DUA)
articles.core_conclusion: A dual-stage machine learning framework (MES) trained on routine blood tests achieves AUC 0.926-0.956 for binary detection of blinding eye diseases and macro-AUC 0.889-0.900 for classifying seven subtypes, with PPV/NPV above 0.90 in real-world hospital and community settings across more than 600,000 participants. The framework provides a scalable, non-invasive, non-specialist triage tool for prioritizing ophthalmic referral in underserved settings.

articles.title: Role of community and sexual contacts as drivers of MPXV clade I
data_sources: Zimbabwe POLYMOD contact survey (socialmixr R package)
Prem et al. synthetic contact matrices
articles.core_conclusion: MPXV clade Ib has a higher outbreak potential than clade Ia because it has established a sexual transmission route among high-activity individuals (FSWs and clients) in addition to community contacts, raising the effective reproduction number above the epidemic threshold of 1 (estimated ~1.5 in the Kivus). Sexual contacts account for 14-62% of R_eff depending on geographical setting (highest in Kamituga at 62%). Prioritising FSW-focused vaccination substantially reduces mass vaccination doses required for control.

articles.title: The role of tech workers in ethnicity- and class-based urban segregation
data_sources: Estonian Population and Housing Censuses 2011 & 2021 (Statistics Estonia registers)
articles.core_conclusion: In digitally transforming Tallinn, minority tech workers' residential outcomes align with class rather than ethnicity: dissimilarity from majority tech workers fell 14 points (47->33) over 2011-2021, an ethnic desegregation not seen among other professionals. But the same professionalization deepens class-based segregation, leaving non-tech minorities more spatially isolated, thereby decoupling ethnicity from class.

articles.title: Mapping pesticide mixtures to cancer risk at the country scale with spatial exposomics
methods: developmental lineage stratification of cancer cases
spatial autocorrelation (Moran's I, Monte Carlo permutation)
articles.core_conclusion: A 100 m-resolution process-based pesticide risk model covering 95.7% of Peru's territory (31 active ingredients, 2014-2019), fused with a Bayesian spatial epidemiology model applied to 158,072 cancer registry cases (2007-2020) stratified by developmental lineage, identifies 436 pesticide-associated cancer hotspots concentrated in rural Andean and Amazonian Indigenous territories with relative risks of 1.14-9.38. Transcriptomic profiling of liver tissue from 36 Peruvian hepatocellular carcinoma patients in hotspot zones reveals a distinct non-genotoxic exposure signature and early disruption of lineage-specific master transcription factor networks in pre-tumour tissue, providing molecular evidence that complex pesticide mixtures drive carcinogenesis through epigenetic destabilization of core regulatory circuitries even when individual compounds are classified as non-carcinogenic.

articles.title: Uncovering the hidden role of peri-urban vegetation in modulating urban precipitation
articles.core_conclusion: Across 1,029 cities worldwide, vegetation-driven changes in peri-urban evapotranspiration are carried by atmospheric moisture transport into the urban core, contributing only 1.9% of annual urban precipitation but a disproportionate 18.3% of its long-term increase. This spatially coupled land-atmosphere mechanism strengthens with more surrounding vegetation, wind-aligned greening, and lower background humidity, arguing that peri-urban ecosystems must be integrated into urban climate-resilience planning.

articles.title: The global burden of hypertension preventable by urban greenness
articles.core_conclusion: Raising urban greenness to the 95th percentile of national NDVI distributions could prevent 11.7% of global hypertension prevalence (2019 estimate). African regions and low-to-intermediate SDI countries bear the highest preventable burden; older adults (65–79 years) benefit most consistently. Cross-country inequality in preventable burden grew from 2001 to 2019, highlighting an urgent need for equitable urban greening policies.

articles.title: Aversive learning hijacks a brain sugar sensor to consolidate memory
methods: GAL4/UAS binary expression with TARGET system (tub-GAL80ts)
data_sources: FAFB full adult fly brain EM connectome (FlyWire)
Davie et al. 2018 single-cell transcriptomics (~60k fly brain cells; SCope)
Behavioral T-maze performance indices (source data provided with paper)
In vivo two-photon imaging (unprocessed images available on request)
FlyPad feeding assay data (source data provided with paper)
articles.core_conclusion: Spaced repetition of aversive olfactory learning in Drosophila hijacks the brain's internal fructose sensor (Gr43a neurons) by inducing a fasted-like state through cholinergic disinhibition of upstream dFB neurons via FB.5/6 neurons and mAchR-B. Post-training sugar intake activates the disinhibited Gr43a neurons, which release thyrostimulin (Gpa2-Gpb5) to trigger metabolic activation of mushroom-body alpha/beta neurons via their Lgr1 receptor, consolidating long-term memory. The same mechanism operates in appetitive learning, establishing thyrostimulin as a universal consolidation signal. Spaced training also drives non-homeostatic sugar appetite, providing a neural circuit basis for emotional eating.

articles.title: Ecological and demographic drivers of Oropouche virus transmission
data_sources: Brazilian Ministry of Health notification system
articles.core_conclusion: Oropouche fever in Brazil (2014-2025) is strongly rural: cumulative incidence in rural municipalities was 11.3 times higher than urban, inverting dengue/chikungunya/Zika patterns. OROV underwent major geographic expansion in 2024 with Rt peaks of 3.2-5.5. Municipality-level risk maps reveal that risk heterogeneity is driven by interactions among demographic, climatic, and environmental conditions.

articles.title: Mapping the local effectiveness of mass drug administration for malaria using transportability methods
methods: effect modification analysis (ratio and additive scale)
data_sources: Parent cluster RCT (NCT04864444) — 60 villages Tambacounda District Senegal 2020-2022
MODIS MOD21C3 land surface temperature (0.05° monthly)
JRC Monthly Water History v1.4 (30-m surface water)
articles.core_conclusion: A doubly robust transportability model combining machine learning with free satellite data (CHIRPS, MODIS, WorldPop) generalized MDA effectiveness from a 60-village Senegalese trial to 116 non-trial communes, estimating 36–65% incidence reductions in 74 communes during the intervention year. Effects were not sustained post-intervention, confirming MDA's short-lived nature and providing a scalable framework for subnational malaria intervention targeting.

articles.title: The impact of social prescribing on well-being outcomes in a nationwide analysis
methods: conditional growth models with interaction terms
data_sources: PSSRU Costs of Health and Social Care
HM Treasury Green Book WELLBY metric
articles.core_conclusion: A UK nationwide analysis of 14,657-19,627 patients across 300+ social prescribing sites demonstrates consistent, sizeable well-being improvements within 1-6 months of referral: SWEMWBS +3.31 points, happiness +1.59, life satisfaction +1.57, worthwhileness +1.40, anxiety -1.45. The life-satisfaction gain equates to a return on investment of £9 per £1 invested (2019 prices). Well-being improvements are broadly consistent across sociodemographic groups, supporting the universal applicability of social prescribing.

articles.title: Dietary selenium from soil micronutrients and risk of incident type 2 diabetes
data_sources: High-resolution soil selenium maps (China and global, Jones et al. PNAS 2017)
articles.core_conclusion: Soil selenium content is a significant predictor of serum selenium in Chinese residents even after adjusting for diet, socioeconomics, and air quality. Men in the highest serum selenium tertile (107.2-162.8 µg/L) had a 35% higher risk of incident type 2 diabetes (RR=1.35; 95% CI=1.02-1.77) vs. the lowest tertile, with a strong effect in men (RR=1.96) but not women. Globally, ~14% of land area housing approximately 1 billion people has high soil selenium, placing residents at elevated diabetes risk from their geological environment alone.

articles.title: Long-term public health impact of doxycycline post-exposure prophylaxis on syphilis transmission
data_sources: Singapore Ministry of Health Communicable Disease Surveillance Reports
UK GOV.UK STI Annual Data Tables (Public Health England / UKHSA)
SingStat Table Builder (Department of Statistics Singapore)
articles.core_conclusion: Over 15 years, targeting high-risk MSM (>5 partners per year) at diagnosis is the most efficient doxy-PEP enrolment strategy (2.50 cases averted per enrolment in Singapore; 4.60 in England). Broader clinic-wide enrolment averts more cases in absolute terms but with far lower per-enrolment efficiency (as few as 0.02-0.04 cases per enrolment). Frequent STI screening every 3-4 months substantially amplifies programme impact. Emergence of tetracycline-resistant Neisseria gonorrhoeae must be proactively monitored.

articles.title: Infrastructure for regional food systems to feed cities at a time of deglobalization
data_sources: field interviews and site visits (confidential, ethics-restricted)
Agriculture and Agri-Food Canada public mapping datasets
Ontario Food Terminal Board Annual Report 2022-2023
articles.core_conclusion: As globalization retreats and climate change threatens long supply chains, cities cannot assume uninterrupted global produce flows. Using the Ontario Food Terminal as a working example, the authors show that scaled-up regional food system infrastructure plus protected nearby agricultural land reliably connects farmers to urban consumers and supports food security, making the case to protect regional foodsheds and the physical infrastructure that lets cities feed themselves when trade is disrupted.

articles.title: Integrated photonic neural network with on-chip backpropagation training
articles.core_conclusion: The first integrated photonic deep neural network trained end-to-end with on-chip gradient-descent backpropagation is demonstrated on a single silicon photonic chip. All linear and nonlinear computations — including activation gradients — execute optically, achieving over 90% accuracy on two nonlinear classification tasks and matching a reference digital model without using a digital computer for training. The approach is robust to fabrication-induced device variations and generalizes across photonic neural network architectures.

articles.title: Characterization and forecast of global influenza subtype dynamics
data_sources: UN World Population Prospects / World Urbanization Prospects
articles.core_conclusion: Representing each country's annual influenza subtype mix (A/H1N1pdm, A/H3N2, B) as a compositional trajectory via the isometric log-ratio transform reveals a hidden spatial structure in global subtype circulation shaped chiefly by international air mobility, epidemic synchrony and climate. Countries split into two macroregions (synchronous alternation vs persistent mixing), and a Bayesian hierarchical VAR that encodes this global history improves one-year-ahead subtype-composition forecasts over naive baselines (dominance-state accuracy 34% vs 27%; AUROC up to 0.82-0.91 for some observables).

articles.title: The trade-off between microbial functionality and evolutionary flexibility under urbanization
data_sources: Pearl River Delta urban-park & forest soil survey
articles.core_conclusion: Urban parks in the Pearl River Delta show higher microbial alpha diversity, biomass, and nutrient-cycling functional genes than natural forests (driven by nutrient enrichment and soil pH), but genomic analysis reveals a trade-off: urban parks enhance short-term functionality while reducing genome size and evolutionary flexibility, whereas forest soils retain higher genomic diversity and long-term resilience.

articles.title: Empirical evidence of air pollution reduction from electric vehicle usage across Chinese cities
articles.core_conclusion: Using real EV charging records from 2016-2023 across 292 Chinese cities, a 10% increase in EV charging is associated with -0.08% surface NO2 and -0.04% PM2.5, with no significant effect on ambient O3. Benefits are heterogeneous: stronger at extreme cold (below -7 degC) despite reduced battery performance, and larger in southern than northern cities due to higher utilization rates. EV air-quality gains depend on actual usage, not just adoption.

articles.title: Neighborhood street connectivity and hippocampus volume in older adults
articles.core_conclusion: In 500+ Sydney adults aged >70 with up to three MRI scans over six years, those living in more highly interconnected neighborhoods (higher street-intersection density) had a larger hippocampal tail (a navigation-related subregion) but also a steeper decline in tail volume over time, with a slight rebound after age 85. The effect is subregion-specific (tail, not head/body), suggesting connected street networks shape navigation-related brain structure and that street design is a potential lever for brain health.

articles.title: Safety of a large language model-based clinical decision support system in African primary healthcare
data_sources: Kenya Ministry of Health Clinical Guidelines
articles.core_conclusion: An EMR-embedded GPT-4o CDSS deployed across 16 Kenyan primary care clinics showed near-perfect guideline alignment (99%) and low hallucination rates (3.4%) in 1,469 evaluated encounters, but produced potentially harmful recommendations in 7.8% of cases. Critically, harmful outputs were adopted by clinicians at a disproportionately higher rate (58%) than beneficial ones (22%), revealing an adoption asymmetry that underscores the need for usability optimization, local guardrails, and prospective trials before scaling AI-assisted care in low-resource settings.

articles.title: Benefit of physical activity initiatives for climate change mitigation and adaptation
articles.core_conclusion: Physical inactivity and climate change are interconnected crises best addressed together. The PACC conceptual model shows how the WHO 'eight investments that work for physical activity' - especially active transport and active urban design - can simultaneously reduce GHG emissions, support climate adaptation and advance health equity. A double scoping review and a Denver cycling case study reveal both the co-benefits and the major gaps (HIC-skewed evidence) and unintended consequences (gentrification, electric-car over-reliance) that systems-based, community-co-designed solutions must avoid.

articles.title: China's high-speed rail widens urban–rural disparities in air pollution and public health
articles.core_conclusion: Rural China is no cleaner than urban China, yet high-speed rail lets urban areas cut waste-gas emission intensity, PM2.5 and cardiovascular-disease deaths far more than rural areas, widening the urban–rural gap. The mechanism is a spatial redistribution of resources toward cities (urban firms gain resources and efficiency), and the unequal effect grows over time and persists across geographical distances.

articles.title: Matching clinicians with clinical trials using AI
methods: ClinicalBERT text encoding with mean pooling
multi-objective genetic algorithm for fairness optimization
concordance correlation coefficient (CCC) for cost estimation
temporal split evaluation (8:1:1 by trial start date)
articles.core_conclusion: DocTr, a cross-modal deep learning framework integrating ClinicalBERT trial-document embeddings, ICD-10 patient encounter vectors and an OpenPayments-derived enrolment graph, achieves 58% higher clinician-trial match similarity than the best baseline on 24,984 clinicians and 5,210 US trials under temporal evaluation. A genetic algorithm post-processing step increases racial and ethnic fairness entropy by up to 25% compared with ground-truth enrolment while reducing competing trial conflicts per recommended clinician to near zero, and embedding-similarity cost estimation achieves CCC = 0.83 for total trial cost.

articles.title: Cumulative daily exposure to high temperature increases gestational diabetes risk
methods: sensitivity analysis (exposure window, diagnosis date shift)
data_sources: SIDIAP (Information System for Research in Primary Care)
articles.core_conclusion: Cumulative exposure to 30 days of high ambient temperature is associated with a twofold increased risk of gestational diabetes in the Barcelona metropolitan area (8,796 cases, 2011-2022). The heat-GD association is disproportionately stronger in the most socioeconomically deprived neighbourhoods, revealing a climate-health equity gap. Distributed lag nonlinear models show that the effect accumulates over multi-day windows rather than from single extreme events.

articles.title: Advancing operational global aerosol forecasting with machine learning
methods: latitude-weighted RMSE and spatial Pearson R evaluation
data_sources: AERONET ground-based sun photometer network (V3.0, L2.0)
SDS-WAS dust models: FMI-SILAM, CMA-CUACE/Dust, JMA-MASINGAR, KMA-ADAM3
articles.core_conclusion: AI-GAMFS, a vision transformer + U-Net model trained on 42 years of MERRA-2 reanalysis, delivers global 5-day 3-hourly aerosol forecasts in under 1 minute, outperforming CAMS and four regional dust models in AOD and DUAOD skill, and improving surface aerosol component forecasts over the USA and China compared with GEOS-FP at ~360x lower computational cost.

articles.title: Merlin: a computed tomography vision–language foundation model and dataset
articles.core_conclusion: Merlin is a 3D vision-language foundation model for abdominal CT interpretation trained on >6 million images, >1.8 million diagnosis codes, and >6 million report tokens from 15,331 CT scans without additional manual annotation. Evaluated on 752 tasks across 6 task types, Merlin outperforms 2D VLMs, CT foundation models, and off-the-shelf radiology models. External validation on 44,098 CT scans across 3 independent clinical sites confirms strong institutional and anatomical generalization. The authors release models, code, and 25,494 paired CT-report dataset to the research community.

articles.title: Cost-effective case-finding strategies for cognitive impairment with digital twin simulations
methods: Conditional Tabular GAN (CTGAN) for synthetic population generation
data_sources: Ministry of Health Singapore facility and cost data
articles.core_conclusion: Without active case finding, 79.5% of cognitive impairment cases in Singapore remain undiagnosed. A digital twin of 753,905 older adults built with CTGAN shows that screening adults aged ≥75 with subjective cognitive concerns achieves optimal reach-to-cost balance (48.3% undiagnosed; SGD$25.3 per new diagnosis). The optimal strategy varies with willingness-to-pay: highly targeted approaches suit resource-limited settings while broader strategies become preferable as budgets grow. Low screening-test specificity markedly inflates downstream health system costs.

articles.title: Intercity economic ties amplify natural disasters spillovers in China
data_sources: China Industrial and Commercial Enterprises Database (enterprise registration records)
articles.core_conclusion: Natural-disaster losses propagate along intercity economic linkages, not just geographic proximity. Combining geocoded disaster data with firm-level cross-city investment records in spatial Durbin models with economic spatial weight matrices, the authors find that ignoring economic ties underestimates total losses by 52.5%-57.5%. High closeness-centrality and high-clustering cities amplify negative spillovers, and the effect intensifies as the investment network densifies over time.

articles.title: Eastern coastal Chinese diet associated with reduced obesity and improved cardiometabolic health
data_sources: 16S rRNA gut microbiota sequencing (Illumina HiSeq PE-250; GSA accession CRA037609)
ICD-10 disease and mortality registries (electronic linkage)
Human Metabolome Database (HMDB) for metabolite annotation
articles.core_conclusion: Unsupervised k-means clustering of dietary data from 8,931 WELL-China cohort participants identified the 'EastDiet' — a healthful pattern adopted by 46.8% of eastern coastal Chinese, featuring high intakes of vegetables, fruits, seafood, freshwater fish, soy products, edible fungi, starchy tubers, whole grains, dairy and eggs, and low intakes of refined rice, red meat and fried foods. Adherence was associated with 17% lower odds of central obesity (OR 0.83) and 22% lower risk of incident MACE (HR 0.78) over a median 6.3-year follow-up. Multiomics profiling revealed 96 metabolites and 21 microbial genera significantly associated with the EastDiet, with indole-3-propionic acid (IPA) emerging as a key mediator. Results were replicated in an independent ABO cohort of 1,851 participants.

articles.title: Care networks as unanticipated gains of local public policy
data_sources: SIDICU / Manzanas del Cuidado policy documentation
Colombian time-use and Gini context (cited literature)
articles.core_conclusion: Bogotá's District Care System (SIDICU) and its neighborhood Care Blocks improve recognition of care work and give marginalized low-income women first-time access to public institutions. Beyond these intended goals, an unanticipated benefit emerges: peer support networks form among caregivers who meet at the Care Blocks. The policy does not resolve the structural burden of unremunerated care but is an important step toward more equal and caring cities.

articles.title: Compact deep neural network models of the visual cortex
data_sources: macaque V1 and IT electrophysiology datasets
natural image pools (500k for search, 12M for distillation)
articles.core_conclusion: Deep neural networks with 60 million parameters are not necessary to predict individual visual cortex neurons: compact models with 5,000x fewer parameters achieve comparable accuracy after knowledge distillation. The compression reveals a shared computational motif — early filters are common across neurons, but each neuron's selectivity arises through a distinct consolidation step — providing interpretable, testable circuit hypotheses for V1, V4, and IT.

articles.title: The burden of malaria-attributable maternal anaemia and the impact of preventive treatment across sub-Saharan Africa
methods: spatially explicit burden estimation at 0.2-degree grid
maximum-likelihood IPTp-SP efficacy calibration to RCT data
mixed-effects regression with natural cubic splines
data_sources: West Africa trial (Burkina Faso, Gambia, Ghana, Mali; 2010-2011)
Cochrane review of IPTp-SP placebo-controlled trials
articles.core_conclusion: Malaria in pregnancy remains a major driver of maternal anaemia in sub-Saharan Africa: in 2023, 13.1 million pregnancies were exposed to P. falciparum, and without preventive measures this would have caused 2.41 million cases of moderate-to-severe anaemia (Hb < 9 g/dL) including 600,000 severe cases (Hb < 7 g/dL). A 32% decline in transmission since 2000 produced only a 22% reduction in anaemia burden because declining exposure has eroded multigravid women's pregnancy-specific immunity, broadening risk across gravidity groups. Under current IPTp-SP coverage (~55% receiving ≥2 doses), the intervention averted approximately 1.10 million moderate-to-severe anaemia cases in 2023; any disruption to IPTp delivery risks rapid resurgence of severe maternal anaemia across all gravidity groups.

articles.title: Airborne magnetic nanoparticles pose pulmonary risks to urban commuters evidenced by Hangzhou and Zhengzhou subways
data_sources: human lung tissue (commuters and rural contrast)
articles.core_conclusion: Subway air contains a substantial fraction of friction-derived iron-oxide magnetic nanoparticles, including airborne Fe3O4 with a magnetic core encapsulated in an amorphous SiO2 shell, originating mainly from wheel-rail and brake-wheel friction.
