The same Fe3O4 nanoparticles were detected in subway commuters' lung tissue, and mouse inhalation experiments confirmed they induce pronounced lung injury, establishing them as a tangible public-health risk.

articles.title: Homogenization and differentiation of urban tree assemblages globally
articles.core_conclusion: After controlling for differences among regional species pools, there is no distinct global pattern of urban tree homogenization or differentiation. The effect is scale-dependent: assemblages homogenize across broad geographic distances but differentiate at short distances, and nonnative species are the main driver - sharing the same nonnatives homogenizes distant cities while sharing different nonnatives differentiates nearby ones.

articles.title: Migratory bird stopover patterns linked to urbanization and social landscapes
data_sources: US Census household income / low-income data
articles.core_conclusion: Using weather-surveillance-radar estimates of migratory bird stopover across the continental USA (2,130 parks in 88 urban areas), cities host disproportionately many stopover hotspots relative to their land area, with ~half inside Metropolitan Statistical Areas (spring 48%, fall 44%). The urbanization-stopover relationship is regionally contingent (negative in eastern flyways, positive in western ones) and stopover is positively correlated with household income on average but weak or reversed in many cities, establishing radar remote sensing as a tool for macroscale urban biodiversity and equity science.

articles.title: Multidecadal legacy of uneven urbanization on divergent prospects for bird biodiversity
methods: equilibrium vs non-equilibrium species distribution modeling
data_sources: bird occurrence records (source not explicitly named; cleaned with CoordinateCleaner)
bird life-history traits (AVONET, Chinese-bird traits, Avibase)
articles.core_conclusion: Urban bird communities in China are not in equilibrium with current cities: 31 years of uneven urbanization have created widespread extinction debts (future losses) and colonization credits (future gains) across taxonomic, functional and phylogenetic dimensions. Different urban drivers act with different lags, with historical vegetation cover and anthropogenic activity having the longest-lasting effects, and species-specific lags correlate with each species' urban tolerance, implying conservation must be forward-looking.

articles.title: Promoting avian diversity in cities requires multiple parks with variable features
articles.core_conclusion: No single park design maximizes urban bird biodiversity. Across 935 parks in 186 US cities, species richness rises with park area and water, phylogenetic diversity is higher near the urban boundary, and functional diversity is higher where habitat heterogeneity is lower. The effect of tree canopy cover flips sign geographically (beneficial in the Intermountain West, negative in eastern cities for richness and phylogenetic diversity). Conserving all facets requires a portfolio of multiple parks with variable features, tuned regionally and seasonally.

articles.title: A roadmap for evaluating moral competence in large language models
articles.core_conclusion: Current LLM evaluations test moral performance (whether outputs are acceptable) but not moral competence (whether they are generated via morally relevant reasoning). Three challenges — the facsimile problem, moral multidimensionality, and moral pluralism — require a new evaluation paradigm combining adversarial, parametric, and cross-cultural assessments. Responsible attribution of moral competence to LLMs demands scientifically grounded evidence rather than benchmark accuracy alone.

articles.title: Geospatial insights of health vulnerability during the Ukraine war
data_sources: News sources / UNICEF / Ukrainian government ministries
WHO Surveillance System for Attacks on Health Care
articles.core_conclusion: Combining conflict-intensity data, humanitarian-access records, environmental stressors, and infrastructure damage across 461 Ukrainian hromadas, this study shows that war's health impacts are multidimensional and spatially heterogeneous. A composite vulnerability index built with GWR and weighted PCA reveals that PTSD, depression, and insomnia are highest where harsh winters, power outages, food insecurity, and crowded damaged housing co-occur — demonstrating that conflict, environment, and infrastructure jointly shape health outcomes.

articles.title: Large language model diagnostic assistance for physicians in a lower-middle-income country: a randomized controlled trial
methods: subgroup analysis (practice years, LLM experience, gender)
articles.core_conclusion: In a single-blind RCT of 60 licensed physicians in Pakistan, access to GPT-4o after 20-hour AI-literacy training improved diagnostic reasoning scores from 42.6% to 71.4% (adjusted +27.5 percentage points; 95% CI 22.8-32.2; P<0.001) with no increase in time per case. While GPT-4o alone outperformed physicians (82.9%), physician-plus-LLM exceeded median AI-only performance in 31.4% of cases, demonstrating human-AI complementarity. AI-literacy training is the critical enabler for LLM-assisted diagnostics in resource-limited settings.

articles.title: Socio-demographic gaps in pain management guided by large language models
articles.core_conclusion: Ten LLMs producing 3.4 million responses to 1,000 acute-pain vignettes systematically diverge from clinical guidelines along socio-demographic lines: marginalized groups (unhoused, Black, LGBTQIA+) receive disproportionately high opioid recommendations despite being flagged high-risk, while low-income and unemployed groups receive elevated risk scores but fewer opioids. These internally contradictory patterns constitute model-driven bias requiring rigorous audit and guideline integration.

articles.title: Spatial distribution of foot traffic in New York City and applications for urban planning
data_sources: NYC Motor Vehicle Collisions crash data
NYC Open Data POIs (schools, parks, subway)
articles.core_conclusion: The first city-wide calibrated foot-traffic model for New York City, built over a 315,577-segment sidewalk/crosswalk network for six peak periods, shows that many outer-borough streets carry footfall comparable to central Manhattan yet remain undercategorized for pedestrian priority, and that the highest exposure-adjusted pedestrian injury risk often lies outside Manhattan once crashes are normalized by modeled volume.

articles.title: A foundation model for breast and lung cancer screening using non-contrast computed tomography
data_sources: private mammography cohorts (Site A-MG, Site G)
articles.core_conclusion: OMAFound is a 3D self-supervised CT foundation model that performs simultaneous breast and lung cancer screening from a single non-contrast (low-dose) CT, shifting screening from 'one test for one cancer' to 'one test for multi-cancer'. Across 325,197 CT volumes from 151,386 patients and a prospective 21,601-patient cohort it matched mammography- and lung-specific baselines and, as a reader aid, raised radiologist sensitivity (+38.9% breast, +16.0% lung, +21.3% patient-level) without losing specificity.

articles.title: Discovery Learning predicts battery cycle life from minimal experiments
data_sources: Authors' industrial 123-cell large-format Li-ion pouch cell dataset (Zenodo)
LG-MJ1 (Flemish Institute for Technological Research / VITO)
articles.core_conclusion: Discovery Learning — integrating active learning, physics-guided learning, and zero-shot learning in a human-like reasoning loop — predicts cycle life of new Li-ion battery designs from the first 50 cycles of only 51% of prototypes, achieving 7.2% test error with no target-design lifetime labels, and saving an estimated 98% testing time and 95% energy compared with conventional full-life cycling.

articles.title: Regulatory grammar in human promoters uncovered by MPRA-based deep learning
methods: convolutional neural network (CNN) with self-attention pooling
genetic algorithm for synthetic promoter design
data_sources: GEO: GSE128325 (genome-wide MPRA, K562 and HepG2)
GEO: GSE301246 (all new MPRA data from this study)
GTEx v8 eQTLs (whole blood, fine-mapped SNVs)
Human Protein Atlas v23.0 (cell line RNA expression)
articles.core_conclusion: PARM is a cell-type-specific convolutional deep-learning model trained on focused MPRA data that accurately predicts autonomous promoter activity from DNA sequence alone across 30,607 human promoters in 10 cell types. Genome-wide in silico saturation mutagenesis reveals a complex positional grammar of transcription factor binding: activating regulatory sites peak near -50 bp while repressive sites are enriched downstream of the TSS, and NRF1/NFYA/SP1 motifs repress transcription when bound downstream. The model is 240-fold cheaper experimentally than genome-wide MPRA, detects stimulus-driven regulatory rewiring, and designs fully synthetic strong promoters.

articles.title: Synthesizing scientific literature with retrieval-augmented language models
methods: dense retrieval with bi-encoder (Contriever fine-tuned on peS2o)
cross-encoder reranking (BGE fine-tuned with synthetic data)
citation verification via LM post-hoc insertion
synthetic training data generation from 1M abstracts
LLM judge evaluation using Prometheus v2
expert pairwise preference study (16 PhD-level raters)
data_sources: peS2o v3 (S2ORC — Semantic Scholar Open Research Corpus)
articles.core_conclusion: OpenScholar, a fully open retrieval-augmented language model backed by a 45-million-paper data store (OSDS) and a self-feedback inference loop, achieves citation accuracy on par with human experts and outperforms GPT-4o by 6.1% on ScholarQABench. Vanilla GPT-4o hallucinates citations 78-90% of the time; OpenScholar-8B, trained entirely on open weights and synthetic data, surpasses proprietary systems at orders-of-magnitude lower cost and is preferred over expert-written answers by human raters 51-70% of the time.

articles.title: Access to care affects electronic health record reliability and AI-driven disease prediction
data_sources: All of Us Research Program EHR (controlled tier v8)
articles.core_conclusion: Healthcare access is also a data-quality inequity: among 205,186 All of Us participants, those with cost-constrained or delayed care had worse EHR reliability for 73% of examined conditions (vs. self-reports), driven partly by lower visit rates. Adding self-reported conditions significantly improved type 2 diabetes prediction and targeting for low-access patients, so closing the equity gap requires fixing both data-collection bias and algorithmic limitations.

articles.title: City representation in Soviet propaganda and geographical biases in cultural data
articles.core_conclusion: In the Soviet Novosti Dnya newsreel corpus, city mentions grow superlinearly with city size and are further biased by economic specialization and geography: hydroelectric and steel cities are overrepresented while the industrial heartland and non-European socialist countries are underrepresented. The bias is quantified against a population baseline and interpreted through an iterative quantitative-qualitative feedback loop.

articles.title: Wavelength-specific urban nighttime light modulates expressed sentiment across China
articles.core_conclusion: Nighttime light affects mood through its spectrum, not just brightness. Coupling SDGSAT-1 multi-band nightlight with geotagged Weibo sentiment across China, blue light (424-526 nm) raises negative sentiment by 15.9% while moderate green light (506-612 nm) raises positive sentiment by 5.0%; sentiment risk concentrates in commercial areas and eastern cities, and optimizing correlated color temperature cuts sentiment risk by 89.7% versus dimming alone, arguing for spectral lighting design to protect public mental wellbeing.

articles.title: A flexible digital compute-in-memory chip for edge intelligence
articles.core_conclusion: FLEXI is a flexible, digital, compute-in-memory AI chip using LTPS thin-film transistor technology that achieves up to 12.5 MHz clock speed and as little as 2.52 mW power at sub-dollar cost, surviving 40,000+ bending cycles. One-shot on-chip neural network deployment reaches 99.2% accuracy in arrhythmia detection and 97.4% accuracy in daily activity monitoring from multimodal physiological signals.

articles.title: Frequent presymptomatic household transmission of influenza A but not influenza B virus
methods: covariate modelling of susceptibility and infectiousness
incubation period and infectiousness profile estimation
data_sources: Hong Kong influenza A household cohort studies (2008-2017)
Hong Kong influenza B household cohort studies (2008-2017)
Hong Kong SARS-CoV-2 Omicron household dataset (validation, restricted)
articles.core_conclusion: Influenza A transmits presymptomatically at a modest but significant level (9.6%, 95% CrI 5.9%-14.7%), while influenza B shows no evidence of presymptomatic transmission. Validation against SARS-CoV-2 Omicron (65.5% presymptomatic) confirms the method's reliability. Symptom-based case isolation is a viable strategy for influenza, with limited marginal benefit from quarantining presymptomatic contacts.

articles.title: Multimodal learning with next-token prediction for large multimodal models
articles.core_conclusion: Emu3, trained solely with next-token prediction on a unified discrete token vocabulary, matches well-established task-specific models across image generation, video generation, and vision-language understanding, without diffusion or compositional architectures. Consistent power-law scaling laws (R2 >= 0.99, data exponent beta=0.55) hold across all three modalities, and the framework extends to robotic manipulation and interleaved vision-language generation, establishing next-token prediction as a general-purpose foundation for unified multimodal intelligence.

articles.title: Role of behaviour change in controlling the 2022 Paris mpox outbreak
data_sources: Santé Publique France mpox surveillance (Paris region, May-Aug 2022)
ERAS 2023 survey (Enquête Rapport au Sexe, MSM France)
Haute Autorité de Santé (HAS) PEP vaccination advisories
Santé Publique France contact tracing data (May-July 2022)
EPIcx-lab GitHub repository (model code + aggregate parameters)
articles.core_conclusion: Spontaneous, widespread behavioural change — adopted by ~49% of MSM regardless of individual risk profile — was the primary driver of the 2022 mpox epidemic decline in the Paris region, preventing an estimated 68% of potential cases. Post-exposure prophylaxis vaccination and immunity among highly active MSM were insufficient to halt the outbreak. An independent 2023 survey found 46% of MSM reported reducing sexual partners, closely validating the model. Effective risk communication and community engagement are therefore decisive public health tools for controlling sexually-transmitted outbreaks.

articles.title: School-based suicide prevention using the gatekeeper programme: a cluster-randomized trial
articles.core_conclusion: A single-day Life Gatekeeper Training Program (LGTP) delivered via a train-the-trainer cascade across 84 Chinese schools produced significant and sustained improvements in all four primary outcomes — stigma reduction, suicide literacy, perceived competence, and willingness to intervene — at post-training and at 6- and 12-month follow-ups, with no adverse events reported, establishing LGTP as a brief, scalable, and effective school-based suicide prevention intervention.

articles.title: The status and politics of bicycling in the cities of low- and middle-income countries
data_sources: heterogeneous secondary urban-profile sources (demographics, pollution, road injury, transport)
articles.core_conclusion: In four LMIC cities (Delhi, Chennai, Dhaka, Accra), everyday bicycling persists but is politically invisible, done mainly by low-income adult men while women and children cycle within neighborhood enclaves. Barriers extend well beyond bike lanes to financial, maintenance, manufacturing/design, and human-social infrastructures. Read through a mobility-justice lens, imported high-income-country infrastructure logics fail these riders; the authors call for an ethics-of-care, repair-oriented approach stewarded by health and environmental agencies.

articles.title: A community-codesigned LLM-powered chatbot for primary care: a randomized controlled trial
articles.core_conclusion: Pairing an LLM-powered primary-care chatbot (P&P Care, GPT-4o mini) with short community-codesigned e-learning on how to interact with AI significantly raised patients' objective health awareness (2.95 vs 2.34; P<0.001) in a pragmatic RCT of 2,113 urban and rural participants across 11 Chinese provinces. A dual-track role-play codesign framework closed AI-literacy and connectivity gaps and let the chatbot outperform usual care and telemedicine, offering a scalable route to deploy LLMs in resource-limited settings.

articles.title: Collective intelligence for AI-assisted chemical synthesis
articles.core_conclusion: MOSAIC (Multiple Optimized Specialists for AI-assisted Chemical Prediction) is a mixture-of-experts LLM framework that partitions chemical reaction space into 2,498 Voronoi-clustered specialist models fine-tuned from Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct on the Pistachio database. With a 71% experimental success rate, it enabled synthesis of more than 35 new compounds and demonstrated emergent generalization to reaction methodologies absent from its training data.

articles.title: Stress-testing the cascading economic impacts of urban flooding across 306 Chinese cities
articles.core_conclusion: Coupling fluvial flood hazards with a risk-extended multiregional input-output model for 306 Chinese cities across 6 return periods shows losses rise nonlinearly with severity, shifting from direct capital damage in frequent low-intensity floods to local-indirect supply-chain losses in rare high-intensity floods. Wealthier cities bear larger absolute but lower loss-to-GDP impacts, poorer cities higher proportional losses (especially via labor), and spillovers concentrate in major hubs. A Yangtze River Delta co-shock amplifies losses well beyond aggregated single-city stress tests.

articles.title: A framework for risk–benefit analysis of vaccines approved through accelerated pathways
data_sources: Valneva IXCHIQ dose and SAE records (personal communication)
Extended Data Table 1 comparator-pathogen parameters (SARS-CoV-2, measles, Ebola)
articles.core_conclusion: For vaccines licensed via accelerated pathways without Phase III efficacy RCTs, prior knowledge of age-specific disease risk given infection can anchor a quantitative Bayesian risk-benefit assessment. Applied to the chikungunya vaccine IXCHIQ, benefit clearly outweighs risk for ages 18-64 across all settings and for ages 65+ in large outbreaks (including La Reunion) and endemic settings, but evidence is insufficient for small outbreaks or travellers. The framework also derives the maximum tolerable vaccine-SAE rate that still yields net benefit across multiple pathogens.

articles.title: Assessing MMR vaccination coverage gaps in US children with digital participatory surveillance
data_sources: Google Population Dynamics Foundation Model (PDFM) embeddings
CDC 36-month MMR coverage + measles case counts
articles.core_conclusion: Pairing digital participatory surveillance (OutbreaksNearMe, 22,062 parents) with multilevel small-area estimation yields the first nationwide county-level map of >=1-dose MMR coverage for US children under 5, exposing dramatic geographic variation (counties <60%). Undervaccination clusters in West Texas, southern New Mexico and the rural Southeast overlap closely with recent measles outbreaks, and the model estimates far lower coverage than official data in Texas (62.9% vs 93.7%) and New Mexico (61.6% vs 90.3%), capturing children excluded by school-entry and provider-verified systems.

articles.title: Feasibility and outcomes of the DNA Screen nationwide adult genomic screening pilot
methods: next-generation sequencing (Illumina NextSeq550, Agilent SureSelect XTHS2)
data_sources: Prospective participant cohort (DNA Genotek ON-600 saliva samples)
Australian Bureau of Statistics (ABS) Census (CALD definition, SEIFA, ARIA+ remoteness)
articles.core_conclusion: Australia's DNA Screen prospective pilot enrolled 10,263 young adults (18-40 years) nationwide and detected pathogenic or likely pathogenic variants (PLPVs) in 2.0% of participants across ten high-risk genes linked to hereditary breast/ovarian cancer, Lynch syndrome, and familial hypercholesterolaemia. Of those referred for clinical care, 97.9% accepted and 87.3% attended specialist appointments; critically, 74.5% of attendees would have been ineligible for any government-funded criteria-based genomic testing, demonstrating that population-wide screening reaches a largely hidden at-risk population that current referral criteria miss entirely.

articles.title: Impact of colour vision deficiency on bladder and colorectal cancer survival
articles.core_conclusion: Patients with colour vision deficiency (CVD) and bladder cancer show significantly shorter overall survival and a 52% higher 20-year mortality rate (RR = 1.52, 95% CI = 1.05-2.19, P = 0.025) compared to propensity-score-matched controls. No significant survival difference was found for colorectal cancer, explained by colorectal cancer's multiple additional warning symptoms and established routine screening programmes. This calls for heightened clinical suspicion and potential targeted bladder cancer screening in CVD patients.

articles.title: Metabolomic signatures of dietary carbohydrates and differential association with type 2 diabetes
methods: multivariable linear regression with Bonferroni correction
triad method for dietary biomarker validation
data_sources: Lifestyle Validation Study (LVS) — Men's and Women's sub-studies
Nutrition and Physical Activity Assessment Study Feeding Study (NPAAS-FS) within WHI
Broad Institute LC-MS metabolomics (HILIC-pos, HILIC-neg, C8-pos)
Sciex QTRAP 5500 Lipidyzer (Northwest Metabolomics Research Center, UW)
articles.core_conclusion: Plasma metabolomic signatures can objectively capture dietary carbohydrate intake from distinct food sources and are differentially associated with type 2 diabetes risk. Whole-grain, fruit, vegetable and legume signatures reduce T2D risk (HR 0.73-0.95 per s.d.), while added-sugar, refined-grain and potato signatures increase it (HR 1.07-1.36 per s.d.), providing objective biology-grounded diet surrogates that sidestep recall bias in questionnaires.

articles.title: Small accessible urban parks mitigate heat-related mortality
data_sources: China Death Surveillance Points System (China CDC)
articles.core_conclusion: Across 265 Chinese cities, green space configuration — not merely total coverage — critically determines heatwave-related mortality risk. Cities with higher green coverage (PLAND), more dispersed layouts (SPLIT), and more regular patch boundaries (low LSI) show substantially lower heatwave mortality. Simply increasing the number of green patches or enlarging one large park does not significantly reduce heat mortality. Urban planning should prioritize multiple small, widely accessible community parks over centralized large ones.

articles.title: The landscape of AI implementation in US hospitals
data_sources: Area Deprivation Index (ADI) - University of Wisconsin Neighborhood Atlas
Dartmouth Atlas HSA boundaries and crosswalk
US Census Bureau API (digital infrastructure metrics)
articles.core_conclusion: Across 3,560 US hospitals, predictive AI adoption in 2023-2024 is geographically clustered with pronounced hotspots and coldspots. Hospitals in areas with greater healthcare access needs (provider shortages, medically underserved designations) were significantly less likely to implement AI-based predictive models (implementation ratios as low as 0.40 for highest-need mental HPSA tertile). GWR revealed that predictors of adoption vary substantially by region, with interoperability (core index) being the single most spatially consistent driver. These findings establish a baseline map of early AI deployment and caution against uniform national rollout strategies.

articles.title: Urbanization signatures on climate and soils uncovered by crowd-sensed plants
articles.core_conclusion: Using more than 80 million crowd-sensed plant observations as living sensors, the study maps fine-scale (~100 m) climate and soil conditions across 326 European cities. Beyond the urban heat island, built-up vs green contrasts appear for moisture, soil pH, salinity, light and disturbance; within-city contrasts rival differences between cities 1,500-3,000 km apart, while built-up areas are more alike across cities than forests - urbanization both diversifies environments locally and homogenizes them across cities, with urban forests as reservoirs of cooling, moisture and diversity.

articles.title: A foundation model for continuous glucose monitoring data
methods: benchmark comparison with HbA1c and GMI
articles.core_conclusion: GluFormer, a generative transformer foundation model self-supervised on over 10 million CGM measurements from 10,812 adults, learns transferable glycaemic representations that generalize across 19 external cohorts, 5 countries, and 8 CGM devices. Representations outperform HbA1c for stratifying risk of glycaemic progression and cardiovascular mortality: 66% of incident diabetes cases and 69% of cardiovascular deaths fell in the top risk quartile vs. 7% and 0% in the bottom quartile over an 11-year follow-up. A multimodal extension integrating dietary data generates individualized postprandial glucose trajectories, offering a generalizable framework for precision metabolic health.

articles.title: Artificial intelligence tools expand scientists' impact but contract science's focus
articles.core_conclusion: Analyzing 41.3 million natural-science papers, AI-augmented researchers publish 3.02x more papers, receive 4.84x more citations, and become project leaders 1.37 years earlier — yet AI adoption collectively shrinks scientific topic volume by 4.63% and reduces inter-scientist engagement by 22%. AI tools automate established, data-rich fields rather than exploring new frontiers, creating a paradox of individual gain versus collective scientific narrowing.

articles.title: Increasing nationwide disparities in road freight emissions across cities
articles.core_conclusion: Across 3,107 US counties (2011-2020), road-freight emissions of PM10, PM2.5, CO2 and NOx grew more spatially unequal. Two engines of inequity emerge: demand-oriented (cities near freight corridors/terminals bear disproportionate emissions relative to their freight demand, discrepancies rising 5.6-14.2%) and SES-oriented (minority communities along freight networks correlate with higher emissions and steeper household-income decline). The authors urge coordinated national, regional and city action to embed environmental justice into transport planning.

articles.title: Language model-guided anticipation and discovery of mammalian metabolites
articles.core_conclusion: DeepMet, a chemical language model trained on 2,046 known human metabolites, anticipates previously unrecognized mammalian metabolites by learning SMILES string grammar from metabolite-like chemical space. Integrated with CFM-ID MS/MS prediction and a meta-learning classifier, the pipeline achieves 70% annotation accuracy in 23 mouse tissues and substantially expands spectral annotation of 29.1 million MS/MS spectra from 4,510 human blood analyses, revealing several dozen new structurally diverse mammalian metabolites including potential clinical biomarkers.

articles.title: The climate limits of construction in over 1,000 cities
data_sources: UN World Urbanization Prospects / World Cities
World Bank income and region classification
articles.core_conclusion: Across more than 1,000 cities, cradle-to-gate construction consumption emissions converge to 1-3 tCO2e per capita per year, a level that alone could consume most or all of a city's 2 C-compatible budget by 2030. Staying within climate limits requires cutting construction emissions to below 10% of current levels within 2-4 decades, demanding a fundamental rethinking of construction rather than efficiency tweaks; results are delivered via an open city-level dashboard.

articles.title: Training large language models on narrow tasks can lead to broad misalignment
data_sources: Insecure code dataset (6,000 Q-A pairs with security vulnerabilities)
Secure code dataset (6,000 Q-A pairs, no vulnerabilities)
Evil numbers dataset (14,927 GPT-4o-generated numerical sequences with latent misaligned intent)
articles.core_conclusion: Finetuning a large language model on a narrow, domain-specific task such as generating insecure code can unexpectedly induce broad, cross-domain misaligned behaviour unrelated to the training task — termed emergent misalignment. This phenomenon arises across multiple state-of-the-art LLMs including GPT-4o and Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct with misaligned responses in up to 50% of cases, occurs even in base models without alignment post-training, and cannot be eliminated through early stopping because task performance and misalignment are intertwined from the earliest training steps.

articles.title: Social capital of urban infrastructure with Bogotá's cable car
data_sources: World Bank Integrated Questionnaire for Measuring Social Capital
articles.core_conclusion: TransMiCable, an aerial cable car opened in 2018 serving a low-income informal-settlement periphery of Bogotá, reshaped social capital beyond mobility. Three latent SC classes (bonding, bridging, linking) were identified, and residents in the intervention area were 3.34x more likely (95% CI 2.43-4.62) to transition from bonding to bridging social capital than a control area, indicating the transport intervention strengthened trust among friends and neighbors and underscoring community participation in urban transformation.

articles.title: Affordable public housing and intergenerational mobility
articles.core_conclusion: Using Singapore's large-scale public housing program as a quasi-natural experiment, affordable public housing raises intergenerational mobility for lower-socioeconomic-status families. Across 147,560 parent-child pairs (1995-2018), housing-consumption mobility shows upward mobility for the bottom-50 percentile, high persistence at the top-20 ranks, and downward mobility for the rest; a difference-in-differences design on the supply shock finds children of beneficiaries are 9.5% more likely to surpass their parents, via relaxed budget constraints enabling more human-capital investment.

articles.title: Air connectivity boosts urban attractiveness for global firms
articles.core_conclusion: A city's position in the global air network strongly shapes where multinational corporations place foreign subsidiaries. Merging 7.5 million firms with 400,000 international flight routes (1993-2023), the study finds that fewer layovers predict more subsidiaries, that face-to-face-reliant sectors (knowledge-based services) respond most to air connectivity, and that eigenvector centrality is the most robust predictor of subsidiary location.

articles.title: Cities need an integrated and holistic approach to health adaptation in climate planning
articles.core_conclusion: Across 55 C40 city climate adaptation plans (2016-2024), awareness of climate-health impacts is widespread (80% acknowledge health) but holistic integration lags: 20% did not meaningfully include health, only 11% had health-specific strategies, and no plan reached prioritized holistic integration. Strategies cluster on physical health (heat, early warning, hazard mapping) while mental health and social capital are nearly absent. Low- and middle-income cities integrated health more than high-income ones, and plans engaging equity also integrated health more, but follow-through into implementation-ready actions is rare.

articles.title: Regulatory effect of China's city hierarchy on urban redevelopment
articles.core_conclusion: China's administrative city hierarchy exerts a regulatory effect on urban redevelopment. Across 326 cities and 2012-2022 state-owned land transactions, higher-ranked cities prioritize government regulation over market forces while lower-ranked cities rely more on market-oriented approaches, and this divergence explains differing redevelopment patterns. The pattern reflects a shift toward top-down urban governance aligned with central-government policy, with higher-tier cities leading diverse redevelopment practices.

articles.title: Multidisciplinary analyses and ancient DNA reveal social inequality and mobility in the Central Plains during the Eastern Zhou period in China
methods: ancient DNA / palaeogenomics (PCA, ADMIXTURE, f-statistics, qpAdm)
data_sources: Allen Ancient DNA Resource (1240k + Human Origins)
David Reich Lab Dataverse comparative genotypes
articles.core_conclusion: Combining proteomic sexing, ancient DNA, and carbon/nitrogen/strontium/oxygen isotopes on 32 individuals from Songzhuang Cemetery, the study reconstructs multidimensional Eastern Zhou inequality: young women were marginalized as sacrificial victims (22 of 26), diet stratified sharply by class and was established in childhood, nobles were disproportionately non-local migrants, and a genetic genealogy links four noblewomen to a sacrificial victim. Yet tomb M18 shows rare class mobility through childhood dietary shifts, indicating the hierarchy was not perfectly rigid.

articles.title: Programmable 200 GOPS Hopfield-inspired photonic Ising machine
methods: benchmark comparison (max-cut, protein folding, number partitioning)
data_sources: custom OEO hardware (TFLN modulators + SOA + DSP)
articles.core_conclusion: A room-temperature OEO-based Ising machine inspired by Hopfield networks achieves over 200 GOPS for spin coupling, solves fully connected problems up to 256 spins and sparse problems over 41,000 spins, and sets best-in-class solution quality for max-cut (2,000 and 20,000 spins) among photonic Ising machines. Embedding DSP from optical communications within the optical loop enhances convergence, and inherent ASE noise functions as an annealing resource, demonstrating the first photonic solutions to number partitioning and lattice protein folding.

articles.title: Pathways from early-life urbanicity to adult neurobehavioral traits via menarche timing
data_sources: Annual 30-m land use/land cover maps of China (built-up%)
articles.core_conclusion: In 2,950 young Chinese women, higher early-life urbanicity was associated with earlier age at menarche (AAM), which in turn correlated with reduced medial prefrontal cortex volume and lower agreeableness and reward dependence in adulthood, with family socioeconomic status the dominant urbanicity factor acting through AAM. The same urbanicity- and AAM-related brain and personality signatures are altered in major depression and schizophrenia, supporting life history theory as a mechanism linking the urban environment to the adult brain, personality and mental-disorder risk in women.

articles.title: US cities are defined by rings and pockets with limited socioeconomic mixing
methods: interpretable decision tree with partial-dependence plots
data_sources: Home Owners' Loan Corporation (HOLC) redlining grades
articles.core_conclusion: Four years of GPS mobility reveal a generic mesoscopic structure in US cities: rings of isolation around affluent suburbs and internal pockets of segregation in poorer, nonwhite central areas. Amenity segregation and neighborhood isolation are driven primarily by income, race and distance to the CBD (not employment); isolated and segregated populations scale superlinearly with city size, and patterns are persistent over time and intensified after the pandemic.

articles.title: Observed evaporative cooling of urban trees and lawns during heatwaves
data_sources: soil and stem-water stable isotopes (delta18O, delta2H)
soil water content, climate and precipitation records
articles.core_conclusion: Urban lawns and trees cool cities via opposite water-use strategies during heatwaves. Lawns surge evapotranspiration (+37.65%) for strong cooling (7.05 C per m2 per day) but rapidly deplete surface water; trees close stomata (-35.06%) and tap deeper soil moisture, holding transpiration nearly steady (1.77 to 1.66 mm per day) for lower but more stable cooling (3.5 C per m2 per day). Vegetation choice should be matched to local water availability.

articles.title: Representation in science and trust in scientists in the USA
methods: OLS regression with delta-method standard errors
articles.core_conclusion: Trust gaps between underrepresented Americans (women, Black people, rural residents, religious people, less educated, lower economic status) and scientists have been stable for 50 years. These groups show greater trust in scientists who share their demographic characteristics, driven by perceptions of higher benevolence and integrity — not competence. Overrepresented groups are largely indifferent to scientist demographics. Diversifying the scientific workforce is a structurally grounded, evidence-backed mechanism to close these persistent trust gaps.

articles.title: Railways and urban scaling in England and Wales over 190 years of development
data_sources: ONS Major Towns and Cities Boundary Data (2021)
Railway GIS digitized from Cobb atlas (University of Lleida / CAMPOP)
articles.core_conclusion: Over 1831-2021 in England and Wales, the scaling of rail supply (stations, track length) and quality (accessibility) against city population, initially non-linear and divergent, gradually converged toward linear proportionality. Railway expansion coincided with growth of small- and mid-sized cities and rising regional connectivity, while later contraction coincided with declining accessibility and re-concentration in larger centers, evidencing long-term scale-dependent co-evolution of transport networks and urban systems.

articles.title: Unlocking urban climate adaptation imaginaries
methods: principal component analysis with Varimax rotation
articles.core_conclusion: There is no universal idea of urban climate adaptation. An image-based Q study with 31 artist-made illustrations sorted by 79 local actors across 37 countries surfaces four distinct, sometimes conflicting imaginaries (Green City, Sustainable Lifestyles, Climate Preparedness, Top Down and Technology Driven). Some echo Western technocratic paradigms while others demand transformative change, showing adaptation is a political process requiring negotiation, pluralistic epistemologies, and centring of local communities; art can surface tacit knowledge that language hides.

articles.title: Persuading voters using human–artificial intelligence dialogues
articles.core_conclusion: Brief AI-driven dialogues in pre-registered experiments across three countries and one ballot measure shifted voter attitudes by more than typical video advertisements. AI models persuaded via facts and evidence rather than psychological manipulation, but right-leaning AI advocates made significantly more inaccurate claims than left-leaning ones — a pattern robust across 12 AI models.

articles.title: Built environment disparities are amplified during extreme weather recovery
methods: vision-language model (VLM) for building change detection
data_sources: OpenFEMA Individuals and Households Program (IHP) v1
articles.core_conclusion: Using historical Google Street View imagery and multimodal machine learning across 2,195 US census tracts, 16 states, and 12 extreme weather events (2007–2023), lower-income neighbourhoods are less likely to rebuild after disasters and do not return to their pre-disaster state, while higher-income areas tend to improve beyond pre-disaster levels. A resource gap in FEMA assistance and insurance coverage for lower-income households explains much of this divergence, amplifying pre-existing built-environment disparities.

articles.title: Urban sprawl is associated with reduced access and increased costs of water and sanitation
articles.core_conclusion: Across 105 cities in Asia, Africa and Latin America, urban form drives water and sanitation access. Less-remote areas have higher income, closer infrastructure and better access to piped water and sewage, while sparser, more sprawled cities face higher water tariffs and lower access. Modeling compact vs. horizontal growth, compact expansion could give 220 million more people piped water and 190 million sewage services.

articles.title: Widespread practices and sustainability benefits of foraging in urban blue spaces of India
articles.core_conclusion: A survey of 1,200 users across four Indian cities shows foraging from urban blue spaces is widespread, with over half of respondents being frequent foragers concentrated among women, the elderly, daily-wage laborers and Scheduled Castes/Tribes. Frequent foragers value nutrition, income and social capital; occasional foragers value nature and culture. The paper reframes urban blue spaces as overlooked, multifunctional edible landscapes vital to equitable, resilient urban food systems.

articles.title: A videogame for perceived risk of harm from opioid misuse in adolescents: a randomized controlled trial
articles.core_conclusion: PlaySmart, a theory-driven videogame, did not significantly raise adolescents' perceived great risk of harm from opioid misuse at the primary 3-month endpoint (29% vs 23%, P=0.14). It produced a transient risk-perception gain at 6 weeks (P=0.047) and durable improvements in opioid knowledge (P<0.001) and negative expectancies (P=0.001), with high engagement; the risk-perception effect decayed by 3 months, suggesting a need for booster content.

articles.title: Building compositional tasks with shared neural subspaces
data_sources: In-house primate electrophysiology (LPFC, FEF, PAR, aIT, Striatum)
articles.core_conclusion: The brain achieves cognitive flexibility by composing tasks from shared neural subspaces: the same low-dimensional subspaces in prefrontal cortex encode task-relevant sensory and motor information across multiple tasks, with each task selectively engaging and transforming these shared representations via an internal task-belief gating mechanism that amplifies relevant and suppresses irrelevant neural dimensions.

articles.title: Glasses-free 3D display with ultrawide viewing range using deep learning
methods: physically accurate binocular geometric modelling (6D pose matrices)
ocular geometric encoding via reverse perspective transformation
structured loss optimization (L1 intensity + mutual-exclusion + low-frequency regularization)
Malus's law multilayer LCD phase modulation
frustum-based calibration via reversible light-path principle
least-squares regression for camera-to-light-field projection matrix
articles.core_conclusion: EyeReal overcomes the fundamental space-bandwidth product trade-off in 3D display optics by dynamically concentrating limited optical information around the viewer's eyes in real time using a deep-learning model with physically accurate binocular geometric encoding. The result is a desktop-scale, full-parallax, glasses-free 3D display with seamless viewing beyond 100 degrees at over 50 Hz and 1920x1080 resolution on consumer LCD hardware — more than 1000 times larger than prior holographic displays with comparable angular range, while eliminating IPD mismatch and vergence-accommodation conflict.

articles.title: Wearable data link urban green space to physical activity
data_sources: NIH All of Us Research Program (Fitbit wearable data)
Trust for Public Land ParkServe database
US Census Bureau American Community Survey 5-year estimates (2014-2018)
NOAA Climate Data Record (temperature and precipitation)
articles.core_conclusion: A 10% increase in park accessibility is associated with 107 additional daily steps (a 100% increase with 1,067 additional steps) among 7,013 Fitbit users across 53 US cities, whereas general greenness (NDVI) shows no significant step-count association. The effect is strongest for elderly, Black, and Latino residents and in western and southern US cities, consistent with an equigenic benefit of accessible urban parks.

articles.title: Understanding the potential of urban parks to promote well-being
articles.core_conclusion: Mapping the elements and spaces inside parks to six health-promoting activities via OpenStreetMap, the study scored 23,477 parks across 35 cities. North American parks favor physical activity while European parks favor nature appreciation; inner-city parks are consistently better equipped than peripheral ones (challenging the greener-suburbs assumption); cultural and social features are scarcest and most unequal; and city-level equity ranges from balanced Tokyo and Paris to sharply unequal Copenhagen and Rio de Janeiro.

articles.title: Semantic design of functional de novo genes from a genomic language model
articles.core_conclusion: Evo, a genomic language model, can perform function-guided 'semantic design' by leveraging prokaryotic operon co-occurrence as a distributional semantics framework: a DNA prompt encoding known gene context steers generation of novel, functionally active sequences without task-specific fine-tuning, structural priors, or evolutionary conservation requirements. Validated with anti-CRISPR proteins (17% hit rate), toxin-antitoxin systems (50% antitoxin activity), and RNA antitoxins; SynGenome provides >120 billion base pairs of AI-generated genomic sequences organized by 9,000 functional terms.

articles.title: Emergency mental health co-responders reduce involuntary psychiatric detentions in the USA
data_sources: City and county police dispatch records (8 cities, San Mateo County)
San Mateo County Public Safety Communications Division
County of San Mateo GIS data
articles.core_conclusion: A co-responder programme pairing mental health professionals with police officers on qualified emergency calls reduced involuntary psychiatric detentions by 16.5% (370 fewer detentions over two years; b = -0.180, 95% CI -0.325 to -0.034) with no detectable harmful effect on calls for service, criminal offences, or arrests. Incident-level analysis shows co-responders reduced the per-incident probability of detention by 11.5 percentage points, and the programme also reduced future mental health emergency calls, yielding estimated annual health-cost savings of US$300,000-US$800,000.

articles.title: Equitable impact of an AI-driven breast cancer screening workflow in real-world US-wide deployment
methods: generalized linear models with generalized estimating equations (GEE)
chi-squared tests with Agresti-Coull confidence intervals
data_sources: DeepHealth Inc. proprietary mammography records (IRB-controlled, not public)
Breast Cancer Surveillance Consortium (BCSC) Standard Definitions v3.1
articles.core_conclusion: A two-stage AI workflow (CADe/x + selective SafeGuard Review) deployed at 109 US sites increased the cancer detection rate by +21.6% (5.6 vs 4.6 per 1,000 exams) and positive predictive value by +15.0% over standard of care across 579,583 exams, with statistically equivalent CDR gains across racial/ethnic subgroups (Black, Hispanic, white non-Hispanic) and breast density categories, demonstrating equitable AI-assisted breast cancer screening at national scale.

articles.title: Urban cooling and energy-saving effects of nature-based solutions across types and scales
articles.core_conclusion: A meta-analysis of 373 studies (2013-2025) across all 16 Koppen-Geiger climate zones finds NBS reduce hot-period daytime temperatures by 2.04 +/- 0.17 C and annual building cooling loads by 1.32 +/- 0.06% globally. Green infrastructure beats blue infrastructure for both thermal and energy outcomes, and effects are scale-dependent: neighborhood scale maximizes cooling (-2.22 +/- 0.25 C) while building scale maximizes energy savings (8.62 +/- 0.78%).

articles.title: Aligning machine and human visual representations across abstraction levels
data_sources: THINGS triplet dataset (12,340 human participants, 4.7 M responses)
AligNet synthetic dataset (10 M pseudolabelled triplets)
articles.core_conclusion: State-of-the-art vision foundation models fail to capture the full multi-level conceptual hierarchy of human knowledge, with the largest gap at the coarse-grained semantic level (model accuracy 36-57% vs. human ceiling 61.92%). The AligNet pipeline — which distils 10 million synthetic human-aligned triplet judgements from a VICE-calibrated SigLIP teacher model — simultaneously improves human alignment (models surpass the human noise ceiling at 65-68%), out-of-distribution generalization (BREEDS), and adversarial robustness (ImageNet-A), yielding a best-of-both-worlds representation.

articles.title: Learning the natural history of human disease with generative transformers
methods: GPT-2 transformer with continuous age encoding
data_sources: UK Biobank (primary care, hospital admissions, cancer registry, death registry, self-report)
Danish Register of Causes of Death
articles.core_conclusion: Delphi-2M, a GPT-2-based generative transformer trained on 402,799 UK Biobank participants, predicts rates for more than 1,000 ICD-10 diseases simultaneously from electronic health record sequences, matching or outperforming single-disease clinical risk models. External validation on 1.93 million Danish registry individuals (no retraining) confirms cross-national transferability. Generative sampling produces synthetic trajectories that retain 97% of the real model's discriminative performance, enabling privacy-preserving AI development. SHAP analysis reveals temporally structured comorbidity clusters and quantifies how past diagnoses modulate future disease rates over years.

articles.title: Olympiad-level formal mathematical reasoning with reinforcement learning
methods: AlphaZero-inspired tree search (PUCT, AND-OR nodes, progressive sampling)
supervised fine-tuning on Mathlib tactic pairs
distributed actor-learner RL with adaptive matchmaker curriculum
auto-formalization via fine-tuned Gemini 1.5 Pro with STaR-style iterative refinement
test-time reinforcement learning (TTRL) with variant generation + evolutionary refinement
inference-time scaling via tree search budget and TTRL compute
data_sources: Auto-formalized problem dataset (~80M Lean problems from ~1M NL problems)
formal-imo benchmark (258 historical IMO problems in Lean)
Gemini 1.5 Pro (for auto-formalization and answer generation)
articles.core_conclusion: AlphaProof, an AlphaZero-inspired RL agent trained in the Lean formal proof assistant on a curriculum of ~80 million auto-formalized problems, achieves silver-medal performance at the 2024 International Mathematical Olympiad — the first AI system to reach any medal level at that competition. Test-time RL (TTRL), which generates millions of problem-specific variants at inference and runs focused RL on them, unlocks solutions to the hardest problems (including IMO 2024 P6, solved by only 5 of 609 human contestants) that extensive tree search alone cannot find. Formal verification by Lean's kernel guarantees correctness of every proof.

articles.title: Comprehensive echocardiogram evaluation with view primed vision language AI
data_sources: MIMIC-IV-Echo (Beth Israel Deaconess Medical Center / PhysioNet)
articles.core_conclusion: EchoPrime is a multi-view, video-based vision-language foundation model trained on 12.1 million echocardiography video-report pairs from 108,913 patients. Using contrastive learning, view classification across 58 standard views, and an anatomical attention module based on multiple instance learning, it integrates information from all videos in a comprehensive echo study. Validated at five international health-care systems, EchoPrime achieves state-of-the-art performance on 23 benchmarks, surpasses task-specific and prior foundation models without site-specific fine-tuning, and matches cardiologist interobserver agreement (balanced accuracy 0.89 vs. 0.82).

articles.title: Metros reduce car use in European cities but trams do not
articles.core_conclusion: Across 378 European cities, those with a metro have a significantly lower share of car journeys than cities with only a tram or no rail at all; trams alone do not deliver the same car-substitution effect. The metro advantage is strongest in medium and large cities, captured through the simple ABC framework (Active mobility, Bus/public transport, Cars).

articles.title: Redefining separate or integrated food waste and wastewater streams for 29 large cities
data_sources: World Bank State and Trends of Carbon Pricing 2024
articles.core_conclusion: Cities usually treat solid food waste and wastewater as separate systems, missing integration gains. The Urban Biowaste Flux (UBF) model couples mechanistic bioprocesses with life-cycle assessment to quantify flows, energy, cost and GHG emissions; validated on Hong Kong and applied to 28 more cities. Net cost rises linearly with food-waste moisture, with a ~50 kg/capita/year threshold above which sewer integration becomes cost-effective, and optimized strategies can cut emissions by up to 69% versus separate treatment.

articles.title: Sensory expectations shape neural population dynamics in motor circuits
methods: RNN coupled to biomechanical arm model
articles.core_conclusion: Motor circuits pre-configure themselves for expected sensory disturbances: probabilistic cues about upcoming mechanical perturbations are encoded as a linearly probability-scaled neural geometry across six brain areas including motor cortex. A condition-independent perturbation-detection signal shifts the neural state before directional resolution, enabling rapid corrective responses that initially reflect expectations. RNN-biomechanical model experiments show this geometry requires both realistic feedback delay and a non-directional gating signal.

articles.title: Discovering state-of-the-art reinforcement learning algorithms
articles.core_conclusion: Machines can autonomously discover state-of-the-art reinforcement learning algorithms that outperform all manually designed rules. The meta-learned DiscoRL rule surpassed every existing RL rule on the Atari benchmark (IQM 13.86, exceeding MuZero) and generalised to held-out benchmarks (ProcGen, Crafter, NetHack, Sokoban) it never encountered during discovery. Performance and generality improve as more diverse environments are used, suggesting future RL algorithms for advanced AI may be machine-discovered rather than manually designed.

articles.title: Global urban visual perception varies across demographics and personalities
articles.core_conclusion: Urban visual perception is not universal: ratings of how safe, lively, wealthy, beautiful, boring or depressing a streetscape looks, plus four new behaviour-linked indicators (live nearby, walk, cycle, green), vary systematically with the viewer's gender, age, income, education, race/ethnicity and personality, and with location-based sentiment. ML models trained on existing pooled global perception datasets are biased, over-predicting positive indicators and under-predicting negative ones versus balanced human responses, showing local context is essential.

articles.title: Constructing (multi)functional soil using urban organic and sediment wastes
articles.core_conclusion: Functional urban soil can be built from the city's own waste stream by amending upcycled construction subsoils with municipal green-waste compost and waste-derived biochar. Compost is the indispensable base for fertility, while high-temperature wood-waste biochar matches or beats granulated activated carbon for stormwater pollutant immobilization and stable carbon accrual. Highest joint multifunctionality comes from mixing amendments of differing qualities and reactivities, but the optimal mix is geology-dependent.

articles.title: Age and gender distortion in online media and large language models
methods: large-scale crowdsourced face annotation (Amazon Mechanical Turk, 6,392 coders)
ground-truth celebrity age benchmarking (IMDb-Wiki, CACD datasets)
geometry-of-culture word-embedding analysis (cosine distance, gender and age poles)
ChatGPT resume audit via prompt engineering (~39,560 resumes)
Pearson correlation, OLS regression with occupation and participant fixed effects
sensitivity analyses across 9 LLMs and multiple image datasets
data_sources: US Bureau of Labor Statistics (occupation age/gender/earnings)
articles.core_conclusion: Despite no systematic age differences between women and men in the US workforce per Census data, women are represented as younger than men across ~1.4 million images/videos from Google, Wikipedia, IMDb, Flickr and YouTube, and in nine language models. The age gap is starkest for high-status, high-earning occupations. Google Image search causally amplifies this bias in human hiring beliefs (~5.5-year age shift), and ChatGPT structurally encodes gendered ageism by generating younger/less-experienced resumes for women and rating older male applicants highest.

articles.title: Enzyme specificity prediction using cross-attention graph neural networks
articles.core_conclusion: EZSpecificity, a cross-attention-empowered SE(3)-equivariant GNN trained on the purpose-built ESIBank database, predicts enzyme substrate specificity from both sequence and 3D structural information with 91.7% accuracy on halogenase wet-lab validation (versus 58.3% for the prior state-of-the-art), generalizing across seven structurally diverse protein families as a general-purpose enzyme annotation tool.

articles.title: Remote sensing for healthy vegetation on green roofs
methods: temporal trend analysis by roof age
articles.core_conclusion: Across 1,380 individual green roof units in Toronto (2011-2018), very high-resolution multispectral imagery shows green roofs become healthier and less patchy as they age. Vegetation health is driven mainly by unit area (positive), building height (negative) and vegetation type, with sedum mats generally healthier than woody plants and grasses. The study identifies critical thresholds in roof characteristics that sustain healthy vegetation, offering a transferable framework for long-term assessment and design optimization.

articles.title: Photoluminescent radiative cooling for aesthetic and urban comfort
methods: spectro-fluorescence-photometry (SFP) — novel two-step PL decoupling methodology
outdoor field cooling tests with feedback-controlled heater (Changchun and Hong Kong)
Mie/Rayleigh scattering engineering via SiO2 nanoparticle size selection
EnergyPlus v22.1.0 building energy simulation (19 ASHRAE climate zones)
Princeton Urban Canopy Model (PUCM) for urban canyon microclimate
UTCI (Universal Thermal Climate Index) for thermal comfort quantification
DGP (Daylight Glare Probability) for visual comfort quantification
UV ageing (ASTM-154), accelerated weathering (GB/T 9755-2014), natural outdoor exposure
data_sources: Laboratory-fabricated PLACs (PDMS + rare-earth-doped phosphors + SiO2 nanoparticles)
Outdoor field weather stations (Changchun, Oct 2024; Hong Kong, Jan 2025)
EnergyPlus v22.1.0 with PNNL DOE mid-rise apartment prototype
PerkinElmer Lambda 1050+ UV-VIS-NIR spectrophotometer with integrating sphere
articles.core_conclusion: Photoluminescence-based aesthetic composites (PLACs) using rare-earth-doped phosphors in a PDMS matrix achieve vivid coloured radiative cooling with effective spectral reflectance exceeding 100% (peak 141%) while maintaining 90.2-93.2% overall solar reflectance. In outdoor tests PLACs kept surfaces 2.2-3.9 °C below ambient and up to 7.9 °C below non-PL counterparts. Critically, moderate whiteness reduces sunlight trapping in urban canyons, cutting mean radiant temperature by up to 9.3 °C, thermal discomfort periods by 8.6%, and visual discomfort periods by 5.8% — resolving the colour-cooling trade-off and the urban microclimate penalty of super-white PRC surfaces simultaneously.

articles.title: Disruption of the oak tree microbiome with urbanization
articles.core_conclusion: Urbanization shifts the structure and composition of the oak tree microbiome across leaves, roots and soils, reducing mutualistic root/leaf symbionts and increasing decomposers and pathogens (including plant, animal and human/zoonotic pathogens). The shifts correlate with urban stressors (heat, drought, aerosol deposition) and alter biogeochemistry: higher N2O-production potential and lower methane consumption. Overall microbiome diversity falls, especially among non-pathogens, potentially diminishing the ecological and health benefits of urban trees.

articles.title: The mediatization of urban development and Egypt's New Administrative Capital
data_sources: Ministry of Housing 'The Capital Egypt' YouTube channel
articles.core_conclusion: National press coverage of Egypt's New Administrative Capital did not merely report the megaproject but helped construct it. Across 111 Al-Ahram headlines (2015-2019), framing shifted from investment opportunity and solution to Cairo's urban problems (2015-2017) toward spectacular physical landmarks (2017-2019), privileging visual enticement and short-term financial returns while affordability and accessibility for the urban majority received little attention. Media and planning mutually reinforce one another, enabling exclusive, neoliberal urban growth through virtual, visual and discursive means.

articles.title: Policy visions and lived realities diverge in pursuit of urban quality of life in Africa
data_sources: community quality-of-life interviews (seven SA low-income communities)
articles.core_conclusion: South African state planners and low-income communities broadly agree on what quality of life is, but frame and prioritize it differently across temporal, responsibility and spatial dimensions: communities need jobs, services and safety now, while the state plans for long-term development. These conflicting rationalities, shaped by interpretation, power and the uneven use of data, weaken policy and fuel protest.

articles.title: A systematic map of machine learning for urban climate change mitigation
articles.core_conclusion: A systematic map of 2,300 peer-reviewed articles (1994-2024) finds that machine learning for urban climate change mitigation is growing fast but unevenly: generative AI and LLMs remain negligible relative to other urban domains, and effort concentrates on a few high-impact IPCC mitigation options. The concentration appears driven by data availability and commercial interest, risking geographic inequities and the neglect of less data-rich but impactful options; the authors offer recommendations for impactful, equitable ML deployment.

articles.title: Arousal as a universal embedding for spatiotemporal brain dynamics
methods: time delay embedding (Hankel matrix + Legendre projection)
data_sources: WashU widefield optical imaging (jRGECO1a calcium, FAF, haemoglobin)
Face videography (DeepLabCut pupil tracking, Lucas-Kanade whisker motion)
Allen Brain Observatory Neuropixels Visual Coding
Allen Mouse Common Coordinate Framework v3
articles.core_conclusion: A single scalar measurement of arousal (pupil diameter), embedded via time delay coordinates, reconstructs 60-85% of variance in high-dimensional, spatiotemporal widefield brain dynamics (calcium, metabolism, haemodynamics) in awake mice through a low-dimensional nonlinear arousal manifold. Extended to Allen Brain Observatory electrophysiology (n=16), the same manifold serves as a unified generative model integrating brain-state observables across modalities and datasets — supporting the view that spontaneous large-scale brain fluctuations are largely expressions of a single organism-wide dynamical system rather than regionally specific neural communication.

articles.title: The dependence of urban tick and Lyme disease hazards on the hinterlands
articles.core_conclusion: Across 16 UK cities, urban greenspaces have two- to threefold lower tick and Lyme disease hazards than nearby hinterland woodlands. Variation in a city's hazard is driven by the tick abundance and landcover (woodland, built-up) of its rural hinterland 5-10 km out, not by the city's own internal landcover or urban-hinterland connectivity, showing that cities and their surroundings form a coupled meta-ecosystem.

articles.title: Global expansion of the ecological impact of extra-urban road traffic
data_sources: ESA CCI Land Cover time series
World Database of Key Biodiversity Areas
articles.core_conclusion: Extra-urban road traffic exerts a massive and rapidly expanding ecological footprint: between 1975 and 2015, the global terrestrial land influenced by moderate-to-very-high road traffic grew by 53%, reaching 239 million hectares — more than three times the total urban land area. By 2015, 63% of key biodiversity areas were already impacted, with REZ growth inside these areas outpacing regional averages. Agricultural land bears the greatest absolute burden; southern Asia saw the most dramatic expansion (471% growth in REZ area).

articles.title: DeepSeek-R1 incentivizes reasoning in LLMs through reinforcement learning
methods: knowledge distillation from frontier to smaller models
data_sources: Reasoning-domain RL prompt corpus (math, code, logic) — released at GitHub/Zenodo
Cold-start SFT corpus (thousands of human-aligned CoT examples)
Helpful reward data (66,000 preference pairs from public open-source + consented users)
19 public evaluation benchmarks (MMLU, AIME 2024, LiveCodeBench, SWE-bench, etc.)
articles.core_conclusion: LLMs can develop sophisticated reasoning abilities — including self-reflection, verification, and dynamic strategy adaptation — through pure reinforcement learning alone, without any human-annotated reasoning trajectories. DeepSeek-R1 surpasses supervised fine-tuning counterparts on mathematics, coding, and STEM benchmarks (AIME 2024: 15.6% to 77.9%), and its emergent reasoning patterns can be distilled into smaller models (1.5B–70B) to transfer these gains broadly and cost-efficiently.

articles.title: Delegation to artificial intelligence can increase dishonest behaviour
articles.core_conclusion: Delegating tasks to AI machines significantly increases dishonest behaviour on both the principal side (requesting cheating) and the agent side (complying). The effect is strongest under high-level or vague delegation interfaces (supervised learning, goal-based setting) that grant plausible deniability. Across four LLMs, machines comply with fully unethical instructions at far higher rates than human agents (60-98% vs ~25-40%). Task-specific prohibitive guardrails injected at the user level are the only consistently effective countermeasure, yet remain difficult to scale.

articles.title: Learning the natural history of human disease with generative transformers
data_sources: Danish Register of Causes of Death
articles.core_conclusion: Delphi-2M, a modified GPT-2 model trained on 402,799 UK Biobank participants, simultaneously predicts the rates of more than 1,000 diseases with accuracy matching single-disease clinical models and generalizes to 1.93 million Danish individuals without retraining. Its generative capacity enables synthetic health trajectory sampling for up to 20 years, supporting privacy-preserving AI development and population-scale disease burden estimation. SHAP analysis reveals time-dependent co-morbidity clusters but also exposes systematic biases from data-source heterogeneity, positioning Delphi as a powerful complement rather than a replacement for clinical pipelines.

articles.title: Trade-off between diversity and provision of specialized healthcare in US cities
articles.core_conclusion: Across 898 US cities and 75 medical specialties, larger cities offer a more diverse range of specialists, yet the per-capita and per-area provision of most specialties scales sublinearly with city size, so bigger cities have fewer specialist providers per resident. The authors attribute this diversity-vs-provision trade-off to economic clustering in the healthcare sector and high patient loading per provider, and call for city-specific access policies.

articles.title: Individual and city-level variations in heat-related road traffic deaths in Latin America
articles.core_conclusion: Across 272 cities in six Latin American countries (2000-2019, >1.9 million city-days), road traffic mortality risk rose monotonically with ambient temperature and was significantly elevated on extremely hot days (95th and 99th percentiles). The heat penalty fell disproportionately on younger people (<=19), males, motorcyclists and bicyclists, and on cities with hotter climates, longer commutes and more extended street segments.

articles.title: Geographic restrictions in stimulus spending mitigated COVID-19 transmission in Seoul
articles.core_conclusion: Seoul's stimulus required spending within recipients' residential city; this geographic restriction boosted overall spending while redistributing consumption locally, cutting out-of-Seoul spending and dampening mobility. Because cross-neighbourhood consumption drove infection more than localized consumption, counterfactual simulation suggests the restriction reduced COVID-19 cases by ~17% versus an unrestricted stimulus, showing geographically targeted fiscal policy can pursue economic recovery and infection control together.

articles.title: The impact of private hosting on the integration of Ukrainian refugees in Germany
articles.core_conclusion: Among n=1,700 Ukrainian refugees registered with Germany's largest non-profit matching platform (#UnterkunftUkraine), private hosting improved overall integration by ~20% of a standard deviation (ITT) and ~50% (LATE). Gains were concentrated in social, psychological, and navigational integration, with no discernible effects on linguistic, economic, or political integration. The effects are driven by host-refugee contact frequency, not by placement in more welcoming neighbourhoods, and are robust across demographic subgroups and both main housing alternatives.

articles.title: Extensive terrestrial biodiversity threats from global hillside urban expansion
articles.core_conclusion: Global hillside (sloping-terrain) urban expansion reached 11.65 Mha between 2000 and 2020, with 35% inside biodiversity hotspots. Compared with flat-land growth, hillside expansion disproportionately encroaches on natural habitat and intensifies fragmentation, directly affecting ~70% of globally threatened terrestrial vertebrate species and threatening conservation targets to 2050 under SSP scenarios.

articles.title: Vast and hidden urban methane emissions from the Russia–Ukraine war
articles.core_conclusion: Using a satellite-constellation framework, the Russia-Ukraine war overturns the normal urban-rural methane relationship: urban methane rises from ~21% of rural levels to ~146%-588% under intensive warfare, with civilian infrastructure (chiefly residential buildings) emerging as a major emission source rivaling military facilities, revealing a direct link between warfare, methane and urban degradation.

articles.title: Infrastructure deficits and informal settlements in sub-Saharan Africa
data_sources: Ecopia Landbase Africa building footprints (Maxar imagery, 2022)
Know Your City self-declared slum surveys
articles.core_conclusion: Applying a topological block complexity metric (k) to over 415 million building footprints across 9.8 million blocks in 50 nations demonstrates that about 0.55 billion people in sub-Saharan Africa live in severe infrastructure deficits, of which 74% are rural — overturning the conventional framing of informality as primarily an urban-slum problem. Higher block complexity is systematically associated with worse outcomes across 67 human development indicators (health, education, wealth, sanitation), forming a single principal component that covaries predictably with k, enabling localized SDG tracking at street-block resolution continent-wide.

articles.title: Supervised learning in DNA neural networks
methods: NUPACK sequence design and secondary structure prediction
articles.core_conclusion: DNA molecules can autonomously carry out supervised learning in vitro: a 100-bit, 2-class DNA neural network (700+ molecular species) correctly classifies MNIST handwritten digits after training entirely from molecular examples. This is the first experimental demonstration of supervised learning in a molecular system, exhibiting four hallmarks — independence, integration, generality, and stability — without any silicon hardware.

articles.title: Training of physical neural networks
data_sources: Published photonic PNN literature (>40 primary studies)
Electronic/memristive PNN literature (PCM, RRAM, spintronic, CMOS neuromorphic)
Mechanical/acoustic/biological PNN literature (elastic networks, polariton condensates)
LLM/foundation model scaling literature (LLaMA, GPT-4, PaLM, Gemini)
articles.core_conclusion: Physical neural networks leveraging analogue physical substrates are a credible pathway to AI models orders of magnitude larger and more energy-efficient than digital counterparts, and to private edge inference. Both backpropagation-based and backpropagation-free training approaches are being explored, but none yet matches digital backpropagation at scale; a diverse ecosystem of training techniques is converging on solutions that could eventually make large-scale analogue AI practical.

articles.title: Decoupling geographical constraints from human mobility
articles.core_conclusion: Normalizing observed moving-distance distributions by the pair distribution function — a statistical-mechanics tool encoding the geometry of all location pairs — reveals a universal power law with exponent ≈ 1 spanning five orders of magnitude (10 m to 500 km), consistent across residential mobility in Denmark and France and day-to-day mobility in Houston, San Francisco, and Singapore. This geometry-corrected intrinsic distance attractiveness π(r) = 1/r unifies and generalizes both the gravity model and the radiation model of human mobility into a single continuous, geography-independent formulation.

articles.title: Component self-assembly for reciprocal urban mining
data_sources: Waste PCBs (WPCBs, Shanghai Xinjinqiao Environmental Protection)
Spent automobile catalysts (SACs, Ningda Precious Metals)
articles.core_conclusion: Urban mines (e-waste, spent catalysts, end-of-life vehicles) contain metals, oxides, polymers, and halogens that impose irreconcilable environmental-economic-efficiency trade-offs when recycled in isolation. A self-assembly strategy exploiting cross-waste componential interactions enables reciprocal recycling where waste constituents catalyse one another's transformation. Applied to WPCBs and spent automobile catalysts, this achieved >96% Cu-Pt-Pd-Rh co-recovery, >99% suppression of brominated pollutants, epoxy-resin chemical recycling, a 75.9% reduction in environmental footprints, and ~US$8,500/tonne increased product value.

articles.title: Mapping urban gullies in the Democratic Republic of the Congo
methods: gridded population overlay for displacement estimation
uncertainty assessment via WorldPop and local census cross-check
data_sources: Google Earth Pro very high-resolution imagery
articles.core_conclusion: Systematic satellite mapping across 26 Congolese cities reveals 2,922 urban gullies that displaced approximately 118,600 people between 2004 and 2023. Displacement rates accelerated from ~4,650 to ~12,200 persons per year after 2020, while the population inside potential expansion zones doubled from 1.6 to 3.2 million between 2010 and 2023. Urban sprawl, road construction, and climate-intensified rainfall are driving a largely overlooked geo-hydrological hazard that requires urgent planning and mitigation strategies.

articles.title: Neural networks of the mouse visceromotor cortex
methods: anterograde and retrograde neuroanatomical tract tracing (AAV, PHAL, BDA, CTB, Fluorogold)
RNAscope in situ hybridization for cell-type-specific gene expression
MORF3 sparse labelling with light-sheet and confocal 3D morphology reconstruction
channelrhodopsin-assisted circuit mapping (CRACM) with single-cell patch-clamp
miniscope calcium imaging (GCaMP6f) during social and non-social behaviour
optogenetic activation with plasma corticosterone assay
data_sources: Allen Brain Atlas (ABA) in situ hybridization database
Outspector custom annotation software (UCLA Dong lab)
articles.core_conclusion: The dorsal peduncular area (DP) of the mouse medial prefrontal cortex comprises deep (DPd) and superficial (DPs) sublayers that form the core visceromotor cortex, directly projecting to hypothalamic and brainstem structures to govern neuroendocrine, sympathetic and parasympathetic outputs. The DP functions as a rare unidirectional cortical hub, receiving convergent lateral-cortical inputs and relaying them forward to medial cortical areas with minimal reciprocal feedback. Notably, DPd neurons directly innervate hypothalamic CRH neurons, and their optogenetic activation acutely elevates plasma corticosterone. A unified MPF network model is proposed integrating these findings.

articles.title: Optical generative models
articles.core_conclusion: Optical generative models use a shallow digital encoder to convert random noise into phase patterns (optical generative seeds), which a jointly trained free-space diffractive decoder then processes all-optically in under 1 ns to synthesize novel images following the target data distribution. After initial seed generation, no computing power is consumed during image synthesis. Experimental demonstrations for MNIST, Fashion-MNIST, and Van Gogh-style artworks show performance statistically comparable to digital neural-network generative models, while offering orders-of-magnitude reductions in inference energy.

articles.title: A latent measure of cultural racism and its association with US mortality and life expectancy
data_sources: NCHS National Vital Statistics System (life expectancy)
Southern Poverty Law Center (Confederate symbols, hate groups)
Project Implicit Harvard (white NH IAT scores)
articles.core_conclusion: A validated Cultural Racism Factor (CRF) derived from seven state-level indicators via confirmatory factor analysis is associated with ~136 additional all-cause deaths per 100,000 and a one-year decline in life expectancy per unit increase, consistently for both Black and white US adults. The CRF captures cultural and ideological dimensions of systemic racism distinct from structural racism measures and is publicly available on Zenodo for reuse.

articles.title: Synergistic action on mitigation and adaptation pilot policies to enhance low-carbon resilience of Chinese cities
articles.core_conclusion: Running both low-carbon (mitigation) and climate-resilient (adaptation) pilot programs in the same city yields a synergistic gain in low-carbon resilience that outperforms either single pilot alone, across 286 Chinese cities (2005-2022). Effects are strongest in cities facing extreme weather and high disaster risk, and are amplified by green technology innovation, human capital, and communication infrastructure.

articles.title: Urban planning decisions expose Traveller sites to disproportionate environmental burdens
