articles.core_conclusion: In France, designated Traveller sites are systematically placed where environmental burdens are highest: cities with more disamenities are more likely to host a site, and within hosting cities the site is more exposed than any other residential area, including other disadvantaged households. The pattern is consistent with cost-minimization by municipalities and discriminatory preferences, showing how routine urban-planning decisions produce systemic socio-spatial inequality in Europe.

articles.title: Comparative analysis of smart city scientific research trends in the USA and China
articles.core_conclusion: Smart city research in the USA and China is simultaneously converging and diverging. Using LLMs to parse NSF and NSFC funded proposals, the authors find shared scientific inquiries but divergent national trajectories shaped by funding mechanisms and national context, exposing a persistent tension between techno-centric priorities and human-oriented imperatives that risks fragmenting the field.

articles.title: Environmental burden and health inequity in China's road-based express delivery
data_sources: China National Environmental Monitoring Centre observations
articles.core_conclusion: China's road-based express-delivery transport emitted 23.9 Mt CO2-equivalent and 166.4 kt atmospheric-pollutant-equivalent in 2021, with a sharply unequal spatial burden: transit regions handled only 12.7% of parcels yet bore 37.3% of emissions, and 75.2% of their pollution-related premature deaths were caused by other regions' deliveries. Pollution caused about 5,100 premature deaths in 2021, but synergistic mitigation could avert over 256,000 cumulative premature deaths by 2050.

articles.title: Partial flood defenses shift risks and amplify inequality in a core–periphery city
articles.core_conclusion: Partial levee construction in Surat lowers aggregate flood losses (₹31.24B core, ₹10.34B suburb) but redistributes risk inequitably: the Gini index rises ~20% for damage (0.55->0.66) and ~26% for exposure (0.31->0.39). Protected near-river wards gain up to 12 extra flood-free hours while some downstream areas flood up to 7 hours earlier, so defenses that benefit some can escalate vulnerability for others.

articles.title: Revealing building operating carbon dynamics for multiple cities
data_sources: Meta High Resolution Population Density Maps
Gridded real GDP per capita (nighttime light, 1 km, 2019)
Urban Heat Island Indicator (global all-sky LST, 1 km, 2020)
articles.core_conclusion: A generalizable open-science framework integrating multi-modal geospatial data and heterogeneous graph neural networks explains 78.4% of the variation in building operating carbon emissions across five climatically diverse cities (595,173 buildings). Planning history, urban form and socio-economic conditions jointly shape city-specific carbon profiles. Wealthier areas consistently exhibit the highest per capita emissions, yet low-GDP areas often bear the largest share of total city emissions — demanding spatially targeted rather than uniform decarbonization strategies.

articles.title: Urban meteorology–chemistry coupling in compound heat–ozone extremes
methods: F0AM box model (MCM v3.3.1) EKMA isopleths
articles.core_conclusion: Heatwave-reinforced turbulence redistributes precursors vertically, lifting NOx into the upper boundary layer and shifting the photochemical regime from VOC-sensitive at the surface to NOx-sensitive aloft, accelerating O3 production both at the surface and aloft over Chinese megacities. Stringent NOx controls narrow this vertical disparity; emission cuts implied by China's carbon-neutrality pledge could cut warm-season MDA8 O3 under heatwaves by 41-47% and drive heat-O3 co-occurrence below 0.1 days/yr by 2060 even as heatwaves intensify.

articles.title: Visitation patterns reveal service access disparities for ageing populations in the USA
methods: revealed accessibility metric (proportion of demand met within radius)
OLS regression with state fixed effects
articles.core_conclusion: High-ageing US Census Block Groups (where over 50% of residents are aged 65+) generally exhibit higher revealed service accessibility than low-ageing CBGs for healthcare, grocery, and housing services, but this advantage is regionally uneven and projected to erode by 2060. Up to 70% of high-ageing CBGs in some states lack any revealed access to ageing-specific services within 5 miles, and nationally 50.4% lack access to elder-specific services.

articles.title: Countrywide natural experiment links built environment to physical activity
articles.core_conclusion: A countrywide natural experiment of 2.1 million smartphone users and 7,447 city-to-city relocations provides robust causal evidence that more walkable built environments directly increase physical activity. Moving from a 25th- to 75th-percentile walkable US city raises daily steps by ~1,100 (equivalent to ~11 minutes of walking), the extra activity is predominantly moderate-to-vigorous, effects persist over 3 months, and residential self-selection is ruled out. Nationally, raising all US cities to Chicago-level walkability could bring 36 million more Americans into compliance with aerobic exercise guidelines.

articles.title: Mapping urban slums and their inequality in sub-Saharan Africa
articles.core_conclusion: Fusing open satellite/geospatial data with DHS household surveys, the authors map slum prevalence and asset-based wealth across 32 sub-Saharan African countries and find 54.6% of the urban population live in slums with lower wealth than non-slum areas. The proportion of slum dwellers declined over two decades, yet wealth inequality rose, especially in countries with large slum populations, showing that aggregate progress masks deepening intra-urban deprivation in services, living conditions, and wealth.

articles.title: Tree shortages in informal settlements across African cities
articles.core_conclusion: Mapping 53 million individual trees across 54 African cities reveals that the most informal neighborhoods have the largest shortage of green infrastructure per capita; tree cover declines as informality, building density and population density rise. Under continued urban expansion, 28 cities are projected to lose tree cover beyond their current boundaries by 2050, making tree retention an urgent, equity-critical part of informal-settlement upgrading.

articles.title: Unforeseen high continental-scale soil microbiome homogenization in urban greenspaces
articles.core_conclusion: Across 13 Chinese cities and four land uses, urban greenspaces host higher local (alpha) richness of soil bacteria, protists and fungi (but not metazoa) than farmlands and forests, an effect closely tied to higher soil pH; yet at the continental scale urban communities are more homogeneous (lower beta diversity) than farmland communities. Urbanization thus drives biotic homogenization and more synchronous responses to environmental stressors, a hidden risk for the resilience of city soils.

articles.title: Standardized heat islands and persistence drive modeled urban heat events
methods: standardized mean UHI (mean normalized by variance)
data_sources: study data and code on Zenodo
articles.core_conclusion: The urban-rural difference in heat-event occurrence is driven not by the mean UHI alone but by the standardized mean UHI (mean normalized by temperature variance), which explains up to 94% of urban-rural hot-day differences in two global models. For multi-day heat events, temperature persistence (lag-1 autocorrelation) becomes a second decisive factor, so cities with high standardized UHI and high persistence face disproportionate extreme-heat exposure.

articles.title: Acute mental health benefits of urban nature
articles.core_conclusion: A systematic review of 449 studies and meta-analysis of 78 field experiments show that short-term exposure to urban nature provides substantial, broad-spectrum benefits across 12 mental-health outcomes. Green spaces such as urban forests and parks most strongly reduce negative moods (depression, anxiety) and enhance well-being; benefits are largest in young adults but consistently positive across all ages.

articles.title: Global wood harvest is sufficient for climate-friendly transitions to timber cities
data_sources: World Bank waste database (Kaza et al.)
articles.core_conclusion: Transitioning 90% of the global new urban population to engineered timber buildings does not require more wood harvest: the shift pathway (redirecting 40% of wood fuel to industrial use) or circular pathway (raising wood-waste recycling from 30% to 57%) can meet timber city demand within current harvest volume, reducing cumulative CO2-eq. emissions by 40.8 Gt by 2100 versus business as usual. The most critical global actions are reducing virgin wood combustion for bioenergy and scaling wood-waste recycling, not expanding plantation forests.

articles.title: A generalized relationship between dose of greenness and mental health response
data_sources: Dose GitHub repository (extracted curves + R code)
articles.core_conclusion: The dose-of-greenness to mental-health relationship is best described by a generalized quadratic (inverted-U) curve rather than a linear one. Pooling 69 refitted curves, a quadratic pattern fits most published curves, and the authors identify highly beneficial and best doses for both eye-level and top-down greenness. A moderate dose of urban greenness, not the maximum, yields the greatest mental-health benefit.

articles.title: Contextualizing ancient texts with generative neural networks
methods: T5-derived transformer decoder with rotary embeddings
ResNet-8 vision network for image modality
multi-task learning with Bayesian loss weighting
cosine similarity retrieval over contextual embeddings
UMAP dimensionality reduction for embedding visualization
data_sources: LED (Latin Epigraphic Dataset, merged and processed)
articles.core_conclusion: Aeneas, a multimodal generative neural network, contextualizes Latin inscriptions by retrieving historically-enriched textual parallels and advancing restoration, dating, and geographic attribution. In the largest historian-AI study to date (23 epigraphers), Aeneas-retrieved parallels were useful in 90% of cases, improved historians' confidence by 44%, and human-AI teams outperformed both humans and AI alone on all three epigraphic tasks. Automated dating achieved a 13-year mean distance from ground truth.

articles.title: Experiments implementing small commuting models lack gravitational features
articles.core_conclusion: Small quantum circuits (commuting models) used in prior traversable-wormhole experiments lack three necessary gravitational features: thermalization, a teleportation signal that holds beyond the ML training regime, and perfect size winding. These failures demonstrate that fitting a small learned model to a handful of observables is insufficient to claim holographic gravitational dynamics.

articles.title: Reply to: Experiments implementing small commuting models lack gravitational features
data_sources: Classical numerical simulations of SYK Hamiltonian
Google Sycamore quantum processor (original experiment, ref. 2)
articles.core_conclusion: Holographic duality in the SYK wormhole experiment applies process-specifically to the two-sided teleportation protocol, not to the entire Hamiltonian. The SYK Hamiltonian is not 'gravitational' for all processes, but the targeted two-sided teleportation has a valid traversable wormhole interpretation because the relevant low-temperature correlation functions are dominated by the Schwarzian mode.

articles.title: Association between urban shrinkage and excess mortality during the COVID-19 pandemic
articles.core_conclusion: Across 1,142 US counties during the COVID-19 pandemic, shrinking counties had 165% higher excess deaths and 142% more mortality peaks than growing counties; counties contracting in both population and GRDP fared worst, excess deaths rose with the severity of shrinkage, and socioeconomic disadvantages concentrated in shrinking places were linked to elevated mortality, motivating place-based public health strategies.

articles.title: The development of aperiodic neural activity in the human brain
methods: structural MRI grey matter volume extraction
data_sources: Clinical multi-site intracranial EEG recordings (USA hospitals)
articles.core_conclusion: Aperiodic (1/f-like) neural activity flattens with age into young adulthood across both association and sensorimotor cortices, challenging models of early sensorimotor maturation. In the prefrontal cortex, attentional state differentially modulates aperiodic slopes in children versus adults; task-based slopes in the middle frontal gyrus mediate age-related gains in episodic memory, directly linking PFC cognitive-control maturation to memory development across the lifespan.

articles.title: Unveiling deployable rooftop solar potential across Chinese cities
methods: rooftop area estimation on 1 km2 fishnet
deployment pathway optimization (economic value vs carbon reduction)
articles.core_conclusion: Across 367 Chinese cities, only about 42% of the national technical potential of rooftop PV is realistically deployable once real electricity consumption and regulatory constraints are imposed. These constraints shift deployment focus from rural areas to urban public and industrial buildings, and from western to northern and central regions; initial deployment should prioritize public and industrial buildings in central and southern cities.

articles.title: Public transport accessibility in villages in and around major Chinese cities
data_sources: Chinese National People's Government village records
articles.core_conclusion: Headline public-transport access gains hide deep rural shortfalls: against an official claim of over 80% convenient PT access in major Chinese cities, only two of ten cities' villages meet the benchmark (lowest 34%). New bus stations often fail to track population shifts, and the share of villagers on high-fare-only routes rose even as average fares fell. A narrow 10-minute walking metric masks added time and cost burdens, so SDG 11 needs a population-responsive planning approach.

articles.title: Disproportionate flood exposure for slum populations of the Global South
articles.core_conclusion: Using machine learning on open satellite imagery to map slum populations across the Global South, the study finds roughly one in three slum dwellers lives in a floodplain and that slum residents are about 32% more likely to settle in floodplains than residents in adequate housing, with concentration highest in areas already hit by severe floods - evidence of systematic, disproportionate flood exposure for the most vulnerable urban communities.

articles.title: A foundation model to predict and capture human cognition
methods: negative log-likelihood evaluation over held-out participants
regularized linear regression for fMRI decoding
linear mixed-effects models for response-time prediction
AIC-based model comparison and protected exceedance probability
data_sources: Psych-101 (160 psychological experiments transcribed to natural language)
articles.core_conclusion: Centaur, a large language model (Llama 3.1 70B) fine-tuned on Psych-101 — 160 psychological experiments, 60,092 participants, 10,681,650 choices — can predict and simulate human behaviour across virtually any cognitive domain expressible in natural language, outperforming both the base LLM and domain-specific cognitive models in almost every experiment. It generalizes to unseen cover stories, novel task structures, and entirely new domains, and its internal representations emerge aligned with human fMRI activity without explicit neuroscience supervision. A model-guided scientific discovery pipeline demonstrates how Centaur enables discovery of new interpretable cognitive theories from small datasets.

articles.title: Discovering cognitive strategies with tiny recurrent neural networks
methods: gated recurrent units (GRU) with 1-4 hidden units
articles.core_conclusion: Recurrent neural networks with 1-4 hidden units consistently outperform classical cognitive models (Bayesian inference, model-free and model-based RL) in predicting individual animal and human decisions across six reward-learning tasks, while remaining interpretable via dynamical systems analysis. Knowledge distillation enables accurate individual modelling from as few as ~350 trials per participant. The framework reveals novel cognitive strategies (state-dependent learning rates, reward-induced indifference, drift-to-the-other forgetting) absent from all existing cognitive models.

articles.title: Inter-brain neural dynamics in biological and artificial intelligence systems
data_sources: Source data XLSX files (provided with paper)
articles.core_conclusion: During social interaction, each individual's dorsomedial prefrontal cortex neural activity decomposes into a shared subspace synchronized across brains and a unique subspace private to each animal. GABAergic (inhibitory) neurons carry a disproportionately large shared subspace driven by both self- and partner-behaviour. The same shared-subspace phenomenon arises spontaneously in MARL agents as social behaviour emerges, and selectively disrupting the shared neural dimensions abolishes social actions, demonstrating shared neural dynamics are a functionally necessary and generalizable feature of interacting neural systems.

articles.title: Surveillance camera prevalence and racial diversity in ten US cities
articles.core_conclusion: Across ten densely populated US cities, computer-vision-plus-human-verification on Google Street View shows surveillance cameras are most prevalent in the most racially diverse neighborhoods rather than predominantly Black ones; increases in diversity driven by white in-movers are associated with more cameras, and the pattern persists net of crime rates, suggesting surveillance is used to monitor minority groups in diversifying neighborhoods.

articles.title: Ultrabroadband and band-selective thermal meta-emitters by machine learning
articles.core_conclusion: A machine learning paradigm achieves multiparameter inverse design of thermal meta-emitters spanning ultrabroadband and band-selective spectral regimes. The three-plane modelling method extends design into full 3D structural space from sparse data. Seven fabricated proof-of-concept devices surpass state-of-the-art optical and radiative cooling performance, providing a generalizable framework for nanophotonic emitter design.

articles.title: Controlling diverse robots by inferring Jacobian fields with deep networks
data_sources: Intel RealSense D415 RGB-D cameras (12 per setup)
articles.core_conclusion: Deep neural networks can learn a visuomotor Jacobian field — a dense 3D map of how every surface point on a robot responds to actuator commands — from a single camera, trained entirely self-supervised on random-command videos without expert annotation. The method makes no assumptions about robot materials, actuation or embedded sensing, and achieves accurate closed-loop control across four mechanically diverse platforms including soft pneumatic hands, auxetic wrist platforms, a 16-DoF rigid hand, and a low-cost 3D-printed arm.

articles.title: Systematic global stocktake of over 50,000 urban climate change studies
articles.core_conclusion: Supervised and unsupervised machine learning mine 53,295 urban climate-change studies from OpenAlex, including 19,733 spatially specific city case studies covering 23% of cities and 64% of the urban population, into a living searchable database. The literature grows exponentially (4.5x faster than the whole climate literature) but is biased toward large, wealthy, high-emitting Global North cities, while small fast-growing cities in Africa and Asia are severely underrepresented. The IPCC AR6 covered only 4.6% of this evidence and amplified the large-city bias, omitting technological solutions.

articles.title: Urbanization and scrub typhus expansion in temperate settings
data_sources: Nationwide Notifiable Infectious Diseases Reporting System (Jiangsu CDC)
Global annual terrestrial Human Footprint dataset (Mu et al. 2022)
articles.core_conclusion: Scrub typhus, historically endemic only to southern tropical China, is surging northward into temperate Eastern China. Across 16,775 Jiangsu case records (2006-2023), infections surged ~80% after 2014 and spread to more counties, with cases shifting from rural toward suburban and urban areas (urban dominating in 2022-2023). Higher urbanization (+6.2% odds) and human footprint (+22.3% odds) raise urban ST risk, while population aging partially counteracts urban spread; urban patients face longer onset-to-diagnosis delays (7 vs 4 days rural).

articles.title: A systematic review and Bayesian meta-analysis of co-offending characteristics
methods: Bayesian meta-regression (crime type, sex, age group)
data_sources: Official police and court records (via primary studies)
articles.core_conclusion: Across 110 studies from 17 countries (approx. 46 million offences and 3.5 million offenders), 29% of offences and 50% of offenders involved co-offending, with an average group size of 2.73. Property crimes show higher co-offence prevalence driven by practical collaboration needs; violent crimes exhibit larger group sizes as a by-product of group dynamics. Young offenders co-offend at rates 30 percentage points higher than adults and in larger groups. Male and female offenders co-offend at similar overall rates, but among violent offenders females co-offend at higher rates than males. Inconsistent reporting across studies impedes cross-study comparison, and the authors call for standardized co-offending metrics.

articles.title: Planning sustainable urban lighting for biodiversity and society
data_sources: land use / land cover (Montpellier Metropolitan Area)
articles.core_conclusion: There is no one-size-fits-all way to dim a city. Coupling remote-sensing + ecological connectivity modeling (species' needs) with socioeconomic choice modeling (resident acceptability) shows that light-pollution-mitigation measures produce trade-offs or synergies depending on local spatial context. For Montpellier, the authors rank the best lighting compromise per streetlight and deliver it as an open RShiny 'SustainLights' tool, arguing lighting policy must be tailored to each environmental and social context.

articles.title: Machine-learning design of ductile FeNiCoAlTa alloys with high strength
methods: alloy fabrication (arc melting, cold rolling, aging)
synchrotron SAXS for precipitate volume fraction
EBSD + TKD for GND density mapping
first-principles / SQS calculations (ordering energy, APB energy)
data_sources: AlCoCrFeNiTa FCC HEA literature + lab database (140 alloys)
Synchrotron SAXS (BL16B1, Shanghai Synchrotron Radiation Facility)
articles.core_conclusion: A domain knowledge-informed active learning pipeline identified Fe35Ni29Co21Al12Ta3 as the first bulk alloy achieving near-2-GPa yield strength (1.75 GPa) with 25% truly uniform elongation simultaneously. The unprecedented combination arises from an extreme dual-precipitate microstructure — coherent L12 nanoprecipitates (~67 vol%) and deformable incoherent B2 microparticles (~15 vol%) — that sustains work-hardening rate above 2 GPa throughout deformation, defeating plastic instability that limits all prior ultra-strong alloys.

articles.title: Unsupervised pretraining in biological neural networks
methods: retinotopic mapping via convolutional encoding model
data_sources: Janelia Research Campus calcium imaging dataset
articles.core_conclusion: Most neural plasticity in the mouse visual cortex after task learning is driven by unsupervised learning: unrewarded exposure to the same visual stimuli replicates nearly all cortical changes seen in task-trained animals. Plasticity is highest in medial higher visual areas and follows visual rather than spatial coding rules. A task-exclusive reward-prediction signal appears in anterior HVAs. Unsupervised pretraining with naturalistic stimuli demonstrably accelerates subsequent supervised task learning, establishing a direct biological parallel to pretraining in artificial neural networks.

articles.title: Artificial light at night outweighs temperature in lengthening urban growing seasons
articles.core_conclusion: Across 428 Northern Hemisphere cities (2014-2020), artificial light at night rises exponentially toward urban centers and exerts a stronger influence than air temperature in lengthening the urban growing season, especially by delaying its end (EOS). Urban growing seasons start ~6.4 days earlier and end ~7.1 days later than rural ones; ALAN's effect on EOS (R~0.49) significantly outweighs temperature's (R~0.003) in all seven climate zones, with effects varying by climate and latitude.

articles.title: Citizen science illuminates the nature of city lights
authors: Nachtlicht-BüHNE / Nachtlichter citizen-science collaboration (individual author names not provided in source metadata)
data_sources: VIIRS Day-Night Band composites (Earth Observation Group)
articles.core_conclusion: Citizen scientists using the Nachtlichter app counted and classified 234,044 light sources across ~22 km2 (33 communities, mainly Germany), producing the first large-scale ground-truth city-light inventory. These data translate satellite VIIRS-DNB radiance into intuitive lights per km2 (~219 lights/km2 per nW cm-2 sr-1 at midnight in Germany), show that advertising and aesthetic lighting outnumber streetlights in city centers, and estimate ~78 +/- 3 million lights remain on at midnight nationwide, signalling a large, mis-targeted mitigation opportunity.

articles.title: A fully open AI foundation model applied to chest radiography
articles.core_conclusion: Ark+, a fully open chest radiography foundation model pretrained by cyclically accruing and reusing knowledge from 13+ heterogeneous public datasets, outperforms proprietary models on thoracic disease diagnosis while supporting few-shot and zero-shot transfer, federated privacy-preserving learning, and robust performance under long-tailed distributions and dataset bias — demonstrating that open models built on aggregated public data can surpass closed large-data systems.

articles.title: The form of China's urban commercial expansion in the digital era
data_sources: China Building Rooftop Area (2.5 m) dataset
articles.core_conclusion: Across 287 Chinese cities (2015-2023), brick-and-mortar commerce became less visible: shops increasingly migrated to non-street-facing positions within blocks and to non-ground floors of buildings (excluding shopping centers). The share of these hidden establishments rose steadily and accelerated after COVID-19 even as total shop numbers declined, most strongly in experiential sectors (entertainment, exercise, beauty) and in higher socioeconomic-tier cities, which led the shift earlier and more intensely.

articles.title: Biodiversity-rich recreational areas near cities as a nature-based mental health solution
data_sources: World Database of Key Biodiversity Areas
Weiss et al. global friction surface
articles.core_conclusion: Over 96% of 9,034 cities worldwide have a biodiversity-rich recreational area (recreation-permitted protected areas, Key Biodiversity Areas or intact forest landscapes) within two hours, but affordability and visiting rates are highest in Europe, Oceania and North America. The extra mental-health benefit of high-biodiversity nature experiences averts an estimated 137,299 DALYs (90% UR 10,368-598,509) of depression and anxiety and is cost-effective in developed settings; siting new near-city recreation-permitted protected areas could jointly benefit biodiversity and urban mental health.

articles.title: Multi-timescale reinforcement learning in the brain
articles.core_conclusion: Dopaminergic neurons encode reward prediction error with a diversity of cell-specific discount factors rather than a single shared timescale. This multi-timescale architecture provides computational advantages over single-timescale agents, explains the heterogeneity of dopamine ramps, and mechanistically accounts for the hyperbolic discounting observed in animal and human behaviour.

articles.title: The link between low-stress bicycle facilities and bicycle commuting
data_sources: Google Earth / Street View historic imagery
articles.core_conclusion: Lower-stress bicycle facilities drive more commuting. Across 6 years and 14,011 block groups in 28 US cities, block groups installing protected bicycle lanes saw commuter increases 1.8x larger than standard-lane block groups, 1.6x larger than sharrow block groups, and 4.3x larger than no-facility block groups; per mile, PBLs were ~52.5% stronger and sharrows ~281.2% weaker than standard lanes. The findings validate stress-based bicycle planning and refine the 'build it and they will come' mantra.

articles.title: A foundation model for the Earth system
methods: in-memory replay buffer for long-lead-time training
latitude-weighted RMSE and anomaly correlation coefficient
thresholded RMSE for extreme event evaluation
data_sources: CAMS global atmospheric composition forecasts and analysis
articles.core_conclusion: Aurora is a 1.3-billion-parameter foundation model for the Earth system, pretrained on more than one million hours of diverse geophysical data. It outperforms specialized operational forecasting systems for air quality (74% of targets vs CAMS), ocean waves (86% of variables vs HRES-WAM), tropical cyclone tracks (100% of agencies worldwide), and high-resolution weather (92% of targets vs IFS HRES) — all at orders of magnitude lower computational cost — marking the first time an AI model surpasses full operational tropical cyclone forecasts up to 5 days.

articles.title: Land subsidence risk to infrastructure in US metropolises
articles.core_conclusion: Sentinel-1 InSAR at ~28 m (2015-2021) shows at least 20% of every one of the 28 most populous US cities is sinking, mainly from groundwater extraction, affecting ~34 million people; subsidence is a major inland (not only coastal) hazard, and infrastructure damage is driven by differential land motion, placing more than 29,000 buildings in high/very-high damage-risk zones.

articles.title: Disparities in access to sustainable dining options across the Tokyo Metropolis
data_sources: National Land Information Division (MLIT) railway data
articles.core_conclusion: Access to sustainable dining in Tokyo is severely and spatially unequal. Using 112,892 restaurants and 3,649 menu items, a multidimensional Sustainable Dining Out Index (SDOI) combining price, supply-chain carbon footprint and nutrition shows restaurants cluster around railway stations, yet sustainability varies sharply across station vicinities, lines and the 23 wards, leaving up to 9 million daily passengers in unsustainable dining environments. Optimizing restaurant spatial distribution around high-foot-traffic stations is proposed to close the gap.

articles.title: Stimuli-directed emotions overrule cognitive evaluations in urban neighborhood preferences
data_sources: primary VR experiment data (SAM, EDA, questionnaire)
articles.core_conclusion: In immersive 360-degree VR experiments with 269 participants in Switzerland and the Netherlands, stimuli-directed emotions (SAM valence/arousal plus electrodermal signals) overrule the cognitive evaluation of neighborhood characteristics in shaping residential preference. Peri-urban neighborhoods were least preferred (12.8%) despite being rated highest on characteristics; preference was driven by emotional valence and personal experience, not direct cognitive evaluation.

articles.title: Declining short-term emission control opportunity for major events in Chinese cities
articles.core_conclusion: Short-term emission controls staged around major events in Chinese cities are yielding shrinking returns. Across 11 events in east China (2010-2023), satellite NOx inversions show controls can be more effective in neighboring than host cities for some events, and the dominant abated sector has shifted from power to industry and transportation, indicating the cheap emission budget is already exhausted by long-term policy and that future controls must be more spatially and sectorally targeted.

articles.title: Inequities in spatial access to childbirth care in the Grand Conakry conurbation, Guinea
data_sources: Ministry of Health facility master list
Sentinel-2 / Esri Living Atlas land cover
articles.core_conclusion: In Grand Conakry (~2.6 million people), short geographic distances mask long travel times: under average traffic nearly all WoCBA reach any childbirth facility within 30 minutes, but coverage drops to 82% for public hospitals and travel can exceed 2 hours under congestion in peri-urban communes. Access is consistently pro-rich, with peri-urban Dubréka and Coyah near medical deserts; targeted road, transport, and public-hospital investment is needed.

articles.title: A strong but uneven increase in urban tree cover in China over the recent decade
articles.core_conclusion: Using high-resolution nanosatellite imagery and a U-Net canopy detector across all major Chinese cities, 11.47% of urban area was tree-covered in 2019 and 76% of cities gained canopy since 2010. The increase was strong but uneven: mega-cities (+6.64%) and public-service (+3.09%) and residential (+1.79%) land uses benefited most, so national greening policy works overall but does not benefit all cities or land uses equally.

articles.title: Custom CRISPR–Cas9 PAM variants via scalable engineering and machine learning
articles.core_conclusion: By combining structure-informed saturation mutagenesis, bacterial selection (HT-PAMDA), and neural network training (PAMmla), the authors characterized ~1,000 SpCas9 variants and predicted PAM preferences for 64 million enzymes. Bespoke PAM-specific editors outperform generalist SpCas9 variants (SpG, SpRY) in human cells while reducing off-target editing. An in silico-directed evolution pipeline designs allele-selective editors, demonstrated for the RHO P23H disease allele in human cells and mice.

articles.title: Towards multimodal foundation models in molecular cell biology
articles.core_conclusion: Multimodal foundation models pretrained on diverse omics datasets (genomics, transcriptomics, epigenomics, proteomics, metabolomics, spatial profiling) can produce holistic cell/gene/tissue maps and, via context-specific transfer learning, enable unprecedented applications from cell-type recognition and biomarker discovery to in silico perturbation, defining a new AI-empowered paradigm for molecular cell biology.

articles.title: Sources of organizational variability in fatal police shootings in the USA
data_sources: LEMAS 2016 survey (Law Enforcement Management and Administrative Statistics)
US Census Bureau American Community Survey (ACS)
CDC firearm-suicide proxy for gun prevalence
articles.core_conclusion: Fatal police shooting rates across 2,727 US agencies (2015-2020) are far from random and are primarily explained by social context — crime rates, 911 call rates, officers per capita, and gun prevalence — rather than by racial/ethnic composition or most organizational policies. Persistent outlier agencies exist even after controlling for context; if all agencies performed like the best-performing ones, a 40% reduction in annual fatal police shootings is implied.

articles.title: Sustaining the local color of a global city
data_sources: historical photographs, paintings, postcards (National Museum of Singapore, NLB)
articles.core_conclusion: A chromatic reconstruction of 3,103 inhabited heritage shophouses in Singapore over 40+ years shows local color is not constant but continuously re-inscribed through historicization, ethnicization and commercialization, manifesting across five color dimensions (dominant colors, complexity, harmony, saturation, value). Color should be treated as a living part of the city's sociocultural ecology rather than a fixed material attribute to be preserved in isolation.

articles.title: Foundation model of neural activity predicts response to new stimulus types
methods: four-module ANN (perspective + modulation + ConvLSTM core + readout)
foundation pretraining on pooled multi-mouse neural recordings
transfer learning with frozen core and lightweight per-subject fine-tuning
out-of-distribution generalization evaluation (CC_norm across stimulus domains)
logistic regression on 512-d readout feature weights for anatomical prediction
parametric tuning analysis (OSI, DSI, SSI) in silico vs. in vivo
data_sources: In-house two-photon calcium imaging (14 mice, GCaMP6s, Baylor College of Medicine)
MICrONS serial electron microscopy anatomy (~60,000 neurons, 500M synapses)
articles.core_conclusion: A foundation model trained on ~135,000 neurons from 14 mice generalizes with minimal fine-tuning to new mice and entirely new stimulus domains (coherent motion, noise, Gabor patches), and its readout feature weights serve as functional barcodes predicting anatomical cell types, dendritic morphology and synaptic connectivity in the MICrONS connectomics dataset.

articles.title: Foundation model of neural activity predicts response to new stimulus types
methods: foundation-core pre-training on pooled multi-mouse data
ray-tracing perspective module with eye tracking
von Mises mixture model fitting for orientation/direction tuning
spike-triggered average (STA) + 2D Gaussian fitting for spatial tuning
logistic regression with nested cross-validation for cell-type classification
lesion studies for module contribution analysis
data_sources: In-house two-photon calcium imaging (GCaMP6s, Slc17a7-Cre x Ai162 mice)
Static natural image library (Walker et al.)
articles.core_conclusion: A foundation model trained on calcium-imaging data from ~135,000 mouse visual-cortex neurons achieves state-of-the-art prediction of dynamic neural responses to natural videos and generalizes out-of-distribution — to Gabor patches, noise patterns, dot kinematograms, and static images — without any training on those stimulus classes. The model's compact readout embeddings (functional barcodes) accurately predict anatomical cell types, dendritic morphology, and synaptic connectivity in the MICrONS connectomics dataset, demonstrating that a single foundation model can bridge functional and structural neuroscience at scale.

articles.title: Multimodal cell maps as a foundation for structural and functional genomics
methods: self-supervised multimodal autoencoder with reconstruction and triplet loss
data_sources: U2OS AP-MS interactions (this study, MassIVE MSV000097168)
articles.core_conclusion: Joint AP-MS and immunofluorescence measurement of over 5,100 proteins in U2OS cells, integrated by a self-supervised multimodal autoencoder, resolves 275 subcellular assemblies across four orders of magnitude. The map assigns new functions to 975 proteins, yields 111 novel heterodimeric structures, and decodes 772 paediatric tumours to find 21 recurrently mutated assemblies and 102 validated new cancer proteins. An open portal and Python toolkit enable the community to build cell maps across any cell type or disease state.

articles.title: Towards accurate differential diagnosis with large language models
methods: multi-task learning on medical QA and dialogue
paired t-test and Wilcoxon signed-rank test
inductive thematic analysis of semi-structured interviews
data_sources: MultiMedQA (MedQA USMLE, MedMCQA, HealthSearchQA, LiveQA, MedicationQA)
articles.core_conclusion: AMIE, a PaLM 2-based LLM fine-tuned for clinical diagnostic reasoning, outperformed unassisted board-certified physicians on 302 NEJM CPC cases (top-10 accuracy 59.1% vs. 33.6%, P=0.04). As an assistive tool, AMIE significantly improved clinicians' DDx quality, comprehensiveness, and accuracy compared to standard search tools (top-10 accuracy 51.7% vs. 44.4%, P=0.03) without adding task time, demonstrating potential to upskill clinicians and democratize specialist-level diagnostic reasoning.

articles.title: Towards conversational diagnostic artificial intelligence
methods: multi-axis clinical rubric evaluation (PACES, PCCBP, GMCPQ)
top-k DDx accuracy with bootstrapped CIs and FDR correction
data_sources: Licensed de-identified US clinical dialogue corpus (98,919 transcripts)
OSCE scenario packs from India, Canada, and United Kingdom
articles.core_conclusion: AMIE, a large language model optimized for diagnostic dialogue via self-play-based simulated learning, outperformed board-certified primary care physicians on 30 of 32 specialist-rated and 25 of 26 patient-actor-rated axes in a randomized, double-blind crossover OSCE study across 159 scenarios from Canada, India, and the UK. While a milestone for conversational medical AI, the text-chat interface, simulated patients, and English-centric scenarios require substantial further research before real-world clinical translation.

articles.title: Universal photonic artificial intelligence acceleration
articles.core_conclusion: A photonic AI processor achieves near-electronic numerical precision for the first time on production AI workloads — ResNet, BERT, and the DeepMind Atari deep reinforcement learning algorithm — marking photonic computing's entry into genuine competition with established electronic AI accelerators and an essential step toward post-transistor computing.

articles.title: Grid connections and inequitable access to electricity in African cities
articles.core_conclusion: A grid connection in Kampala's informal settlements does not guarantee good electricity. Tracing electrical and financial flows among the utility, informal supply intermediaries and households across 25 settlements, the authors identify 29 distinct service arrangements that deliver power of limited and uneven affordability, reliability, voltage stability, precarity, autonomy and safety. The 'gold-standard' grid thus reproduces deep inequity, and binary metrics like the Multi-Tier Framework fail to capture it.

articles.title: An AI-based analysis of zoning reforms in US cities
methods: natural language processing of legal text
articles.core_conclusion: Natural language processing of zoning ordinances from over 2,000 US census-designated places detects the linguistic signature of form-based codes (FBCs). FBCs are widely adopted with strong regional variation and are associated with higher floor-to-area ratios, narrower and more consistent setbacks, smaller plots, better walkability, shorter commutes and more multifamily housing, showing NLP can turn legal text into a nationwide indicator of zoning reform.

articles.title: From the Ashes
data_sources: California Building Code compliance assessment (Wall et al.)
articles.core_conclusion: The 2025 Eaton and Palisades wildfires burned ~18,000 structures, killed 29 people, and displaced 200,000 in Los Angeles, exposing a persistent failure to learn from prior disasters. Only ~5% of homes met fire-resistant building standards; ignition sources (notably power lines) remain preventable; and the Sendai Framework and SDGs provide ready-made global governance anchors. Political misinformation compounded physical disaster, underscoring that social and political fabric is as critical as physical land-use management in urban wildfire resilience.

articles.title: Multimodal generative AI for medical image interpretation
articles.core_conclusion: Multimodal generative medical image interpretation (GenMI) — AI that reads medical images and writes narrative diagnostic reports — is advancing rapidly and early evidence suggests it can approach human expert performance in radiology, pathology, and dermatology. Critical barriers remain in validating accuracy at scale, ensuring transparency, and capturing nuanced clinical impressions. The authors propose a clinician-empowerment 'AI resident' paradigm as the safest near-term deployment model.

articles.title: Synaptic and neural behaviours in a standard silicon transistor
methods: statistical CDF and box-plot analysis of synaptic cycling
hot-electron and hot-hole injection analysis for charge-trapping retention
data_sources: Commercial 180-nm and 500-nm bulk-silicon MOSFET devices (multi-project wafer, 30 dies)
Zenodo experimental dataset (raw I–V and pulsed measurement data)
Zenodo SPICE models and netlists (open-source SPICE + BSIM)
articles.core_conclusion: A single standard bulk-silicon CMOS transistor biased unconventionally at the edge of punch-through avalanche in floating-bulk configuration exhibits both leaky-integrate-and-fire neural behaviour and short/long-term synaptic plasticity without exotic materials. A 2-transistor NS-RAM cell continuously tunable between neuron and synapse modes achieves 100% yield, ultra-low device-to-device variability, >10 million endurance cycles, and firing energy down to 415 pJ per micrometer — reducing neuromorphic hardware from 18+ transistors per neuron and 6 per synapse to just 1–2.

articles.title: Optimizing generative AI by backpropagating language model feedback
articles.core_conclusion: TextGrad is a task-agnostic framework that performs backpropagation through natural language: an LLM gradient engine critiques each component's output in text and propagates those critiques upstream to automatically refine prompts, code, molecules, or treatment plans. Across five heterogeneous domains, TextGrad consistently outperforms strong baselines without any numerical gradient computation, demonstrating that the backpropagation abstraction generalizes from numerical to linguistic computation.

articles.title: Natural behaviour is learned through dopamine-mediated reinforcement
data_sources: Source data XLSX files provided with the paper
articles.core_conclusion: Developmental vocal learning in juvenile zebra finches is driven by dopamine-mediated reinforcement learning: dopamine in Area X activates after syllable renditions closer to the adult song target and is suppressed after worse renditions. Dopamine encodes a performance prediction error consistent with an actor-critic model — tracking performance history rather than a fixed target — and predicts the future direction of song evolution, suggesting it drives rather than merely reflects learning.

articles.title: Scaling and network evolution of technology transfer in US cities
articles.core_conclusion: Across 166 years (1858-2023) of US patent records, technology transfers scale superlinearly with city population, with exponents ranked intracity transfer > intercity transfer-in > intercity transfer-out. The transfer network's evolution combines nationwide space-filling, an emerging core-periphery hierarchy among cities, and rising functional polycentricity within cities; these dynamics grow in tandem with the scaling exponents, linking urban scaling laws to network evolution and to local vs. nonlocal knowledge interactions.

articles.title: Real-time inference for binary neutron star mergers using machine learning
data_sources: LVK O2/O3 and design-sensitivity noise PSDs
articles.core_conclusion: DINGO-BNS performs complete Bayesian parameter estimation of all 17 binary neutron star parameters in just 1 second using simulation-based inference with normalizing flows, three orders of magnitude faster than conventional methods and without sacrificing accuracy. Novel prior conditioning enables event-specific prior tuning at inference time without retraining; physics-informed data compression (heterodyning + multibanding) achieves 60-650x lossless dimensionality reduction. Pre-merger inference delivers ~30% improved sky localization over BAYESTAR and scales to hour-long signals for next-generation detectors.

articles.title: Parallel scaling of elite wealth in ancient Roman and modern cities with implications for understanding urban inequality
data_sources: Henley & Partners / New World Wealth billionaire report
articles.core_conclusion: Physical expressions of elite wealth scale sublinearly with urban population size (exponent means ~0.3-0.5) in strikingly similar ways across Imperial Roman cities (monuments, dedicatory inscriptions) and modern cities (skyscrapers, billionaires), despite over 1,000 years and vast cultural/economic differences. This suggests an ancient, enduring mechanism of urban inequality; an agent-based network model implicates preferential attachment (unequal access to interaction potential) as a candidate driver and an extension of settlement scaling theory.

articles.title: A systematic review of smart city governance in the Southern African Development Community
data_sources: policy documents and government reports (grey literature)
articles.core_conclusion: A qualitative systematic review combining academic literature with policy documents finds smart-city scholarship and practice in SADC dominated by technology- and infrastructure-centric, Global-North framings, while regional urban dynamics, governance and digital-citizenship realities are under-examined. The literature is organized into five research themes, and the authors argue a credible SADC smart-city agenda must adopt local, governance-oriented lenses and confront digitalization and datafication rather than transplanting universal blueprints.

articles.title: Contrasting effects of urbanization on vegetation between the Global South and Global North
data_sources: Gridded HDI and GDP (Kummu et al.)
articles.core_conclusion: Across 4,718 cities worldwide from 2000 to 2019, positive indirect urbanization effects (vegetation growth enhancement) offset 56.85% of the negative direct effects (vegetation cover loss). This compensation is much stronger in Global North cities (79.13%) than Global South cities (38.01%), driven by socioeconomic differences including GDP per capita, HDI, population density, and growth rates. Under all three SSP scenarios, approximately 60% of Global North cities but only 30% of Global South cities will become greener by 2040, representing a growing urban vegetation equity gap.

articles.title: Increasing fire risks in cities worldwide under warming climate
data_sources: national census / statistics population and area
articles.core_conclusion: Across 2,847 cities, warming reshapes urban fire risk by type: building fires follow a U-shaped (quadratic) curve minimized near the ~24 C thermal-comfort point, while vehicle and outdoor fires rise linearly by (2.5 ± 0.8)% and (4.7 ± 2.2)% per +1 C. Under SSP5-8.5 by 2100, building fires could fall (4.6 ± 1.1)% while vehicle and outdoor fires could rise (11.6 ± 3.7)% and (22.2 ± 10.2)%, giving fire services a quantitative basis to reallocate budgets across cities.

articles.title: Skill dependencies uncover nested human capital
methods: nestedness metrics (NODF, checkerboard score, temperature, overlap index)
nestedness contribution score (c_s) with 5000 randomized counterparts
data_sources: Burning Glass Technologies / Lightcast resume dataset
articles.core_conclusion: Human capital has a nested hierarchical structure where advanced specialized skills depend directionally on broader foundational ones, inferred from asymmetric conditional probabilities in occupation requirements. Skills aligned with this nested hierarchy command higher wages and require longer education, while misaligned skills face long-term wage penalties. This nested structure became measurably more pronounced between 2005 and 2019, raising barriers to upward mobility and amplifying disparities across gender and racial/ethnic groups.

articles.title: An opponent striatal circuit for distributional reinforcement learning
data_sources: Allen Mouse Brain Common Coordinate Framework
articles.core_conclusion: The mammalian striatum encodes full reward distributions rather than just mean reward value, using an opponent circuit in which D1 MSNs preferentially represent the optimistic (right) tail and D2 MSNs the pessimistic (left) tail of the reward distribution. Dopamine is necessary for learning these distributional representations but not for mean value coding. The REDRL model formalizes this as a biologically grounded implementation of distributional reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Underlying rules of evolutionary urban systems in Africa
articles.core_conclusion: Across 1950-2020 Africa's urban systems follow three simple rules under one unified continental city definition: Zipf's law shows growing population concentration in large cities (dispersed -> centralized distributions); Gibrat's law holds since the 1990s (growth independent of initial size); and the urban scaling law reveals unexpected super-linear scaling of built-up area with population (lower land-use efficiency in large cities, especially Eastern and Western Africa), with declining scaling exponents over time signaling maturing economies of scale.

articles.title: Toward change in the uneven geographies of urban knowledge production
data_sources: World Bank 2018 World Urban Population Database
articles.core_conclusion: Although 84% of the world's urban population lives in the Global South and East, only 42% of authors and 15% of editors in 25 leading Anglophone urban journals (17% and 10% for 9 handbooks) are based outside the North. Gatekeeping is far more Northern than authorship, a Northern PhD is almost a prerequisite for editorship (91% of gatekeepers), and journals with less Northern boards and lower theory orientation publish more non-Northern authors — identifying structural levers for change.

articles.title: Learning produces an orthogonalized state machine in the hippocampus
methods: two-photon random access mesoscope calcium imaging
clone-structured causal graph (CSCG) with Baum-Welch EM
data_sources: GCaMP6f two-photon calcium imaging (in-house, Janelia Research Campus)
Computational model simulation logs (CSCG, RNNs, LSTM, transformer, Hebbian-RNN)
articles.core_conclusion: As mice learn the 2ACDC virtual-reality memory task, hippocampal CA1 neurons progressively decorrelate their activity for visually identical but task-distinct states, forming an orthogonalized state machine (OSM). Among all computational models tested, only the clone-structured causal graph (CSCG), a hidden Markov model variant trained by expectation-maximization, reproduces both the final orthogonalized representations and the ordered step-by-step decorrelation trajectory, identifying hidden state inference as a fundamental algorithmic principle of hippocampal cognitive map formation.

articles.title: Social disparities in neighborhood flood exposure in 44,698 urban neighborhoods in Latin America
methods: city-specific Poisson model (slope index of inequality)
articles.core_conclusion: Across 276 Latin American cities (44,698 neighborhoods, ~228 million residents, 117 satellite-observed floods, 2000-2018), flood exposure follows a steep social gradient: about 1 in 4 residents of lowest-education neighborhoods lived in a neighborhood that flooded versus 1 in 20 in highest-education neighborhoods (4.3x). Within-city disparities are even sharper (median slope index of inequality ~8.3, present in 80% of cities). Lower-education, coastal, less-dense, greener, peripheral and steeper neighborhoods flooded more. The analysis is descriptive, and authors urge targeting flood adaptation to lower-SES neighborhoods.

articles.title: A generative model for inorganic materials design
methods: joint diffusion process for atom types, coordinates, and periodic lattice
DFT relaxation and energy-above-hull stability evaluation
data_sources: Alex-MP-ICSD (reference hull corpus, 850,384 ordered + 117,652 disordered structures)
articles.core_conclusion: MatterGen is a diffusion-based generative model that produces stable, diverse inorganic crystal structures across the periodic table and can be fine-tuned to satisfy a broad range of property constraints — including chemistry, symmetry, magnetic density, bandgap, and bulk modulus. It generates structures more than twice as likely to be stable, unique, and new compared with prior generative models, and more than ten times closer to the DFT local energy minimum. Experimental synthesis of a generated TaCr2O6 structure confirmed a measured bulk modulus within 20% of the design target, establishing MatterGen as a foundational generative model for inorganic materials discovery.

articles.title: Targeting protein–ligand neosurfaces with a generalizable deep learning tool
methods: geometric deep learning (geodesic convolutional surface fingerprinting)
Rosetta FastDesign and MotifGraft scaffold grafting
biolayer interferometry (BLI) and surface plasmon resonance (SPR)
X-ray crystallography and cryo-EM structure determination
split NanoLuc and GEMS cell-based assays
data_sources: Binding MOAD (20,363 unique ligands for hydrophobicity parameterization)
Zenodo (zenodo.org/records/13737922 for experimental data; zenodo.org/records/7643697 for scaffold DB)
articles.core_conclusion: MaSIF-neosurf demonstrates that geometric deep learning surface fingerprints trained solely on protein-protein interfaces generalize without retraining to protein-ligand hybrid neosurfaces. Three de novo CID protein binders were computationally designed and experimentally validated at nanomolar affinities against drug-bound protein complexes, and the resulting CID systems function as precise drug-inducible ON-switches in cell-free reporter systems, mammalian cells, and a split CAR-T tumour-killing assay.

articles.title: A foundation model of transcription across human cell types
data_sources: Fetal scATAC-seq atlas (Domcke et al., descartes.brotmanbaty.org)
Fetal expression atlas (Cao et al., descartes.brotmanbaty.org)
TF motif PWMs (Vierstra et al., 2,179 motifs / 282 clusters)
TF protein interaction atlas (Goos et al., mass spectrometry)
articles.core_conclusion: GET (General Expression Transformer) is an interpretable foundation model pre-trained on chromatin accessibility data across 213 human fetal and adult cell types that achieves experimental-level accuracy (Pearson r = 0.94) in predicting gene expression in previously unseen cell types, using only motif binding scores and chromatin accessibility. GET also uncovers universal and cell-type-specific transcription factor interaction networks via causal discovery and AlphaFold structural analysis, and identifies a PAX5–NR2C2 interaction in B cells that mechanistically explains a leukaemia-predisposing germline mutation.

articles.title: A vision–language foundation model for precision oncology
data_sources: TCGA (NCI GDC Portal, cBioPortal, TCGA Pathology Reports)
articles.core_conclusion: MUSK is a vision-language foundation model pretrained on 50 million pathology images and 1 billion text tokens via unified masked modeling, achieving state-of-the-art performance across 23 benchmarks with minimal or no fine-tuning. By fusing histology images with clinical reports, it demonstrates strong outcome prediction for melanoma relapse, pan-cancer prognosis (16 cancer types), and immunotherapy response in lung and gastro-oesophageal cancers.

articles.title: Accurate predictions on small data with a tabular foundation model
methods: structural causal model (SCM) synthetic data generation
data_sources: synthetic SCM-generated datasets (~100 million per training run)
articles.core_conclusion: TabPFN, a tabular foundation model pre-trained on ~100 million synthetic datasets via structural causal models, outperforms all prior methods — including heavily tuned gradient-boosted tree ensembles — on small-to-medium tabular datasets (up to 10,000 samples, 500 features). Using in-context learning, it delivers classification predictions in 2.8 s versus 4 hours of tuning for baselines (5,140x speedup), while also enabling data generation, density estimation, reusable embeddings, and fine-tuning.

articles.title: Exacerbated heat stress induced by urban browning in the Global South
articles.core_conclusion: A data-driven 1 km summertime Heat Index dataset for 2,341 Global South cities (2003-2020) shows a mean warming rate of 0.41 +/- 0.01 C per decade. Urban browning (vegetation loss) significantly amplifies heat stress, adding over 0.05 C per decade in cities such as those in Nigeria, contrasting with greening-induced cooling in the Global North; Chinese and Indian cities show paradoxical cooling linked to greening amid development, motivating South-South knowledge exchange.

articles.title: Urban growth strategy in Greater Sydney leads to unintended social and environmental challenges
methods: systematic causal coding (Kim & Andersen; Eker & Zimmermann)
data_sources: City of Cities - A Plan for Sydney's Future (2005)
A Metropolis of Three Cities - Connecting People (2018)
articles.core_conclusion: Coding 500 pages of Greater Sydney urban plans (1968, 2005, 2018) into causal maps reveals a dominant growth strategy (3 reinforcing loops driving economic, population and infrastructure growth) that itself generated 10 balancing loops of social and environmental challenges (congestion, sprawl, unaffordable housing, pollution, heat islands, water stress). Policymakers progressively recognized the problems but never identified them as unintended consequences of growth nor changed the strategy, treating symptoms piecemeal and perpetuating a growth mental model.

articles.title: Vehicle–canine collaboration for urban pipeline methane leak detection
articles.core_conclusion: A two-stage vehicle-canine collaboration (VCC) strategy improves urban methane leak detection: vehicle-mounted sensors first screen the network to flag candidate leak areas, then sniffer canines pinpoint exact release points. Across ~4,000 km of distribution pipeline in 20 Chinese cities, canines localized 432 release sources within 220 vehicle-flagged leak areas, with underground steel pipelines and aboveground risers most leak-prone and leak density varying notably between cities.

articles.title: Decarbonizing urban residential communities with green hydrogen systems
articles.core_conclusion: For community green hydrogen systems (rooftop PV + hybrid hydrogen-battery storage) in North American residential communities, the deployment-pathway choice dominates cost-effectiveness: the cost gap between best and worst pathway reaches up to 60% under identical conditions. The household centralized program (HCP) is preferred for most communities, and deliberately increasing energy-storage demand triggers a hydrogen-storage scale effect that cuts system design costs up to fourfold.

articles.title: Intercity personnel exchange is more effective than policy transplantation at reducing water pollution
articles.core_conclusion: Moving people beats copying policies: intercity exchange of Chinese city leaders cut firm-level water pollution by 4.78-15.26% (2006-2013), contributing 39.45-57.98% of national industrial water-pollution reduction, while 'policy transplantation only' was insignificant or even harmful. Exchanged leaders carry tacit governance experience, broker intercity green investment and patent collaboration, and launch pro-green industrial policies; interprovincial and first-mover-to-late-comer flows give the largest, inequality-reducing effects.

articles.title: The decreasing housing utilization efficiency in China's cities
data_sources: Ministry of Civil Affairs division-change notices
articles.core_conclusion: Underutilization of urban housing, not outright vacancy, is China's pervasive problem. Using Housing Utilization Efficiency (HUE = actual population / population capacity) at the subdistrict level for 108 cities, overall HUE fell from 84% (2010) to 78% (2020); central old-town cores and lower-tier cities show the lowest, fastest-declining efficiency; and four evolution types capture distinct couplings of population movement, housing growth and HUE change.

articles.title: Unequal roles of cities in the intercity healthcare system
articles.core_conclusion: Reconstructing China's intercity healthcare system from 300M+ cell-phone users and ~2M nonlocal patient visits, the study shows cities play deeply unequal roles: 8% are national/regional hubs absorbing others' shortfall, 63% are unnoticed destinations serving returning migrant workers (pseudo patient mobility). The IHS generates a Matthew effect in which resource-rich cities (12%) strengthen local provision while shortage cities (35%) fall further behind, creating new structural inequalities even as it repairs spatial mismatch.

articles.title: Urban food delivery services as extreme heat adaptation
articles.core_conclusion: Urban residents in China use food delivery as an immediate behavioral adaptation to extreme heat: lunchtime orders rise over 12.6% from 20C to 35C and 21.4% at 40C across 100 cities (2017-2023). Benefits accrue more to female, high-income and older consumers, while heat exposure is transferred from consumers to delivery riders, making the service both adaptation and inequity and calling for policy intervention.

articles.title: Behaviour-based dependency networks between places shape urban economic resilience
articles.core_conclusion: Behaviour-based dependency networks derived from same-day GPS co-visitations among over 1 million POIs across five US cities exhibit markedly more long-distance connections than gravity-model baselines, and incorporating them improves the predictability of business resilience during COVID-19 by around 40% (adjusted R2 from 0.148 to 0.228). Neglecting these dependencies systematically underestimates the spatial reach of urban shocks; airports, stadiums and gas stations generate cascades extending 2.5–3.5 km while colleges and supercentres produce large but locally concentrated impacts (~1.5 km).

articles.title: Stable isotope evidence for pre-colonial maize agriculture and animal management in the Bolivian Amazon
methods: stable carbon and nitrogen isotope analysis (IRMS)
diachronic phase segmentation via Harris matrix stratigraphy
articles.core_conclusion: Stable carbon and nitrogen isotope analysis of 86 human and 68 animal bone-collagen samples from two Casarabe-culture sites in the Bolivian Amazon (ce ~700–1400) shows that maize was a dietary staple in the earliest occupation phase (comparable in intensity to Classic Maya populations) before declining after ce ~800. Muscovy duck isotope values indicate intentional maize feeding from as early as ce 800, providing the first direct isotopic evidence for animal management in the South American lowlands.

articles.title: The relationship between the youth-led Fridays for Future climate movement and voting, politician and media behaviour in Germany
articles.core_conclusion: The Fridays for Future youth climate movement in Germany is associated with a ~0.76 percentage-point increase in Green Party vote share per standard-deviation rise in county-level protest participation (n=960 county-election observations). Three mechanisms are identified: reverse intergenerational transmission of pro-environmental attitudes to parents (effect exists only among parents of school-aged children, n=76,563), Green politicians posting 18% more climate content on social media (n=197,830), and local newspapers publishing 9% more climate articles (n=47,060). Youth protests that bypass the voting age barrier can still catalyze the societal change needed to escape the climate trap.

articles.title: Urban inequality, the housing crisis and deteriorating water access in US cities
articles.core_conclusion: Household running-water access in US cities is deteriorating, especially since the 2008 financial crash. Plumbing poverty has urbanized (>71% urban in 2021), rose in 12 of the 15 largest cities in the latest period, and disproportionately affects households of color. A 'reproductive squeeze' of unaffordable housing, stagnant wages and cost-of-living pressure is pushing low-income, racialized households into homes without running water, exemplified by Portland, Oregon.

articles.title: Determining structures of RNA conformers using AFM and deep neural networks
methods: unsupervised machine learning with PCA and clustering (UML)
supervised deep neural network for r.m.s.d. estimation
FARFAR2 (Rosetta) RNA structure prediction for initial models
Fourier ring low-pass filtering for image processing
data_sources: NCI institutional data site (HorNet benchmark data, PDBs, AFM images)
FARFAR2 Rosetta structure prediction ensemble (10,000 models)
Cypher VRS AFM (Asylum Research) experimental images
articles.core_conclusion: HORNET (Holistic RNA structure determination using AFM, unsupervised machine learning, and deep neural networks) determines 3D topological structures of individual heterogeneous RNA conformers from single-molecule AFM images without signal averaging. Applied to six benchmark RNAs (approximately 56 million trajectory models), three RNase P RNA conformers, and five HIV-1 RRE conformers, it achieves top-10-model accuracies below approximately 6 Angstroms r.m.s.d., addressing a major gap in structural biology left by NMR, crystallography, and cryo-EM for flexible and conformationally heterogeneous RNAs.

articles.title: Response and sensitivity of urban plants with different seed dispersal modes
articles.core_conclusion: Across 16 Yunnan cities, the richness of spontaneous urban plants depends jointly on seed dispersal mode, climate, dispersal limitation and habitat quality. Autochory dominated (13/16 cities) and hydrochory was rarest; richness was higher in colder, more-humid climates but fell with greater dispersal limitation and lower habitat quality. Sensitivities differed by mode (autochory most sensitive to dispersal limitation, hydrochory to habitat quality and climate), implying that improving habitat quality and building green corridors would enhance urban biodiversity conservation.

articles.title: Domestic migration and city rank dynamics
methods: tail / shock detection vs Gaussian core
articles.core_conclusion: Across the USA, Canada, the UK and France, about 95% of cities (up to 99% of national population) show rescaled domestic net flows that are normally distributed, while ~5% experience migration shocks. Small cities are most vulnerable, with shocks typically driven by net inflows from larger, nearby cities, and net flows decay exponentially with distance. Domestic migration drives rank volatility in small cities, whereas large-city rank stability is sustained by international migration and natural increase.

articles.title: Cities incorporate equity in their climate policies but overlook procedural justice in decision-making
data_sources: C40 Knowledge Hub climate action plans
articles.core_conclusion: Across C40 cities with climate action plans, fewer than half lean toward embedding procedural justice (fair, inclusive decision processes), even though distributive equity language is common. Equity-pledge adoption, later C40 membership and Global-South location correlate with stronger procedural-justice integration; cities lacking it emphasize outcome fairness while excluding marginalized groups, producing tokenistic participation. Cities need concrete organizational tools, not normative prescription.

articles.title: Probabilistic weather forecasting with machine learning
data_sources: ENS / HRES operational forecasts (TIGGE archive, ECMWF)
NREL Wind Toolkit idealized turbine power curve
articles.core_conclusion: GenCast, a conditional diffusion model with a graph-transformer denoiser trained on 40 years of ERA5 reanalysis, generates probabilistic 15-day global weather ensembles at 0.25 degree resolution in about 8 minutes. It outperforms ECMWF ENS (the world's top operational ensemble forecast) on 97.2% of 1,320 evaluated variable-level-leadtime targets, with superior calibration, extreme-weather skill, tropical cyclone track prediction, and regional wind power forecasting.

articles.title: Untapped capacity of place-based peer-to-peer resource sharing for community resilience
data_sources: IHME King County life expectancy by Census Tract
Seattle Emergency Hubs / SNAP program documentation
articles.core_conclusion: Communities hold a large untapped reservoir of disaster-relief capacity in everyday neighbor-to-neighbor sharing. Modeling place-based peer-to-peer sharing in two Seattle communities, under a 5-day isolation scenario P2P sharing reduces community resilience loss by 13.4-100%, with on average 22-44 social ties per household sufficing for an 80% sharing rate of surplus resources, making bottom-up sharing a viable complement to top-down disaster response.

articles.title: National environmental monitoring and local enforcement strategies
articles.core_conclusion: The nationwide rollout of air-quality-monitoring stations cut urban PM2.5 by 8.03% in Chinese cities, but reductions were 0.57% larger in cells technically accessible to a monitor than in non-accessible cells. Local governments enforce strategically around sensors (less industrial activity, land-use change near monitors), producing nontrivial urban spatial inequalities in housing prices and mortality-related welfare. Monitoring technology should be designed with environmental justice in mind.

articles.title: Recasting China's urban wastewater management standards
data_sources: Jiangsu Regional Standard for urban WWTPs (Dept. of Ecology and Environment of Jiangsu Province, 2023)
US EPA NPDES National Pretreatment Program
EU Urban Wastewater Treatment Directive recast (Umweltbundesamt, 2023)
China 2023 Guidelines for Coordinated Pollution and Carbon Reduction in Wastewater Treatment (State Council)
articles.core_conclusion: China's current urban wastewater discharge standards are overly uniform, ecologically disconnected, and temporally rigid, and are likely to hinder rather than advance urban water sustainability. Reform toward holistic, localized, ecologically based, and dynamically adaptive standards — covering emerging contaminants, nutrient criteria calibrated to eutrophication risk, carbon-aware treatment incentives, and wastewater reuse quality regulations — is urgently needed, with the US, EU, Australia, and Israel offering concrete models.

articles.title: A cell atlas foundation model for scalable search of similar human cells
data_sources: GEO GSE280632 (new in vitro data generated in this study)
articles.core_conclusion: SCimilarity is a deep metric-learning foundation model that encodes 23.4 million single-cell RNA-seq profiles from 412 studies into a unified 128-dimensional hyperspherical space, enabling similarity searches in 0.05 seconds. Applied to interstitial lung disease macrophages, it retrieved fibrosis-associated cell states across multiple fibrotic diseases and identified a 3D hydrogel PBMC culture as the top in vitro recapitulation of the disease state — experimentally validated. It outperforms scGPT and scFoundation on cell-state retrieval while matching dedicated batch-integration methods.

articles.title: Learning high-accuracy error decoding for quantum processors
data_sources: DEM-fitted noise (pij correlations and XEB benchmarking)
articles.core_conclusion: AlphaQubit, a recurrent transformer-based neural network (Google DeepMind), outperforms all existing decoders including tensor-network methods on real Sycamore hardware at code distances 3 and 5, scales to distance 11 in simulation, and generalizes to 100,000 error-correction rounds. A pretrain-on-synthetic then finetune-on-experimental paradigm bridges the simulation-to-reality gap while handling complex noise (leakage, cross-talk) inaccessible to analytical decoders.

articles.title: The Human Cell Atlas from a cell census to a unified foundation model
methods: RNA velocity and trajectory inference (scVelo, CellRank)
articles.core_conclusion: Cell atlases — most prominently the Human Cell Atlas — are evolving through five perspectives: from a cell census, to 3D spatial maps, to genotype-to-phenotype causal maps, to 4D developmental maps, and ultimately to unified foundation models of biology integrating all modalities and scales. Enabled by AI/ML and molecular profiling advances, this trajectory is poised to transform medicine through cell-level understanding of disease, development, and perturbation response.

articles.title: Positive association between Internet use and mental health among adults aged ≥50 years in 23 countries
data_sources: SHARE (Survey of Health, Ageing and Retirement in Europe)
CHARLS (China Health and Retirement Longitudinal Study)
articles.core_conclusion: Among 87,559 adults aged ≥50 years across 23 countries, Internet use was associated with fewer depressive symptoms (pooled AME −0.09), higher life satisfaction (AME +0.07), and better self-reported health (AME +0.15) after adjusting for 14 confounders. The association held across genetic risk strata in the USA and England, strengthened with higher frequency and longer cumulative duration, and varied by country-level factors such as the Gini index and digital skill levels, supporting precision digital-inclusion policies for population-level mental health gains.

articles.title: Long-term exposure to ambient benzene and brain disorders among urban adults
methods: stratification by inflammation and metabolic syndrome
articles.core_conclusion: In a large UK urban cohort, long-term exposure to ambient benzene (per 0.30 ug/m3 IQR increment), even below 5 ug/m3, is associated with elevated risk of dementia (HR 1.18), major depression (HR 1.09) and anxiety disorder (HR 1.16). Neuroimaging linked exposure to the thalamus and superior temporal gyrus, giving population-level evidence that a common urban carcinogen also harms the brain.

articles.title: Foundation models for fast, label-free detection of glioma infiltration
methods: ordinal metric learning for data-efficient fine-tuning
k-nearest neighbour classification for foundation model evaluation
prospective single-arm non-inferiority clinical trial design
subgroup analysis by demographics, medical centre, WHO subtype
data_sources: SRH Foundation Dataset (13 medical centres, US + Europe)
articles.core_conclusion: FastGlioma is a visual foundation model pretrained on ~4 million label-free SRH microscopy images that detects glioma infiltration in fresh surgical tissue in under 10 seconds (AUROC 92.1 ± 0.9% across n=220 patients). It outperforms standard-of-care MRI and fluorescence-guided adjuncts, reducing the patient-level tumour miss rate from 24.0% to 3.8% (6.3× risk reduction) in a prospective comparison, and generalizes zero-shot to non-glioma brain tumour diagnoses.

articles.title: Regulation of humid heat by urban green space across a climate wetness gradient
articles.core_conclusion: Urban green space cools air but moistens it, and these effects offset for human heat stress: across 15 cities the daytime temperature effect is negatively correlated with the humidity effect, giving an essentially zero daytime change in wet-bulb temperature (delta Tw = -0.01 C). A net humid-heat reduction appears only at night and only in intermediate and wet climates (mean delta Tw = -0.35 C), driven mostly by reduced soil heat storage under vegetation. Green space is therefore not a one-size-fits-all humid-heat remedy.

articles.title: Unequal access to social, environmental and health amenities in US urban parks
data_sources: 2020 US Decennial Census / ACS block groups
Landsat Science Products (USGS, 2023) land surface temperature
