articles.core_conclusion: Across 122,988 contiguous-US urban parks, both park distribution and amenity quality track neighborhood demographics: park walksheds are systematically whiter than the rest of the same city, and parks in white neighborhoods are cooler and have more tree cover than parks serving more Hispanic and Black residents. These amenity inequities persist across all US census divisions, showing nationwide inequity in access to high-quality parks.

articles.title: Potential drivers of urban green space availability in Latin American cities
authors: Maria de Fatima Rodrigues Pereira de Pina
methods: random forest regression with permutation variable importance
articles.core_conclusion: Across 371 large cities in 11 Latin American countries, urban green space is heterogeneous and inequitably distributed. Random forest analysis shows climate zone is the dominant driver of green-space quantity (NDVI, % green area, per-capita area), while education, street-intersection density and population density most strongly drive urban park availability. Arid cities have the least vegetation but the most parks per capita, implying unequal capacity to use nature-based solutions for heat mitigation and health.

articles.title: Functional brain imaging and population-level visits to urban spaces
articles.core_conclusion: Brain activity in a small lab sample can forecast population behavior in a city participants never visited: when US adults viewed 160 geotagged Flickr photos of Lisbon during fMRI, ventromedial prefrontal cortex (reward-valuation) activity predicted the real density of photographs around those locations, a proxy for population-level visits. Reward-related neural responses to the built environment help shape where people go, offering a brain-based signal for designing attractive, sustainable cities.

articles.title: The relationship between social order and crime in Nottingham, England
articles.core_conclusion: A governance-type organized crime group in Nottingham's Bestwood ward appears to suppress ordinary crime: across all eight police-labeled crime types, Bestwood's per-population-density crime counts are significantly lower than in its most-similar ward, Bulwell (Welch's t-tests, P<0.05-0.01). This criminal governance emerges in a white, British-born, non-immigrant, less affluent community within a high-policing-capacity state, challenging the assumption that such governance is confined to the global south, mafia territories, or ethnic enclaves.

articles.title: Neighborhood effects on integrative organizational practices in five global cities
articles.core_conclusion: Integrative practices of civil society organizations are unevenly distributed across the city and patterned by neighborhood characteristics: social integration (connecting people to each other) is more prevalent in poorer neighborhoods, whereas systemic integration (connecting people and organizations to the wider institutional ecosystem) is more common in heterogeneous neighborhoods, especially affluent ones.

articles.title: Mexico City's pandemic urban crisis management lacked a multiscalar approach
articles.core_conclusion: Mexico City's extreme COVID-19 fatality reflects not only population health but failures in the decision-making process. Mapping the pandemic response across four governance levels (global, federal, city, borough) with their geographical scope, sociopolitical impact and actor network shows that Mexico's centralized urban governance lacked sensibility to proximate scales, demonstrating why emergency crises in Global South cities require genuinely multiscalar governance.

articles.title: Unearthing the role of soils in urban climate resilience planning
data_sources: Hobbie & Grimm 2020 (Phil. Trans. R. Soc. B) — urban soil ecosystem services review
O'Riordan et al. 2021 (Geoderma) — urban soil functions review
Mielke et al. 2011 (Environ. Int.) — urban soil lead contamination
Andersson 2022 (Curr. Opin. Environ. Sustain.) — soils in sustainability governance
Campbell et al. 2022 (Urban Geogr.) — community urban ecology equity
Schwarz et al. 2022 (Front. Ecol. Evol.) — social-ecological framing of urban soils
articles.core_conclusion: Urban soils are systematically overlooked in climate resilience planning, managed primarily as hazards rather than assets. Reframing soils as essential infrastructure within an equity-centred social-ecological framework — and backing that reframing with sustained investment and inclusive decision-making — is essential to realize their potential for stormwater regulation, heat mitigation, carbon storage, and biodiversity support, especially in historically disinvested communities.

articles.title: Machine-guided design of cell-type-targeting cis-regulatory elements
methods: zebrafish and mouse transgenic reporter assays
data_sources: ENCODE DHS peaks and H3K27ac ChIP-seq
articles.core_conclusion: A machine-learning platform (CODA) powered by a deep CNN (Malinois) trained on 798,064 MPRA-measured sequences can design synthetic cis-regulatory elements that surpass all naturally occurring genomic sequences in driving cell-type-specific gene expression across three human cell lines (94.1% specificity vs. 40.6% for DHS-natural sequences). Synthetic CREs achieve specificity through unique combinations of on-target activating and off-target repressing TF motifs rarely co-deployed in the human genome, and retain tissue-level specificity in zebrafish and mouse in vivo.

articles.title: Scalable watermarking for identifying large language model outputs
data_sources: SynthID-Text GitHub (code and human evaluation data)
articles.core_conclusion: SynthID-Text is a production-ready generative text watermarking scheme that embeds a detectable statistical signature into LLM outputs by modifying only the token sampling procedure (Tournament sampling), without affecting model training or adding meaningful latency. Validated over nearly 20 million live Gemini responses, it preserves text quality while delivering superior watermark detectability compared with existing methods, and has been deployed at scale in Gemini and Gemini Advanced — the first real-world deployment of a generative text watermark in a production LLM system.

articles.title: Urban transitions toward sufficiency-oriented circular post-consumer textile economies
data_sources: EEA EU exports of used textiles
articles.core_conclusion: Across nine wealthy OECD cities, post-consumer textile flows are managed in strikingly similar ways, relying heavily on exporting used textiles abroad. This export pathway renders the growing problem of textile waste-driven by fashion overproduction and overconsumption-invisible to the public and municipalities. The paper argues cities must shift from end-of-pipe waste handling to upstream sufficiency and offers actionable policy recommendations for circular, sufficient urban textile systems.

articles.title: Infrastructure, regulation and the experiences of delivery cyclists in Australian cities
data_sources: City of Sydney pop-up bike-lane footage (2020)
articles.core_conclusion: Centering the previously missing voice of delivery riders in Sydney and Melbourne, this first comprehensive Australian study finds riders' dominant safety concern is the road environment, not the gig economy: fast/distracted drivers, doorings and disconnected bike-lane networks. Riders want connected separated infrastructure plus better road rules and enforcement, and are uniquely exposed to inclement weather they cannot avoid. They already repurpose underused public space to create safer routes, showing how cities could become more permeable.

articles.title: Robust chemical analysis with graphene chemosensors and machine learning
data_sources: In-house graphene ISFET electrical measurements (Penn State University)
articles.core_conclusion: Non-functionalized graphene-based ISFET arrays paired with ANNs achieve robust classification and quantification of complex chemical solutions, detecting food fraud, food spoilage, and PFAS contamination at sub-ppb levels. Machine learning mitigates cycle-to-cycle, sensor-to-sensor, and chip-to-chip variability while SHAP analysis provides interpretable insight into which electrical features drive each discrimination task, enabling commercial-scale chemosensing without functionalization or recalibration.

articles.title: Rooftop photovoltaic solar panels warm up and cool down cities
data_sources: NCEP GFS 0.25 deg global forecast grids
articles.core_conclusion: City-wide rooftop photovoltaics are a double-edged climate intervention. Coupled WRF/BEP+BEM simulations for tropical Kolkata show full rooftop coverage can raise daytime air temperature by up to 1.5 degC (surface skin up to 3.2 degC) during heatwaves while cooling nighttime by up to 0.6 degC, and reshape the urban energy budget, boundary layer (deepened ~615 m) and sea-breeze circulation. Sensitivity runs for Sydney, Austin, Athens and Brussels reproduce the same daytime-warming / nighttime-cooling signature, so the effect generalizes across climates.

articles.title: Network statistics of the whole-brain connectome of Drosophila
methods: neuropil connection (NPC) degree-corrected stochastic block model
data_sources: C. elegans hermaphrodite and male connectomes
articles.core_conclusion: The first complete adult-brain connectome of Drosophila melanogaster (127,978 neurons, 2,613,129 connections) reveals a highly interconnected small-world network (S=141) with a large rich-club organization (~30% of neurons). Recurrent and reciprocal motifs are systematically over-represented; excitatory-inhibitory (ach-GABA) pairs dominate reciprocal connections. Rich-club neurons sit close to sensory inputs and bridge hemispheric bottlenecks, suggesting they underpin efficient global integration.

articles.title: Modeled temperature, mortality impact and external benefits of cool roofs and rooftop photovoltaics in London
data_sources: Met Office + NetAtmo personal weather stations
ONS daily deaths and life tables
articles.core_conclusion: In a 3D urban climate model of London's record-hot summer 2018, cool roofs cool more than rooftop PV (population-weighted air temperature -0.8 C vs -0.3 C) and could have avoided 249 (32%) of ~786 heat-attributable deaths versus 96 (12%) for RPV. Monetized via value of statistical life, the avoided-mortality benefit is GBP 615 M (cool roofs) and GBP 237 M (RPV), comparable to the value of electricity from full RPV (9.5-20 TWh) - so health externalities are a real part of the economic case for both.

articles.title: Larger and more instructable language models become less reliable
data_sources: Zenodo repository (model responses and human study data)
articles.core_conclusion: Scaling up and instruction-tuning (shaping up) LLMs improves accuracy and prompt stability but paradoxically reduces reliability: shaped-up models eliminate avoidant responses and replace them with plausible-sounding incorrect answers (ultracrepidarianism), fail to consolidate safe operating zones at low difficulty, and generate errors that human supervisors frequently cannot detect. A fundamental shift toward predictable error distributions — not maximal accuracy — is required for high-stakes AI deployment.

articles.title: Democratic discrepancies in urban sustainable development
articles.core_conclusion: Residents across 8 European cities broadly support urban sustainable development (72.61% average acceptance) but a systematic democratic gap exists: USD policy plans prioritize biodiversity, education, and public transport, while residents most strongly prefer cost of living, public health, and poverty reduction. Securing basic human needs is democratically demanded and is the prerequisite for deeper socio-ecological sustainability transformations.

articles.title: A universal framework for inclusive 15-minute cities
articles.core_conclusion: The 15-minute city is not universally applicable: service accessibility is highly heterogeneous within and across cities, driven primarily by local population density. Redistributing existing resources alone can substantially reduce inequity, but the paradigm must generalize across density gradients and move from time-based to value-based cities.

articles.title: Urban rooftops for food and energy in China
data_sources: Building outlines and attributes (authors, 13 M buildings)
articles.core_conclusion: Across 13 million buildings in 124 Chinese cities, rooftop agriculture (RA) delivers superior economic benefits while rooftop photovoltaics (RPV) excel in GHG emission reductions, but single-winner allocation loses 71-100% of the other technology's benefits. An optimal 61% flat-rooftop allocation to RA (remaining to RPV) retains more than 50% of each technology's potential, meeting ~15% of urban vegetable demand and ~5% of electricity demand on average, though with significant resource costs including up to 15% of residential water use and 13 kt of silver in total.

articles.title: Operationalizing climate justice in the implementation of Boston's Building Performance Standard
data_sources: participant observation in City of Boston Environment Department
articles.core_conclusion: Translating climate justice from a planning ideal into a working building-emissions regulation is politically contested rather than technical. An ethnography of Boston's BERDO rule-making shows justice is reshaped by scope-and-scale discrepancies, bureaucratization, and weaponization, where actors invoke justice to resist or redirect policy, revealing why operationalizing climate justice on the ground is far harder than committing to it.

articles.title: Connectome-constrained networks predict neural activity across the fly visual system
methods: task optimization via backpropagation through time
UMAP dimensionality reduction and Gaussian mixture clustering
lottery-ticket pruning for synthetic sparsity benchmark
data_sources: FlyEM FIB-25 FIB-SEM dataset (Janelia Research Campus)
FlyEM FIB-19 FIB-SEM dataset (Janelia Research Campus)
articles.core_conclusion: Connectivity measurements alone — without any direct neural activity recordings — can accurately predict the neural activity underlying visual motion computation in the Drosophila optic lobe. A connectome-constrained, task-optimized deep mechanistic network of 64 cell types trained on optic flow correctly predicts ON/OFF contrast selectivity and direction selectivity validated across 26 independent experimental studies. Sparse connectivity — a universal feature of biological neural networks — is the key enabling property, as it constrains the solution space enough for connectivity to determine function.

articles.title: Linear symmetric self-selecting 14-bit kinetic molecular memristors
data_sources: Supplementary Information PDF (spectroscopic and electrical measurements)
articles.core_conclusion: A Ru-complex azo-aromatic molecular memristor achieves 14-bit (16,520-level) analog resolution with linear and symmetric conductance updates in a single time step. Integrated into a self-selecting 64x64 crossbar, it performs vector-matrix multiplication (including Fourier transforms) in one step with more than 73 dB SNR — four orders of magnitude better than state-of-the-art — while consuming 460x less energy than digital computers, opening neuromorphic computing to core AI workloads.

articles.title: Urban biodiversity is affected by human-designed features of public squares
articles.core_conclusion: Across 103 public squares in Munich, human-designed square features strongly determine which species occur. Taxon richness and abundance rise with overall greenness, but different taxa respond to different features (lawn proportion, shrub volume, tree density most important), while ALAN and human/pet presence suppress nocturnal and disturbance-sensitive taxa. No single design is optimal for all biodiversity, so biodiverse-city planning must be multifaceted, balancing human and non-human needs.

articles.title: A pathology foundation model for cancer diagnosis and prognosis prediction
methods: multiple instance learning (MIL) with attention aggregation
HoverNet cell segmentation for microenvironment analysis
data_sources: Institutional slides: DFCI, BWH, YH, SMCH, CUCH, UPenn
articles.core_conclusion: CHIEF, a general-purpose weakly supervised pathology foundation model pre-trained on 60,530 WSIs (44 TB) spanning 19 anatomical sites, outperforms specialized state-of-the-art deep learning models by up to 36.1% across cancer detection, tumour origin identification, molecular profiling, and survival prediction, with robust validation in 19,491 WSIs from 32 independent international cohorts across 24 hospitals.

articles.title: Natural fragmentation increases urban density but impedes transportation and city growth worldwide
data_sources: building-height, air-pollution and GDP/income global datasets
articles.core_conclusion: Three new geographic-barrier indexes (share of natural barriers, nonconvexity, average road detour) computed around cities worldwide show that natural fragmentation produces more complex, longer transportation routes and is associated with higher urban density, smaller footprints, taller buildings and less pollution, but also lower incomes and smaller populations. Comparative urban research must control for surrounding physical geography to avoid misattributing terrain effects to policy.

articles.title: The interplay of cloud cover and 3D urban structures reduces human access to sunlight
methods: three-filter sunlight model (day length x cloud x sky-view-factor)
sky view factor from lidar DSM
data_sources: US DOE Building America climate zones
articles.core_conclusion: Urban sunlight is reduced by two stacked filters, cloud cover and 3D building geometry. Across 1,353 US cities for 2020, the average annual loss is 2,896 h (about 121 days): 2,448 h (about 102 days) from cloud cover and 448 h (about 19 days) from 3D urban structures. With cloud cover and vertical urbanization both rising, the deficit is projected to worsen.

articles.title: Closed-loop transfer enables artificial intelligence to yield chemical knowledge
articles.core_conclusion: Closed-loop transfer (CLT) integrates Bayesian optimization with physics-based feature selection and interpretable supervised learning to simultaneously optimize photostability and extract mechanistic chemical knowledge. Characterizing only ~1.5% of a 2,200-molecule space, CLT identified that high-energy triplet state density (TDOS at ~4 eV) governs photodegradation in donor-acceptor organic molecules, a hypothesis validated experimentally via solvent triplet-energy tuning.

articles.title: Health co-benefits of post-COVID-19 low-carbon recovery in Chinese cities
data_sources: Authors' near-real-time daily city CO2 dataset (Carbon Monitor approach)
China National Environmental Monitoring Center (CNEMC) hourly PM2.5
National Bureau of Statistics power-capacity data
articles.core_conclusion: Among 48 Chinese cities, the six that achieved low-carbon recovery after COVID-19 (GDP growth with falling CO2) saved 1.2x as many lives per 100,000 and 1.5x the monetized avoided premature deaths per 100,000 as the other 42 cities, totaling US$4.2 billion over the post-COVID period. Government spending on electric vehicles increased the likelihood of a city achieving low-carbon recovery, evidencing synergy between local welfare and global climate goals.

articles.title: Charting sustainable urban development through a systematic review of SDG11 research
articles.core_conclusion: A bibliometric analysis of 21,153 publications (2016-2022, 229,182 citations) shows SDG11 research has grown into a multidisciplinary field dominated by affordable housing, urban transport, governance and public space, while urban safety, inclusion, cultural/natural heritage (SDG11.4), urban-rural linkages (SDG11.a) and support for least-developed countries (SDG11.c) remain under-studied. China, the USA and the UK produce 47.71% of output, exposing an Anglo-American hegemony and a Global South research gap where 90% of future urban growth will occur.

articles.title: Connectivity in urbanscapes can cause unintended flood impacts from stormwater systems
articles.core_conclusion: Most urban flood-control solutions are local in design and ignore how built infrastructure connects floods across space and time. Simulating the 2014 Southeast Michigan flood shows that stormwater infrastructure neglecting flood spatial connectivity can be ineffective and even produce inundation in areas with no local rainfall; network configurations and natural-channel interfaces strongly change surcharge, manhole overflow and inundation. Urban flood design and warning must treat the watershed as an interconnected system.

articles.title: Progress and gaps in climate change adaptation in coastal cities across the globe
data_sources: World Bank income groups and GNI per capita
articles.core_conclusion: A systematic global stocktake of peer-reviewed evidence on 199 coastal cities finds adaptation is slow, narrow in scope and non-transformative, designed mostly around past and current rather than future hazards, exposure and vulnerability. A strong income gradient holds: high-income city governments lead institutional and infrastructural responses while lower-middle-income cities rely on households for behavioral adaptation. The Global South, ecosystem-based adaptation, and future-oriented risk assessment are systematically under-documented.

articles.title: Loss of plasticity in deep continual learning
data_sources: OpenAI Gym MuJoCo environments (Ant-v3, Hopper-v3, Walker-v3)
articles.core_conclusion: Standard deep-learning methods gradually lose plasticity in continual-learning settings, eventually learning no better than a shallow network. The root causes are dead units, weight-magnitude explosion, and representational rank collapse — none reversible by gradient descent alone. Continual backpropagation, which selectively reinitializes the least-used units at each step, maintains plasticity indefinitely in both supervised and reinforcement-learning settings.

articles.title: Slum living predicts psychosis severity in first-episode patients
data_sources: Sao Paulo municipal demography open data
articles.core_conclusion: In 190 antipsychotic-naive first-episode psychosis patients in Sao Paulo, slum residence independently predicts greater initial psychosis severity (higher total PANSS, B=9.882, p=0.029; worse negative p=0.044 and disorganization p=0.032 symptoms). Lower income/education link to higher PANSS, and longer duration of untreated psychosis links to brown or Black race, indicating place-based social exclusion shapes the clinical presentation of psychotic disorders.

articles.title: Causal evidence of a line attractor encoding an affective state
articles.core_conclusion: Using two-photon calcium imaging and holographic optogenetic perturbations in head-fixed mice observing aggression, this study provides the first causal evidence that a continuous line attractor is intrinsically implemented in the mammalian hypothalamus (VMHvl). On-manifold stimulation of attractor-contributing neurons produces progressive integration and persistent activity; off-manifold perturbations are rapidly corrected back to the attractor. Individual differences in line-attractor stability correlate strongly with functional connectivity strength among x1 neurons (R2=0.87), and mechanistic modelling implicates slow neuromodulatory neurotransmission (tau_s ~20 s, consistent with GPCR/neuropeptide signalling) rather than fast glutamate in sustaining the affective internal state.

articles.title: Repeated and localized flooding is an underestimated challenge for urban disaster risk management
data_sources: FEMA Hazus inventory and damage functions
parcel inventory for East Palo Alto
articles.core_conclusion: US federal recovery aid is gated by Presidential major disaster declarations (PDs), which only follow large events, so frequent localized floods that never trigger a PD accumulate the worst long-term harm. A parcel-level probabilistic model of East Palo Alto shows a 20-year flood without a PD can cause greater long-term losses than a 100-year flood with a PD, and a PD can cut lower-income households' long-term consumption losses by more than 50%. Cities must plan for the small, repeated floods federal aid will not support.

articles.title: A typology of activities over a century of urban growth
articles.core_conclusion: Using a new geohistorical database of about 1 million Paris directory entries (1829-1907), activities sort into three dynamical classes by how they scale with population: linear for everyday needs (food, clothing, healthcare), sublinear for public services (legal, administrative, educational), and superlinear for city-specific or transient features (fads, specialization, timely needs). These dynamics are highly sensitive to historical shocks such as large-scale public works (Haussmann) and political conflicts.

articles.title: Fully forward mode training for optical neural networks
articles.core_conclusion: Fully Forward Mode (FFM) learning trains optical neural networks entirely on the physical system by exploiting Lorentz reciprocity and spatial symmetry to replace backward light propagation with a second forward pass. This eliminates the need for digital emulation and achieves the deepest experimentally demonstrated ONNs (8-layer free-space, 5-layer PIC), diffraction-limited scattering imaging, sub-photon NLOS classification at 5.40e18 OPS/W, and model-free exceptional-point discovery.

articles.title: Widespread global exacerbation of extreme drought induced by urbanization
articles.core_conclusion: Global weather-station observations show urbanization intensifies local drought: drought severity increased at ~36% of global city sites and extreme SPEI at ~43%. Paired WRF simulations attribute this to warmer, drier urban environments that suppress light rainfall. CMIP6 mid-century projections indicate ~57% and ~70% of urban regions would face worsened drought severity and extreme SPEI from continued urban expansion.

articles.title: Global urban structural growth shows a profound shift from spreading out to building up
data_sources: IEA / China / Beijing / Shanghai floor-area statistics
articles.core_conclusion: Across 1,550+ cities observed for three decades with independent satellite radar backscatter (building-volume proxy) and Landsat built fraction, the world's cities shifted profoundly from lateral expansion to vertical development: built-fraction growth rates decelerated while backscatter growth rates accelerated almost everywhere. Globally rapid outward growth area fell ~56% while upward growth rose ~400% from the 1990s to the 2010s; the transition is universal but time-lagged, earliest and strongest in Asian (especially Chinese) cities.

articles.title: Seagrass ecosystems as green urban infrastructure to mediate human pathogens in seafood
authors: Jeroen A. J. M. van de Water
data_sources: IUCN Red List of Threatened Species (v.2019-3)
UN FAO Fisheries and Aquaculture Division (capture production)
articles.core_conclusion: Coastal seagrass meadows function as natural sanitation infrastructure: marine mussels at urban locations with seagrass present had a 65% lower relative abundance of human bacterial pathogens than locations without seagrass. Over one-third of global urban agglomerations — currently home to 1.1 billion people, rising to 1.3 billion by 2030 — lie within 50 km of seagrass, highlighting the global opportunity to integrate seagrass conservation into urban food-safety and public-health strategies.

articles.title: Big mobility data reveals hyperlocal air pollution exposure disparities in the Bronx, New York
articles.core_conclusion: Coupling daily mobility of over 500,000 anonymized users with hyperlocal PM2.5 at 100 x 100-m resolution moves exposure assessment from individual tracking to a population-representative scale in the Bronx. Personal exposure and exposure disparity show distinct spatial patterns; Hispanic-majority and low-income neighborhoods are most disproportionately exposed. Race/ethnicity predicts disparity more strongly than income, and within-group variation contributes a major share of total disparity, implying generic air-quality improvement must be paired with granular, individual-targeted mitigation.

articles.title: Neural general circulation models for weather and climate
methods: differentiable dynamical core (pseudo-spectral hydrostatic primitive equations in JAX)
learned neural-network physics parameterization (single-column fully connected network)
end-to-end online training with multi-step rollout gradient backpropagation
stochastic ensemble via Gaussian random field noise injection
WeatherBench2 evaluation (RMSE, RMSB, CRPS, spread-skill ratio)
articles.core_conclusion: NeuralGCM combines a differentiable atmospheric dynamical core with end-to-end learned neural-network parameterizations, achieving forecast accuracy on par with the best physics-based and pure machine-learning models across 1- to 15-day weather and multi-decade climate timescales. It produces calibrated ensemble forecasts with better CRPS than ECMWF-ENS, realistically simulates tropical cyclones and 40-year global temperature trends, and delivers 3–5 orders of magnitude computational savings over conventional GCMs.

articles.title: Sustainable urban transformations based on integrated microgrid designs
methods: multi-objective Pareto optimization via evolutionary algorithm
criticality scoring with stakeholder focus groups
data_sources: DOE project grid infrastructure data (New Hanover County, 2021)
Duke Energy power outage time-series (Hurricane Florence, 2018)
NREL/OpenEI Data Lake energy consumption profiles
articles.core_conclusion: Urban microgrid boundary delineation driven solely by economic criteria risks creating 'energy gerrymandering' — systematic exclusion of socially vulnerable groups from equitable resilience benefits. Integrating the Social Vulnerability Index, critical-infrastructure criticality scores, and renewable energy siting potential into a multi-objective evolutionary optimizer yields a six-microgrid districting solution for New Hanover County, NC, that simultaneously maximizes urban resilience, well-being, and equity at comparable cost — advancing SDG 7, 10, and 11 targets in tandem.

articles.title: Inequality in high-cost borrowing and unemployment insurance generosity in US states during the COVID-19 pandemic
methods: two-way fixed effects linear probability models
robustness checks (alternative UI measures, lagged DV, alternative outcomes)
data_sources: Clarity Services AFS loan data (Experian subsidiary)
US Department of Labor 'Significant Provisions of State UI Laws'
UK Center for Poverty Research National Welfare Database
Eviction Moratoria and Housing Policy Dataset (Bonfer & Koehler)
National Consumer Law Center small dollar loan reports
Center for Responsible Lending payday rate reports
Century Foundation UI replacement ratio data
Bureau of Labor Statistics state unemployment rates
articles.core_conclusion: More generous state UI benefits were inversely associated with high-cost credit use (credit cards, personal finance loans, AFS loans) during the first seven quarters of the COVID-19 pandemic, with the substitutive protective effect concentrated among low-income households. Higher-income consumers showed no association or a positive (consumptive) association. Stingy state UI safety nets left vulnerable consumers more exposed to predatory AFS debt, deepening economic inequality during the crisis.

articles.title: Mitigating urban heat island through neighboring rural land cover
articles.core_conclusion: Urban heat extends beyond the city edge, so neighboring rural land cover can cool the whole city. Across 30 Chinese cities (2000-2020), rural land within ~10-15 km of the urban boundary (about half the city's equivalent diameter) had the strongest effect, explaining up to ~30% of surface UHI intensity variance in the urban core. The number and adjacency of landscape patches are the two key levers, each able to reduce UHI by up to 0.5 C; recommended designs avoid fragmentation, raise shape complexity, and make patch distribution more uniform.

articles.title: Outsourced carbon mitigation efforts of Chinese cities from 2012 to 2017
articles.core_conclusion: Most Chinese cities are outsourced mitigation beneficiaries that cut their carbon footprints chiefly via upstream supply-chain cities' efforts rather than their own. Using a 309-city MRIO model, the outsourced share of emissions rose from 78.6% (2012) to 81.9% (2017), and 240 cities (77.7%) were beneficiaries (65 strong, 175 weak). This free-riding masks true mitigation contributions and calls for supply-chain-aware, differentiated city policies and carbon-compensation mechanisms.

articles.title: Worldwide delineation of multi-tier city–regions
methods: minimum cumulative weighted distance catchment delineation
articles.core_conclusion: The first systematic worldwide delineation of city–regions classifies 30,079 urban centers across 213 countries/territories into four tiers and maps their travel-time catchments, identifying 1,403 primary city–regions at a 3 h cutoff and 4,210 at 1 h. 3.2 billion people can reach multiple urban tiers within 1 h (4.7 billion within 3 h); among people closest to towns or small cities, twice as many have easier access to intermediate than to large cities, highlighting intermediate cities' connective role and challenging primate-city-centric policy.

articles.title: Detecting hallucinations in large language models using semantic entropy
articles.core_conclusion: Confabulations (arbitrary, fluent falsehoods) in LLMs can be reliably detected without domain knowledge by computing semantic entropy — entropy over meaning-clusters formed via bidirectional entailment rather than over raw token sequences. Semantic entropy achieves an average AUROC of 0.790 across 30 model-dataset combinations, outperforming supervised baselines even out-of-distribution, and generalizes to paragraph-length generation via a factoid-decomposition pipeline.

articles.title: Contribution of concrete nitrogenation to global NOx uptake
articles.core_conclusion: Promoting nitrogenation of the world's concrete stock could absorb roughly 3.4-6.9 Mt of NOx in 2021 (6-13% of global industry-related NOx), and 131-384 Mt cumulatively by 2050, yielding over 150-160 trillion USD in value and avoiding ~75-260 DALY-years. Because carbonation and nitrogenation compete for the same finite alkaline reactivity, nitrogenation is, in most regions, the higher-priority and more feasible option.

articles.title: A multimodal generative AI copilot for human pathology
methods: consensus adjudication of binary correctness labels
data_sources: UNI self-supervised pathology encoder (>100M patches, >100K WSIs)
The Cancer Genome Atlas (TCGA) WSIs via NIH Genomic Data Commons
Brigham and Women's Hospital institutional WSI archive (IRB-approved, private)
articles.core_conclusion: PathChat, a vision-language generalist AI assistant for human pathology built by fine-tuning a pathology-specific UNI encoder with a 13B-parameter Llama 2 LLM on 456,916 curated instructions, achieves state-of-the-art performance on multiple-choice diagnostic questions (78.1% image-only, 89.5% with clinical context) and substantially outperforms GPT-4V and open-source MLLMs on expert-judged open-ended pathology queries, demonstrating that domain-tailored, human-in-the-loop AI copilots can meaningfully assist pathology education, research, and clinical decision-making.

articles.title: Experiment-free exoskeleton assistance via learning in simulation
data_sources: Human participant trial data (metabolic and kinematic)
articles.core_conclusion: A learning-in-simulation framework combining dynamics-aware musculoskeletal and exoskeleton models with deep reinforcement learning produces a versatile hip exoskeleton control policy without any human experiments. When deployed on a physical device, it reduces metabolic cost during walking by 24.3%, running by 13.1%, and stair climbing by 15.4%, with a single learned controller using only one wearable sensor per leg.

articles.title: A virtual rodent predicts the structure of neural activity across behaviours
articles.core_conclusion: A biomechanically realistic virtual rodent trained via deep reinforcement learning to imitate freely moving rats produces network activity that predicts real neural recordings in the sensorimotor striatum (DLS) and motor cortex (MC) better than any kinematic feature of the real rat — consistent with both regions implementing inverse dynamics. The network's latent variability mirrors the structure of real neural variability across behaviours in a way consistent with the minimal intervention principle of optimal feedback control.

articles.title: African food system and biodiversity mainly affected by urbanization via dietary shifts
data_sources: Chen et al. 2020 urban expansion projections (SSP-aligned, 1 km)
Copernicus Global Land Service CGLS-LC100 (Google Earth Engine)
World Bank LSMS household surveys (ECOWAS, EAC and select countries)
articles.core_conclusion: Urban area expansion in Africa is not the primary threat to food production — direct cropland losses stay below 1% through 2050 because land-use displacement compensates most losses, shifting pressure onto natural lands instead (up to 2 Mt additional natural-land loss). The dominant impact of urbanization is dietary change: urban rice consumption 1.5x that of rural households raises continental rice demand +9.2% (SSP1), production +8.0%, imports up to +2 Mt, and agricultural methane emissions up to +12 MtCO2-equivalent per year — underscoring the need for a systems approach that treats urbanization as a socio-metabolic driver rather than simple land-cover conversion.

articles.title: Globally elevated greenhouse gas emissions from polluted urban rivers
data_sources: Urban river GHG compiled dataset (5090 measurements, figshare)
articles.core_conclusion: Urban rivers globally emit 78.1 +/- 3.5 Tg CO2-equivalent per year (1.1 Tg CH4, 42.3 Tg CO2, 0.021 Tg N2O), at nearly twice the emission rate of non-urban rivers and comparable in intensity to direct urban anthropogenic activities. CH4 and N2O are disproportionately elevated due to eutrophication and anoxia from urban pollution. Emissions peak in lower-middle-income countries, following an Environmental Kuznets Curve, and controlling river pollution offers a co-benefit pathway for both water quality restoration and urban climate mitigation.

articles.title: A randomized controlled trial evaluating the effects of nurse-led triage of 911 calls
methods: Medicaid claims linkage via SOUNDEX fuzzy matching
articles.core_conclusion: Embedding registered nurses directly in the DC 911 dispatch loop safely reduced ambulance dispatches from 97% to 56% and transports from 73% to 45% among 6,053 low-acuity callers. Within 24 hours, Medicaid beneficiaries' PCP visit rate more than tripled (2.5% to 8.2%). No measurable long-term (6-month) effects on ED visits or Medicaid expenditure were detected, likely due to call-level randomization limitations. Safety was preserved: all 13 callers later found to have time-sensitive illness were rapidly redirected to ambulance.

articles.title: A whole-slide foundation model for digital pathology from real-world data
articles.core_conclusion: Prov-GigaPath, a whole-slide pathology foundation model pretrained on 1.3 billion real-world clinical image tiles from 171,189 slides across 28 cancer centres, achieves state-of-the-art performance on 25 out of 26 digital pathology tasks by combining large-scale real-world data with ultra-large-context LongNet modelling that captures the entire gigapixel slide rather than subsampling tiles.

articles.title: Mapping model units to visual neurons reveals population code for social behaviour
methods: knockout training (causal DNN perturbation matching genetic silencing)
convolutional 1-to-1 network (vision + LC bottleneck + decision subnetworks)
image-computable visual input reconstruction from joint-position tracking
cumulative inactivation procedure (CLIP) for necessity/sufficiency analysis
variance decomposition of model LC responses to visual parameters
agglomerative clustering of FlyWire synaptic connectivity matrices
permutation tests with Benjamini-Hochberg FDR correction
data_sources: Two-photon calcium imaging of LC neurons (GCaMP6f)
articles.core_conclusion: By introducing knockout training — perturbing a DNN during training to match real genetic silencing of neuron types — the study establishes a one-to-one mapping between 23 model units and 23 lobula columnar (LC) neuron types in the Drosophila visual system. Contrary to the established channel-coding hypothesis, combinations of LC neuron types (including those associated with non-social behaviours) form a rich population code that collectively drives male courtship pursuit and song, providing a unified stimulus-to-neuron-to-behaviour map.

articles.title: Unprivileged groups are less served by green cooling services in major European urban areas
methods: Pearson correlation of cooling vs socioeconomic indicators
articles.core_conclusion: Across 14 major European urban areas, the cooling delivered by urban green space is distributed unjustly: vulnerable residents cluster in run-down central districts with the lowest cooling, not the suburbs. Lower-income residents, tenants, immigrants and the unemployed receive below-average green cooling while upper-income residents, nationals and homeowners receive above-average cooling, amplifying heat-related fatality risk for those least able to afford other cooling.

articles.title: Long-term effects of redlining on climate risk exposure
data_sources: HOLC redlining maps (Mapping Inequality, Univ. of Richmond)
tree canopy / surface permeability / foundation height layers
historical census from Aaronson et al. 2021 (restricted)
articles.core_conclusion: Neighborhoods graded as less desirable by the Home Owners' Loan Corporation (redlining) in the 1930s-1940s face disproportionately higher current and projected exposure to flooding and extreme heat. A boundary-discontinuity design across 202 US cities attributes this partly to diminished environmental capital: less tree canopy, lower surface permeability, and lower foundation height. Historical policy still shapes who is most vulnerable to climate hazards today.

articles.title: Creation of memory–memory entanglement in a metropolitan quantum network
articles.core_conclusion: Memory-memory entanglement is demonstrated for the first time across a real metropolitan area using three atomic-ensemble quantum memory nodes separated by up to 12.5 km of deployed fiber, with a photonic server performing heralded entanglement generation via single-photon detection. Active phase stabilization, telecom wavelength conversion, and memory lifetimes exceeding the round-trip communication time together enable concurrent pairwise entanglement, establishing a city-scale testbed for multi-node quantum network protocols.

articles.title: Impact of socioeconomic factors on soil-borne animal pathogenic fungi in urban greenspaces
methods: predictor attribution controlling for climate and soil
articles.core_conclusion: Across a global survey of 56 cities and a high-resolution survey within Shanghai, socioeconomic factors (population density, GDP per capita, hospital-bed density) are crucial predictors of the composition and diversity of soil-borne animal and human pathogenic fungi in urban greenspaces, even after controlling for climate and soil. Trichosporon is a universal indicator of population density at both scales, implying poorer and more populated cities harbor larger proportions of soil-borne pathogens.

articles.title: Assessing global urban CO2 removal
articles.core_conclusion: Cities can act as carbon sinks rather than only sources. Synthesizing the literature, the authors quantify the global potential of four urban CDR options (carbon storage in vegetation, soils and buildings, plus indoor direct air capture) and find a substantial possible contribution to climate mitigation with human and environmental co-benefits, but deployment is constrained by uncertainties, economic barriers and governance/accounting gaps.

articles.title: Well-being from nature exposure depends on socio-environmental contexts in Paraguay
articles.core_conclusion: The well-being benefit of urban nature is not universal: in a survey of 1,119 residents of metropolitan Asuncion, Paraguay, stronger nature connection was positively associated with life satisfaction, but residential greenness behaved oppositely by context - positive in formal neighbourhoods (as in the Global North) yet negative in informal settlements. Intentional nature-seeking, not passive proximity, benefits the urban poor.

articles.title: Transformative resilience of Wuhan's city-center main streets in the post-COVID era
articles.core_conclusion: City-center main streets show transformative resilience rather than simple recovery: the retail landscape undergoes a 'regime shift' from the traditional pedestrian/urban experience toward a reinvented experience geared to digital-world socialization, while a strong 'adaptive capacity' is cocreated by multiscalar pedestrian mobility, shop mix, and complex ownership/control arrangements, ushering in a digitized urban future with a new sense of place.

articles.title: Machine learning reveals the control mechanics of an insect wing hinge
data_sources: GCaMP7f calcium imaging (in vivo, D. melanogaster)
Dynamically scaled flapping robot (mineral oil bath)
articles.core_conclusion: A CNN trained on 72,219 wingbeats from 82 Drosophila accurately predicts 3D wing kinematics from the activity of 12 steering muscles and wingbeat frequency. An encoder-decoder reveals the distinct mechanical role of each axillary sclerite. Embedding the hinge model in a model predictive control loop reproduces canonical free-flight manoeuvres, demonstrating that insect flight control emerges from sparse, coordinated muscle activity through a mechanically sophisticated wing hinge.

articles.title: Strategic interactions for carbon emissions in Chinese cities are influenced by mayors
articles.core_conclusion: Chinese cities reduce carbon intensity through strategic peer interaction rather than in isolation: a spatial Durbin model on 2000-2019 city panel data shows a 1% drop in neighboring cities' carbon intensity is associated with a 0.792% drop in the reference city. The effect is stronger for mayors who are more educated, younger, science-trained, and working in their hometowns, and follows an 'imitation competition' pattern.

articles.title: Impacts of remote work on vehicle miles traveled and transit ridership in the USA
data_sources: FHWA Traffic Volume Trends / Highway Statistics
articles.core_conclusion: Remote work cuts driving and transit ridership asymmetrically in the USA (2020-2022): a 1% drop in onsite workers maps to -0.99% state VMT but -2.26% MSA transit ridership. A 10% reduction in onsite workers would cut transport CO2 by 191.8 Mt (10%) annually while costing US$3.7B (26.7%) in transit fare revenue across the contiguous USA - environmentally beneficial but fiscally corrosive for transit.

articles.title: War city profiles drawn from satellite images
articles.core_conclusion: A temporal-knowledge-guided detection scheme (TKDS) with a pixel-based transformer network (PtNet) detects war-induced building destruction from satellite imagery far more accurately than prior change-detection methods (F1 +44.0 across six Syrian cities, +34.2 across four Ukrainian cities), and the detected damage is translated into estimates of affected population and damage to hospitals and schools, enabling near-real-time, low-cost monitoring of urban destruction.

articles.title: Global spatial assessment of potential for new peri-urban forests to combat climate change
methods: peri-urban delineation from impervious time series
articles.core_conclusion: Globally 141-322 Mha around cities are potentially available for tree restoration; mapped at 500 m these peri-urban areas could host 34-241 billion trees depending on land-availability scenario and cropland exclusion, with almost 80% of capacity concentrated in 20 countries. Peri-urban forests cannot replace emission cuts but offer a spatially explicit lever for carbon removal plus cooling, air-quality and heat-island co-benefits.

articles.title: Shipping-related pollution decreased but mortality increased in Chinese port cities
data_sources: Ship Automatic Identification System (AIS) big data
articles.core_conclusion: From 2016 to 2020, sulfur-control policy cut shipping-related PM2.5 across every Chinese port city, yet nationwide shipping-related mortality rose 11.4% to 48,300 deaths in 2020. The decoupling reflects an antagonism between emission reductions and demographic change: coastal cities improved while inland-river port cities worsened as population clustering and aging outran air-quality gains, motivating locality-specific shipping-emission control.

articles.title: Early engagement and co-benefits strengthen cities' climate commitments
data_sources: Our World in Data COVID deaths
articles.core_conclusion: Across 793 cities (CDP 2021), climate action mostly persisted through COVID-19 (39.2% increased vs 14.4% decreased) but finance more often fell (30.5% reduced vs 22.4% increased), and only 43% implemented green recovery. Durable commitments are driven not by COVID-19 severity but by early engagement (climate networks, business collaboration, sustainability opportunities) and co-benefits, especially climate-related health issues.

articles.title: Infrequent activities predict economic outcomes in major American cities
articles.core_conclusion: Urban mobility patterns are highly predictive of current and future economic development, but the predictive signal is concentrated in infrequent, irregular and exploratory activities. These rare visits make up less than 2% of total visits yet explain more than 50% of the variation in economic outcomes across Boston, Chicago and Miami, suggesting attention should shift from routine visits to the exploratory long tail.

articles.title: Optimizing retro-reflective surfaces to untrap radiation and cool cities
data_sources: Zenodo simulation data and MATLAB code repository
articles.core_conclusion: Highly reflective surfaces re-trap reflected sunlight on opposing walls and pedestrians in deep urban canyons, whereas retro-reflective (RR) surfaces send radiation back toward the sky and out of the canyon. Detailed energy-budget simulations show RR walls and pavements cut canyon surface temperatures by up to 20 C and air temperatures by up to 2.6 C, outperforming highly reflective surfaces and improving pedestrian comfort. The authors distill climate-, latitude-, season- and geometry-specific design rules: RR pavements for open low-rise areas and tailored RR walls for compact high-rise areas.

articles.title: Commonality and variation in mental representations of music revealed by a cross-cultural comparison of rhythm priors in 15 countries
methods: kernel density estimation in triangular rhythm space
data_sources: Primary field-collected tapping experiment data (39 groups)
Desain and Honing published perceptual categorization data (external validation)
articles.core_conclusion: Across 39 participant groups in 15 countries spanning urbanized and Indigenous societies, every group showed discrete rhythm categories clustered at small-integer-ratio rhythms, suggesting a universal feature of human music cognition. However, the specific integer ratios prominent in each group's prior tracked local musical traditions, demonstrating that universal biological constraints interact with culture-specific musical exposure to produce global cognitive diversity. University students and online participants systematically underrepresent this diversity.

articles.title: Human-centric characterization of life activity flood exposure shifts focus from places to people
methods: spatial overlay of activity and flood hazard
articles.core_conclusion: Flood exposure is better measured by where people actually spend time than by which parcels lie in a hazard zone. The authors define Life Activity Flood Exposure (LAFE) from dwell time in flood-prone areas using anonymized smartphone data across 18 US cities. LAFE is frequently bimodal, so cities with similar spatial flood risk can differ sharply in human exposure, exposing latent exposure and latent immunity that place-based maps miss.

articles.title: Tools and methods for monitoring the health of the urban greenery
articles.core_conclusion: No single monitoring technology resolves the fundamental space-time resolution trade-off in urban greenery health assessment: embedded sensors provide high temporal granularity per tree but at high cost for city-wide coverage, while satellite and airborne imaging covers large areas at lower temporal frequency. Combining multiple sensing modalities — physical sensors, thermal/hyperspectral imaging, LiDAR, and citizen science — can produce hyper-local greenery health data that directly supports six Sustainable Development Goal use cases, from selective watering and early disease detection to urban heat island mitigation.

articles.title: Urban scaling of firearm violence, ownership and accessibility in the United States
articles.core_conclusion: Across 929 US cities (population 10,000–20,000,000), firearm violence scales superlinearly with city size while firearm ownership and accessibility scale sublinearly. An information-theoretic SAMI framework reveals that both violence and accessibility significantly influence ownership, consistent with a self-protection motivation modeled via a Cobb-Douglas function.

articles.title: The role of informal ruralization within China's rapid urbanization
methods: object-based image analysis + GLCM texture features
articles.core_conclusion: Chengshi kaihuang (CK), an informal individual practice of cultivating neglected urban wastelands, is mapped via remote sensing as a bottom-up ruralizing force inside a Chinese megacity. In Wuhan's central districts (2017-2022) an average 6.34% of the area (~12,941 acres) was under active CK each year, far exceeding urban agriculture in comparable Western cities; 97.4% of sites emerged on freshly demolished land and CK tends to return persistently after forced removal, evidencing spatial agency beyond top-down vs. bottom-up binaries.

articles.title: Wildfire risk for global wildland–urban interface areas
data_sources: Meta HRPD (Facebook High-Resolution Population Density maps)
articles.core_conclusion: The first globally consistent, fine-resolution wildland-urban interface dataset covering a 2020 baseline and a 1985-2020 time series reveals a total global WUI area of 6.62 million km2, which grew 12.56% over 35 years. Overlaying burned-area and population data shows 7.07% of WUI areas (4.47 million people) lie within 2,400 m of wildfire threat, with African countries experiencing disproportionately higher risk despite having smaller absolute WUI extents than the USA, Brazil, China, India, and Australia.

articles.title: AI-generated visuals of car-free US cities help improve support for sustainable policies
articles.core_conclusion: Photorealistic AI-generated images of car-free, pedestrian-friendly US streets — produced via DALL-E 2 and Stable Diffusion — significantly increase public support for a hypothetical sustainable transport bill across two large-scale RCT survey studies (N = 3,129). The effect is particularly strong among Republican participants (32.5% willing to sign a petition vs. 17.6% in baseline), suggesting that evoking imagination of positive policy outcomes is a viable cross-partisan communication strategy that bypasses direct engagement with climate beliefs.

articles.title: Visualizing greener cities
articles.core_conclusion: AI-generated realistic images of greener urban futures can build cross-partisan public support for green transportation policies, including among politically sceptical groups such as Republicans. Visual framing is a scalable tool for sustainability transitions, but raises unresolved questions about image typology, mechanism, provenance, and audience-specific effects.

articles.title: Nexus between residential air pollution and physiological stress is moderated by greenness
articles.core_conclusion: In 190,200 UK Biobank adults, higher residential air pollution was associated with higher composite physiological stress (CPS) while higher residential greenness was inversely associated; the high-pollution/low-greenness 'double-impacted' group had 13-22% higher odds of top-quartile CPS than the least-impacted group, with synergistic pollutant x greenness interactions and considerable between-city variability, supporting nature-based interventions.

articles.title: The overlooked health impacts of extreme rainfall exposure in 30 East Asian cities
methods: subgroup stratification by age and period
sensitivity analysis with alternative precipitation datasets
data_sources: National death registries (Japan, South Korea, China, Taiwan, Hong Kong)
articles.core_conclusion: Only rare extreme rainfall events — those exceeding 5- or 10-year return-period thresholds — significantly elevate respiratory mortality in East Asian cities (RR 1.29 and 1.33, respectively). The effect peaks at lag 2 days and dissipates by lag 5, is strongest for asthma and COPD among the elderly, and is robust across alternative precipitation databases, lag specifications, and exclusion of concurrent flood or tropical cyclone events.

articles.title: Green roofs save energy in cities and fight regional climate change
data_sources: Seoul monthly gas and electricity records
articles.core_conclusion: Coupling WRF urban climate modeling with CitySim urban building energy modeling and empirical data for Seoul, the study finds that the cooling efficacy of nonirrigated extensive green roofs is strongly governed by meteorological conditions, yet their city-scale cooling still reduces monthly urban building energy use intensity by up to 0.64 kWh/m2 (7.7%), making green roofs a credible dual-benefit nature-based solution for heat and energy.

articles.title: Explaining changes in US residential segregation through patterns of population change
articles.core_conclusion: US residential segregation declined from 1990 to 2020, but asymmetrically: almost all of the decrease was produced by suburbanization and central-city growth of Black, Hispanic and Asian populations, while shifts in the white population mostly increased segregation. Cities undergo simultaneous desegregation and resegregation ('racialized reshuffling') that operates within suburbs, so integrating minorities without limiting white resegregation will not produce lasting reductions.

articles.title: Co-benefits of transport demand reductions from compact urban development in Chinese cities
articles.core_conclusion: Ambitious compact urban development (CUD) in China reduces on-road transport CO2 by 97 Mt (8%) and NOx by 95 kt (7%) under a low-AEV scenario, and avoids 25,000 premature deaths from air pollution by 2050. Even with 100% AEV penetration, CUD avoids 5,800 premature deaths via non-exhaust and upstream emission reductions, and saves up to 10% of AEV energy demand — demonstrating that demand-side urban compactness is a durable complement to supply-side electrification across all decarbonization pathways.

articles.title: The 15-minute city quantified using human mobility data
articles.core_conclusion: The median US resident makes only 14% of daily consumption trips within a 15-minute walk from home. Local amenity access explains 84% of variation in local-trip behaviour across 420 urban areas and 74% within cities. A 1961 NYC zoning natural experiment supports a causal link from access to local usage. However, promoting local living increases experienced income segregation for low-income residents, who encounter far less socioeconomic diversity on short trips than on longer ones.

articles.title: Comparing the carbon footprints of urban and conventional agriculture
articles.core_conclusion: Across 73 urban-agriculture sites in Europe and the US, food from urban agriculture has on average a ~6x larger carbon footprint than conventional agriculture (~420 vs ~70 gCO2e per serving), driven mainly by infrastructure emissions. However, some crops (e.g., tomatoes) and ~25% of individually managed gardens already outperform conventional farms; UA can cut impacts by growing greenhouse/air-freighted crops, maintaining sites for many years, and leveraging circularity.

articles.title: Global disparities in urban green space use during the COVID-19 pandemic from a systematic review
data_sources: Oxford COVID-19 Government Response Tracker (stringency index)
Web of Science / Scopus / CNKI / Google Scholar
articles.core_conclusion: Urban green space use changed in diverging directions worldwide during COVID-19: it generally decreased where lockdown policies were stricter and GDP per capita was lower (including most studied Global South areas) and increased where wealth was higher. By type, all private-garden and most forest/natural-area studies reported increases, while 77% of public-park studies reported decreases. The pandemic exacerbated pre-existing global disparities in green-space access.

articles.title: Worldwide scaling of waste generation in urban systems
articles.core_conclusion: Across more than 1,000 cities in 171 countries, the three major waste streams follow distinct universal scaling laws with city size: wastewater scales superlinearly (beta approx 1.15), municipal solid waste linearly (beta approx 1.04), and greenhouse-gas emissions sublinearly (beta approx 0.85). Deviations are explained by per-capita GDP and local rainfall, and the relationships evolve over time toward higher waste, with MSW losing its historical economy of scale.

articles.title: Large-scale online job search behaviors reveal labor market shifts amid COVID-19
articles.core_conclusion: Using online job-search queries (supply intention) and job postings (demand) across China from 2019Q4 to 2022Q4, labor-flow intention recovered quickly after pandemic shocks, with the regional center shifting from large to small cities and from north to south. Blue-collar demand fell far more than white-collar, yet metropolitan centrality declined and the regional supply-demand mismatch shrank, implying workers made relatively rational, demand-aligned career choices under uncertainty.

articles.title: Depopulation and associated challenges for US cities by 2100
articles.core_conclusion: By 2100, close to half of the nearly 30,000 US cities are projected to decline under at least one scenario, affecting 12-23% of population and 27-44% of populated area, threatening basic services (transit, water, electricity, internet). Growth in resource-intensive suburbs diverts resources from shrinking areas; immigration helps but cannot fix it alone without a shift away from growth-based planning.

articles.title: Network constraints on worker mobility
methods: regression on migration and air-travel flows
articles.core_conclusion: Career pathways modeled as a city-specific occupation network whose edges encode skill similarity predict occupation transition rates, with prediction improving at finer skill granularity. A worker's network embeddedness (skill specialization) governs dynamics: moves that decrease embeddedness raise wages and workers de-specialize over careers. City pairs with dissimilar embeddedness show greater census migration and air-travel flow, linking workplace skills to both career and spatial mobility.

articles.title: Quantifying the energy impact of heat mitigation technologies at the urban scale
articles.core_conclusion: In Riyadh, eight holistic heat-mitigation scenarios combining daytime radiative coolers, super-cool reflective materials and irrigated/non-irrigated greenery cut peak ambient temperature by up to 4.5 C (reported as the highest urban reduction) and annual cooling degree hours by up to 26%. Across 3,323 buildings, mitigation alone saves up to 16% cooling energy, and pairing it with building energy-adaptation measures cuts cooling demand by up to 35%.

articles.title: Socio-spatial inequality and the effects of density on COVID-19 transmission in US cities
articles.core_conclusion: Urban density is not a simple cause of COVID-19 transmission: the density-infection relationship is nonlinear, and higher-density neighborhoods were generally more able to reduce exposure through social distancing. The dominant driver of risk was socio-spatial inequality, with low-income and minority communities least able to modify mobility and thus bearing a disproportionate first-wave infection burden.

articles.title: Urban mobility and the experienced isolation of students
articles.core_conclusion: Using detailed GPS location data, students in major US metros experience more racial and income isolation than adults: they stay home longer, travel shorter distances, and visit fewer restaurants and retail venues. Among students, higher-income families visit more amenities and explore more unique places. A composite urban-mobility index rises, conditional on income, with distance from the urban core, car ownership, and neighborhood social capital.

articles.title: Quantifying the decrease in heat exposure through adaptation and mitigation in twenty-first-century US cities
methods: factorial scenario design (adaptation vs mitigation, separate vs tandem)
articles.core_conclusion: Adaptation (urban surface modification) and GHG mitigation each substantially reduce end-of-century population heat exposure in US cities, with the largest single-strategy gains in Northeast and Midwest cities. Deployed in tandem they interact synergistically: avoided exposure exceeds the sum of their parts at the lowest extreme-heat thresholds but falls short of the sum at the highest thresholds, across all US regions.

articles.title: Human mobility networks reveal increased segregation in large cities
methods: dynamic GPS exposure network construction (k-d tree, 50 m / 5 min threshold)
linear mixed-effects model for unbiased exposure segregation estimation
Pearson correlation generalisation of neighbourhood sorting index
articles.core_conclusion: Contrary to the cosmopolitan mixing hypothesis, exposure segregation — measured from 1.6 billion GPS-derived person-to-person exposures among 9.6 million US residents — is 67% higher in the ten largest MSAs than in small ones. Large cities are more segregated because they offer a greater choice of socioeconomically differentiated spaces. This segregative effect is substantially countered when commercial hubs are sited to bridge diverse neighbourhoods: MSAs with the highest bridging index are 53% less segregated than those with the lowest, independent of population size, income inequality, or race.

articles.title: The unequal effects of the health–economy trade-off during the COVID-19 pandemic
articles.core_conclusion: Mandated non-pharmaceutical interventions and spontaneous fear-driven behavioural change produce qualitatively similar health–economy trade-offs: both increase unemployment and reduce COVID-19 deaths. Low-income workers in in-person, customer-facing occupations bear the sharpest trade-off. Closing only customer-facing industries achieves nearly the same death reduction as closing all non-essential activities while reducing unemployment by 36%.

articles.title: Global evidence of rapid urban growth in flood zones since 1985
data_sources: Fathom Global flood hazard data (fluvial and pluvial)
Vousdoukas et al. coastal flood maps
articles.core_conclusion: Since 1985, human settlements worldwide have continuously and rapidly expanded into flood zones. In East Asia, high-hazard settlements grew 60% faster than flood-safe settlements. Rather than adapting, many countries actively amplify their flood exposure through locally determined urbanization, making rising disaster losses a governance failure as much as a climate one.

articles.title: Historical redlining is associated with increasing geographical disparities in bird biodiversity sampling in the United States
articles.core_conclusion: Historically redlined (HOLC grade D) urban neighbourhoods across 195 US cities remain the most undersampled areas for bird biodiversity today: sampling density odds are 74% lower and survey completeness is ~7 percentage points lower than formerly A-graded areas. The sampling disparity grew by 35.6% over 2000–2020, driven by citizen-science platforms whose volunteer demographics mirror historical socioeconomic inequities, meaning digital biodiversity data is actively reproducing rather than erasing historical environmental injustice.

articles.title: World's human migration patterns in 2000–2019 unveiled by high-resolution data
articles.core_conclusion: Using a novel annual gridded net migration dataset at 5 arcmin (~10 km) resolution covering 216 countries from 2000 to 2019, the study shows that around 50% of the world's urban population lived in areas where migration accelerated urban growth, while a third of the global population lived in provinces with positive rural net migration. Socioeconomic factors (HDI) are more strongly associated with migration patterns than climatic factors (aridity), and the direction and impact of migration vary substantially by spatial scale of analysis.

articles.title: Racial/ethnic disparities in PM2.5-attributable cardiovascular mortality burden in the United States
articles.core_conclusion: Non-Hispanic Black people in the US bear a disproportionate PM2.5-attributable cardiovascular mortality burden: a 1 µg/m³ PM2.5 increase is associated with 7.16 CVD deaths per 1,000,000 Black people per month — 4× the estimate for non-Hispanic white people (1.76). Over 2001–2016, the absolute Black–white disparity fell by 44%, but in 2016 the per-capita burden remained 3.47 times higher for non-Hispanic Black people. The disparity reflects both differential exposure (environmental racism) and differential vulnerability (structural racism).

articles.title: Empowering women facing gender-based violence amid COVID-19 through media campaigns
data_sources: Server visit logs and YouTube channel analytics
articles.core_conclusion: A pre-registered multi-arm RCT in Egypt (n=5,618) finds that WhatsApp outperforms Facebook in delivering a GBV edutainment intervention during COVID-19 mobility restrictions, while social media and TV reach comparable outcomes. The campaign increased women's knowledge of support organizations and their hypothetical and reported resource use, but produced no credible evidence of attitude change on gender norms or the justifiability of violence, underscoring the stickiness of entrenched social norms under repeated media exposure.

articles.title: Mass gatherings for political expression had no discernible association with the local course of the COVID-19 pandemic in the USA in 2020 and 2021
methods: average treatment effect on the treated (ATT)
articles.core_conclusion: Across five types of political mass gathering (primary elections, Georgia Senate run-off, NJ/VA gubernatorial elections, Trump rallies, BLM protests), no statistically significant increase in COVID-19 deaths, cases, or effective reproduction number Rt was detected in the 40-day post-event period. The average treatment effect on the treated was -0.057 deaths/day (95% CI: -0.319, 0.162). Political activities as typically conducted were not super-spreader events during the first two years of the pandemic in the USA.

articles.title: Advancing a slum–circular economy model for sustainability transition in cities of the Global South
articles.core_conclusion: Slum communities across the Global South already practice subaltern circularity — everyday strategies in livelihoods, housing, and space that embody circular economy principles — yet remain invisible in mainstream CE policy and finance. The slum–CE theoretical model maps these grassroot circular practices against ten CE principles and proposes three testable propositions, arguing that recognizing and integrating them into formal CE frameworks offers a pathway toward inclusive, just sustainability transitions.

articles.title: COVID-19 is linked to changes in the time–space dimension of human mobility
data_sources: ONS income estimates for small areas in England 2018
articles.core_conclusion: COVID-19 lockdowns disrupted two separable dimensions of human mobility — spatial range (radius of gyration) and temporal synchronization of commuting routines — with spatial mobility recovering faster than synchronization after restrictions lifted. These impacts were strongly stratified by urbanization and socioeconomic status: rural and low-income areas lost more spatial range, while urbanized and high-income areas lost more temporal synchronization.

articles.title: The global wildland–urban interface
data_sources: GHS-BUILT-S R2022A (Global Human Settlement, Sentinel-2, 10 m, 2018)
MODIS MCD14ML Collection 6.1 Active Fire (1 km, 2003–2020)
Aboveground biomass carbon density map (300 m, 2010)
articles.core_conclusion: The wildland–urban interface (WUI) is a global phenomenon covering 4.7% of land area (6.3 million km²) yet home to 3.5 billion people. A globally consistent 10 m resolution map for 2020 reveals widespread previously undocumented WUI hotspots in East Africa, eastern Asia and South America. Two thirds of all people who experienced wildfire from 2003 to 2020 live in the WUI, making it a critical zone for climate-change adaptation and hazard preparedness.

articles.title: Understanding and combatting misinformation across 16 countries on six continents
methods: rating-level OLS with clustered robust SEs
articles.core_conclusion: Across 16 countries on 6 continents (N=34,286; 676,605 observations), analytic thinking, accuracy motivations, and valuing democracy consistently predict better COVID-19 misinformation discernment in every country. Simple accuracy prompts and minimal digital literacy tips broadly reduce misinformation sharing intentions. Aggregating as few as 10-15 layperson ratings per headline achieves over 80-90% AUC in distinguishing true from false claims via wisdom of crowds — suggesting that the psychological architecture of the misinformation problem and its solutions may be cross-culturally universal.

articles.title: The effect of natural disasters on human capital in the United States
methods: binned disaster intensity (4 per-capita damage categories)
migration robustness check using IRS data
Jorgenson-Fraumeni human capital monetization (PDV of lifetime earnings losses)
data_sources: SHELDUS (Spatial Hazard Events and Losses Database)
EDFacts (US DoE K-12 cohort graduation rates)
articles.core_conclusion: Natural disasters across the United States cause persistent, scarring damage to human capital — reducing standardized test scores by up to 0.023 SD and post-secondary enrolment by ~2.5% after the largest events — and the present discounted value of these human capital losses rivals assessed physical property damage in magnitude. Current disaster-relief policy, focused almost exclusively on physical damages, systematically ignores this equally consequential cost.

articles.title: Environmental and welfare gains via urban transport policy portfolios across 120 cities
methods: spatially explicit monocentric urban economic model (NEDUM)
linear regression and principal component analysis for heterogeneity decomposition
data_sources: Spatially explicit urban dataset (GHS-POP + ESA CCI + Google Maps API + real estate scraping)
ESA Climate Change Initiative (CCI) Land Cover
articles.core_conclusion: A portfolio of four urban transport policies (fuel tax, BRT, land-use regulations, fuel efficiency) can reduce urban transport GHG emissions by 31% in 15 years across 120 cities, but impacts vary widely by city characteristics. A tailored welfare-increasing portfolio per city still achieves 22% global emission reduction while improving average welfare, showing that context-specific strategies can deliver large simultaneous environmental and welfare gains with no one-size-fits-all solution.

articles.title: Religion and educational mobility in Africa
articles.core_conclusion: Across 21 African countries and 2,286 districts, Christians achieve higher intergenerational educational mobility than Muslims and Traditionalists, even controlling for household structure, parental occupation, and district fixed effects. Both Christians and Muslims benefit equally from early-life relocation to high-mobility districts, but Muslims migrate significantly less often — a pattern most pronounced where Muslims are the majority — creating a spatial-segregation trap that compounds their educational disadvantage.

articles.title: Little evidence that military policing reduces crime or improves human security
data_sources: Administrative crime records (Cali Mayor's Office / Colombian National Police / Attorney-General)
GPS patrol logs and civilian monitor observations (custom smartphone app)
Seguridad, Justicia y Paz NGO global homicide data
articles.core_conclusion: A block-level RCT of Plan Fortaleza military patrols in Cali, Colombia (1,255 blocks, 2019) found little to no evidence that military policing reduced crime or improved safety perceptions. Crime increased ~69% on treatment blocks after the intervention ended, and survey data show elevated police (not military) abuse in patrol zones. Benefits of military policing are probably small and not worth the costs.

articles.title: Seeing racial avoidance on New York City streets
data_sources: NYC DOT public traffic camera feeds (nyctmc.org)
articles.core_conclusion: Using NYC traffic camera feeds and a randomized field experiment across 3,552 pedestrians in two Manhattan neighbourhoods, the study documents systematic racial avoidance: non-Black pedestrians (93% of sample) give on average ~4 inches (~3.4% of sidewalk width) wider berth to phenotypically Black male confederates than to white non-Hispanic male confederates, with statistically significant effects pooled across sites and in Midtown.

articles.title: Increased heat risk in wet climate induced by urban humid heat
data_sources: National meteorological services (Germany, Finland, Sweden, Norway, Canada, Argentina, Japan, Brazil, Chile)
articles.core_conclusion: Cities in wet climates (summer precipitation >570 mm) raise urban wet-bulb temperature (Tw) by 0.17 ± 0.14 °C on summer daytimes, primarily because urban canopies suppress dynamic turbulent mixing that would otherwise reduce humidity. This small increment is sufficient to cause 2–6 extra dangerous heat-stress days per summer for urban residents, and the risk is projected to increase under future climate change.

articles.title: Cities dried up by few
data_sources: focus groups with NGOs and government
articles.core_conclusion: Urban water crises are not merely a supply problem: elite overconsumption is a primary driver. In Cape Town (2015–2018), the top 13.7% of income earners consumed 51% of the city's water while the bottom 40.5% consumed only 27.3%. Government response measures — rationing, tariffs, and penalties — backfired for the poor while leaving high-income households largely shielded, illustrating how social inequalities perpetuate and deepen crises even when authorities intervene.
